Based in Cotonou, Benin, which at the time was referred to as Dahomey, Albarika Records was founded by the French-born, Benin-based producer and entrepreneur, Emmanuel "Manu" Dibango, who had a significant influence on the local music scene. The label was instrumental in releasing records from some of the region's most prominent musicians during the golden age of African music.
Many of the artists signed to Albarika Records were known for blending indigenous African rhythms with Western jazz, funk, and soul influences. This made their sound distinctive and part of a broader movement across West Africa during the 60s and 70s, as music became a form of social and political expression.

"We give a French touch to house"
A two hour ride through the wave of French house music that captivated dancefloors around the world from the mid-90s into the early 2000s. Deeply indebted to Chicago house, French producers brought a louche sense of humour and a penchant for colourful maximalism to the genre. 
The party-forward "filtered house" sound which was poorly imitated ad infinitum into the 2000s was but one aspect of French Touch. St Germain were faithful to the deeper shades of the Chicago source material, and producers like Pépé Bradock used samples to take listeners on intricately arranged and psychedelic dancefloor journeys. 
Aspects of French Touch's history have been purposefully omitted from this two hour mix for the sake of brevity, namely the scene's origins in France's hip hop scene, (Philippe Zdar and DJ Mehdi both produced for legendary French rapper MC Solaar) and French Touch's "second wave" in the mid-2000s, spearheaded by Busy P's Ed Banger Records label, and artists such as Justice.

nts1.n: Fifth World w/ Ian Kim Judd (R)
nts1.d: A riff on Jon Hassell's Fourth World series. In these times of global instability and uncertainty, Fifth World seeks to posit an alternative, with scenes from an optimistic future.

nts1.n: NTS Guide to: Komuso flute - Shakuhachi
nts1.d: The Komuso were a sect of non-monastic wandering Buddhist monks who emerged in Edo period Japan, who were famous for their face-obscuring woven straw basket masks called tengai. They also differed from monastic Monks, who chose to meditate in silence, as the Komuso played a bamboo flute called a shakuhachi as a form of meditation.

nts1.n: Cruel Santino Presents: Subaru Live Radio vol. 2
nts1.d:
From the creative mind of Cruel Santino, Subaru Live Radio on NTS explores Nigeria's alternative music scene with rare sounds, global collaborations, exclusive cuts, and special guest features. Set in an immersive underwater theme, each episode brings listeners deeper into Subaru World, building a lasting library of groundbreaking music for future generations to come, soundscaped with ads and various infomercials. 
Visually, Volume 2 expands, capturing raw Subaru-like visuals directed and edited by Santi for upcoming artists while also profiling their background and influences in gaming.

Rhythm Section Radio was founded in 2009 and moved to NTS in 2012, and has since pulled out the stops breaking new music, promoting independent labels and paying homage to the greatest music from the past, through a perhaps unhealthy obsession with record digging.

Part of the NTS family since day dot, Moxie has held her Wednesday show since the stations first broadcasts. Inviting esteemed guests for interviews & mixes and breaking new, unreleased music, she’s got your Wednesday afternoons covered… Tune in to hear anything from Soulful House, Garage, Afro beats and all the way through to rolling techno.
After work fun and global tunes with Tash LC and guests every other Wednesday

nts1.n: Women of Video Game Music: Yuka Kitamura (R)
nts1.d: As lead composer at FromSoftware, the Japanese developer deeply beloved by hardcore gamers across the world, Yuka Kitamura has soundtracked the virtual deaths of millions of players. Her music, which soundtrack the tough as nails, worlds of Dark Souls, Elden Ring, and Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice, is as tense and foreboding as the worlds themselves.

Each hour-long show explores our favorite jams from the city’s bubbling alternative scene, covering alt-afro, indie, electronic, and trap sounds shaping the future of Lagos’ underground.

nts1.n: NTS GUIDE TO: INDEPENDENT NORTH AMERICAN JAZZ VOL 4 (R)
nts1.d: This guide takes another deep dive into the world of independently released and private-press jazz from across the US and Canada. The fourth instalment highlights visionary artists who worked beyond the major label system: from the spiritual minimalism of Ahmed Jamal and the cosmic brass meditations of Brother Ah, to the rare community recording of The African Jazz Ensemble, captured live at Brightwood Elementary School in Washington D.C. Expect modal explorations, small-press ingenuity, and the restless experimentation that defined the continent’s underground jazz movement.
Ziggy Devriendt is the DJ behind Nosedrip. Tune in once a month for an expert selection of any or all of the following: new age, new wave, French psych, minimal synth, post-punk and a whole lot else…

nts1.d: Stay left and take a trip with Macca's Saturday breakfast show on NTS. Weekend musings and eclectic cuts from all over the place, it's a low-key affair and you're all invited. ONE GLOVE!!!

Classic Jamaican records from the "Rocksteady Daddy" Asher G.

Follow him on Instagram here

everything else indoors with me
austere fitting of a tory sort of god
he happens upon my thrifted rug

through the black of my shadow

nts1.d: Vibrant eclecticism from Portuguese selector Mafalda, for two hours every month.

In a prolific era in the late 1980s and early 1990s, New Jersey Twin brothers Reginald (aka Rheji) and Ronald (aka Rhano) Burrell laid upon the early Chicago foundations of deep house with a stack of influential releases under various monikers that would come to define the sound of the iconic Nu Groove label. They channeled their eclectic influences – gospel, americana, new jack swing, hip hop and more into hypnotising dance tracks.
Selected and mixed by Finn.
Deep-cut house and techno sessions from vet club selector Wes Baggaley.
Trips through the breakbeat portal with Tim Reaper, one of modern jungle's most vital talents.

Record maker, player and mixer Martelo - not Martello - joins the musical dots with sonic heat from across the globe. Taking us to hip-hop, RnB, electronica and elsewhere, Martelo keeps it tight and keeps the heat: non-stop classics.
Hi octane monthly missives from Naina & Sherelle, the minds behind the hybrid Jungle, Techno & Footwork label Hooversound.

Nkisi a.k.a Melika Ngombe Kolongo heads up a monthly late slot to rattle through dozens of tracks at breakneck speed, her mixes punctuated by African club beats and tinged by the jagged edges of gabber.

North London native and Hyperdub signee Loraine James is an electronic producer who effortlessly blends Electronica and IDM with whispers of jazz and ambience all wrapped up in the artists key avant-garde aesthetic and sensibility.

nts1.n: Can You Feel The Sun w/ Parris
nts1.d: "A kind of music" - the duo behind the new label come together for a regular hour long session, weaving through unexpected dancefloor sounds…

nts1.n: The Lunacy of Flowers w/ Dave ID (R)
nts1.d: Dave I.D takes us through industrial, and techno – melodic and dark. New and classic goth, his selection sets him apart from most, branching decades and scenes.
The inimitable Kranky Ltd, live from LA once a month.

A monthly journey into H.O.U.S.E. hosted by Dan Beaumont & Nadia Ksaiba featuring interviews & mixes from super special guests. Expect two hours of old bangers and upfront pumpers.
Tune in, turn on, clock out. Sofie K brings sleazy synthpop, pumping proto-house and campy club cuts to the NTS studio once a month.

nts1.d: London-based jazz artist Goya Gumbani stops by to share heartfelt picks from his collection.

DJ, artist and techno impresario, Word of Command welds fevered collage and high-octane techno into a hallucinatory dreamscape. He is the founder of FIST (@funisstilltransgressive), a queer techno party that pays tribute to radical fun, while engaging the visibility of communities of otherness through techno in NYC.

‘When we refuse… we create dissonance and more importantly, we allow dissonance to continue.’ - from Jack Halberstam’s introduction to the Undercommons.
Disruptive music for broken times curated by Nymity. Queering, Unlearning, Fragments and Imaginings.
Artwork by Harry King
Same shit, different month.

Artist and audio maker, Alicia Matthews, takes over the NTS airwaves once a month for a rhythm ’n’ gloom showdown.
Past projects include SUE ZUKI (Domestic Exile/ NTS), LAPS (MIC Records/ DFA Records) and Organs of  Love (Optimo Music).

A monthly transmission of displaced rock n roll, exotic punk and early electronics, paying favor to all things primitive and sleazy. LA visual artist Amanda Siegel delves into the international underground, offering a thorough sonic survey of protopunk, hard rock, synthpunk, powerpop, psych, industrial and more.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ JACK ROLLO & Kaity Fox
nts1.d: First thing on Fridays - Jack Rollo's here to take care of things.

nts1.n: The NTS Breakfast Show w/ ERROL AND ALEX RITA
nts1.d: Wake up with NTS…

nts1.n: SOUP TO NUTS W/ SCARY THINGS
nts1.d: Friday mornings get scary - Bempah & JK elevating your vibe right in time for the weekend.
Raj Chaudhuri - aka Raji Rags. NTS since day one. Always eclectic, never boring. Raj rustles up a seamless monthly mix that journeys through the BPMs.
Iconic queer party crew Adonis showcase their residents on a monthly rotation live from the London studio.

nts1.n: WAYA FYAM W/ PRIESTE$$ TELZZZZZZ - Women in Ke HipHop
nts1.d:
Waya Fyam dives into Nairobi’s wild soundscape – shrap bars, kapuka, and dancehall riddims. Mixing these with moombahton, a Dutch-born sound built on the same Caribbean grooves, to show how Kenyan street energy links up with global club culture. Expect heavy bass, raw slang, and pure movement.
From the host: "This mix starts with a poem from Maya Angelou asking us to be better people, to be stronger in everything we do, to leave a mark that brings hope when you are long gone. For this episode of Waya Fyam we celebrate the women making a difference in Kenyan HipHop"

nts1.n: BASHMENT SOUND w/ Shadow Fyah - Rise & Toast (Live in Miami)
nts1.d:
Miami-based producers and DJs Silent Addy and Disco Neil present Bashment Sound – a regular celebration of Caribbean sounds gone global.
From the host: "Silent Addy on the decks with Shadow Fyah (Bashment Sound) on the mic, recorded live at Rise & Toast at The Urban Miami — March 8, 2026. Pure dancehall energy from one of Miami’s biggest Sunday day parties."

Martha is a DJ and broadcaster from South London with roots in community radio and documentary making. Each of Martha’s thoughtfully curated radio shows navigate the fringes of electronic music, with a clear narrative that is grounded in community and storytelling.
House, techno, pop mutations and club kid rhythms from NYC-based DJ and producer Flirty800, going live from the London studio.

Oldies, jungle, hip hop, bass music, and more from legendary Florida DJ hopper1000.
From the host: _"Legendary Swaglord Irene Lee joins hopper1000 radio for a great chat about life, hopper1000 drops many unreleased songs. 
EVERYONE HAVE A BLESSED FREAKIN WEEK, BE YOURSELF NO MATTER WHAT BOX PEOPLE TRY N PUT YOU INTO. WE ALL HAVE A SPECIAL PURPOSE AND WE MUST FOLLOW THAT!"_

Journeys through the past, present, and future of dance culture with The co-founder of techno and elevator of house music, Kevin Saunderson. Expect deep blends of house, techno, electro, and disco alongside stories of the scenes and sounds that shaped them.

From the host: _"We're celebrating 20 years of releasing sound system music in 2026!
Goth-Trad's been a consistent part of our journey since "Cut end" in 2007!

Gilded Sound Reflection is the musical collective of Tai Thittichai and David Lane. They magnify the bleed between genre lines, blurring the boundaries of krautrock, jazz, psych, soul, pop and more – a monthly show, settle in for a meandering couple hours early in the afternoon.

nts1.n: WE ARE... W/ PAUL CAMO & Ranie Ribeiro

Artist, graphic designer and DJ Paul Camo sets out to push the boundaries: free of all restrictions. Jump in the chat and listen as Paul Camo's WE ARE brings you on a voyage through the spectrum of jazz fusion, spiritual jazz, experimental jazz and everything in between.
From the host: "Paul Camo talks with Ranie Ribeiro about how he discovered the harp, his musical journey, the process for creating the Lp Contemplation, his soundtrack scores and performing at the Wales Bonner's fashion week show in Paris last year. The show also features a live performance from Ranie."

nts1.n: Blank Forms - Let’s all oppose authenticity

From the host: "A quick trip through the world of Scott Foust, who passed away this month. Let’s All Oppose Authenticity gathers a small constellation of recordings from across his many projects—Idea Fire Company, The Pickle Factory, The Tobacconists, and others. We are all lost at sea."
Awe is something you feel when confronted with forces beyond your control: nature, the cosmos, chaos, human error, hallucinations.

nts1.n: Alice Coltrane Special: Brandee Younger Live Performance (R)
nts1.d:
Jazz harpist and Alice Coltrane acolyte Brandee Younger pays tribute to her hero with a live harp performance. Featuring Brandee Younger on harp and Rashaan Carter on double bass, recorded at Younger’s home studio in New York, NY.
"I specifically chose these three pieces for NTS Radio to pay tribute to the groundbreaking work of Alice Coltrane and Dorothy Ashby, while also integrating my own voice. The set reflects their profound influence on my music and the continued evolution of harp music, which remains a significant source of inspiration for my work today."

Rising alongside the sophisti-pop strain of British New Wave, sophisti-funk names an early-to-mid-1980s current in R&B and funk that fused post-disco rhythms with high-gloss studio modernism. Defined by digital precision, immaculate surfaces, and a newly cosmopolitan sensibility in Black popular music, it recast funk’s language of grit and excess into one of polish, restraint, and spacious minimalism — total sonic luxury.
Forged in major-label studios with state-of-the-art equipment, with its machine-forged rhythms and melodic extravagance, sophisti-funk laid the sonic and aesthetic foundations for much followed: from street soul and new jack swing through the ’90s boy-band explosion into vaporwave and contemporary digital R&B."
This Guide To presents a selection of tracks that incapsulates the sound – featuring production work from Kashif, Jam and Lewis, Nick Martinelli and more, as well as artists like Luther Vandross, The System, Patrice Rushen, Reid and Babyface among others.

8ULENTINA is an interdisciplinary artist, music producer and DJ based in Brooklyn, New York. Known for his dynamic DJ sets and music composition synthesizing a range of genres, geographies and tempos. He co-founded CLUB CHAI (2016-2021), a record label and curatorial platform with Lara Sarkissian and currently runs a SILLAGE, a platform for contemporary sonic artifacts. His NTS show explores the sounds that inspire his production process, focusing on experimentation, percussive intensity, and non linear storytelling through sound.
A swift hour long excursion into the unknown. Expect the unexpected from host Tom Cathcart as almost anything goes: techno, post-punk, minimal synth, dub and new wave. Don’t be a prude.
Los Angeles producer & sound artist claire rousay journeys through healing plaintive electronics, quotidian field recordings & minimalist compositions.

nts1.n: Fifth World w/ Ian Kim Judd (R)
nts1.d: A riff on Jon Hassell's Fourth World series. Where Hassell focused on creating ritual music of potential societies that didn't exist; Ian Kim Judd seeks to evoke the notion of potential utopian societies that could exist. In these times of global instability and uncertainty, Fifth World seeks to posit an alternative, with scenes from an optimistic future.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ JACK ROLLO & Fenland Drone Workshop
nts1.d: First thing on Fridays - Jack Rollo's here to take care of things.

A radio tribute to the late dub mystic, Lee Scratch Perry, widely credited with pioneering the roots reggae and dub sounds through his extensive work behind the mixing desk in a career that spanned six decades. 
_ “I see the studio must be like a living thing, a life itself… The machine must be live and intelligent. Then I put my mind into the machine and the machine perform reality.”_

Radio Jiro is the two hour show of artist Jiro Bevis, every 4 weeks, Monday 2-4pm. The show features a wide variety of sounds from across the globe with previous specials covering themes such as Meditation, Italian, UK punk, Japanese Techno-Pop, Library and French.

nts1.n: PUM PUM POWER HOUR W/ LIL C
nts1.d: Party starter & dancehall connoisseur Lil C live in session.

nts1.n: TEARS IN THE CLUB W/ B4mba
nts1.d:
"Tears in the Club" presents a unique blend of structured and chaotic music, combining intense hip hop, drum and bass, footwork, jersey club, and sometimes Afro rhythms. This multi-genre musical show explores diverse musical landscapes, creating a striking contrast between genres, while also showcasing new artists.
From the host: "For this episode, I invited B4mba around a selection of afro-diasporic rap.
Between club and rap, the selection moves through afro-club and afro-influenced rap, alongside gqom, experimental club and percussive bass sounds. B4mba is a Senegalese-Hispanic producer, DJ and vocalist based in Barcelona."

A somber and experimental guest mix from produder and DJ Maral – featuring Iranian classics, early shellac recordings of Iranian classical Music, and field recordings of folk music from different regions of Iran.
Tapping into the liminal feelings of being in the diaspora, mixtures of sadness, anger, fear and nostalgia. A cathartic mix for anyone in the world.
contemporary french + international rap presented by Stephane.

A singular figure in grime who cut his own road through the scene, Dot Rotten had a brief dalliance with mainstream sounds, but was always committed to the underground. A multifaced talent, the artist born Joseph Daniel Ellis-Stevenson, could handle introspective and intricate storytelling, and tackle diss tracks with the fearlessness and sportsmanship that grime demanded. He was also an influential producer, making instrumentals under the name Zeph Ellis. 
NTS selects an hour of tracks from the sadly passed grime MC and producer Dot Rotten.

nts1.n: RUF DUG b2b DANIEL JOHN WILLIS
nts1.d:
Ruf Dug brings the leftfield: old video game soundtracks, dusty analogue dreamers and poolside Balearic sounds. No nonsense from the original tropical cyberpunk.
From the host: "Recorded last night at the Rainy Heart x Loft party in central London. Ruffy was joined on the 1s and 2s by professional man-about-town DANIEL JOHN WILLIS for an all wax evening of high-end sound, Eames chairs and tacos"
Ira demonstrates how to do extra soulful with a masterclass in vibes. It's easy when you know how.
Special guest shows from around the world.

Innervisions brings its sound to NTS with monthly shows featuring its core artists and special guests.
From the host: "This mix is a small window into our emotional musical landscape, a moment between light and shadow. Imperfect but honest."

ENDLESS rolls through NTS on a monthly basis. Sounding like Empyrean cracked open, the airwaves morph into into a tunnel linking South London to the end of the world through blown-out drums & and redlined ambience. Including exclusive dubs and guest mixes from friends.

nts1.n: Bubble Gum Violence w/ In Heaven Everything is Fine - Imitation/Sincerity/Flattery 2

nts1.d:
MOBBS chopping up whatever he finds 
ohm.mobbs@protonmail.com
Nubian songs from northern Sudan and southern Egypt.

For this month's episode I am super thrilled to welcome Fireground, a duo from Napoli. For the show they are presenting "Marea Live" ~ exclusive material recorded live with a full hardware live set that includes live keyboards (synths and electric piano) performed in real time.There are no official recordings or releases from this set available yet, so this is the first time it's shared publicly.
The finest London selection of Japanese grooves, from the 30s to 80s, and more. Anything from folk, jazz, psychedelia, new wave, soul, disco and electronic, all Made In Japan.
Nick Malkin hosts Post-Geography, featuring ambient and atmospheric selections, live from the NTS studio in L.A.
Sounds of the Dawn is a monthly two hour session through new age, ambient, environmental, meditation and relaxation cassettes from the seventies to the nineties. Tune in and zone out.

nts1.n: THE ONE GLOVE BREAKFAST SHOW W/ Soul System
nts1.d: Stay left and take a trip with Macca's Saturday breakfast show on NTS. Weekend musings and eclectic cuts from all over the place, it's a low-key affair and you're all invited. ONE GLOVE!!!

Ross Allen knows music. Mainly new but plenty of old. The broadest range of music that moves dance floors from across the era’s and across the planet. On his regular Foundation Music Specials he invites guests to share their histories and seminal tracks…
From dub and dancehall to acid house and grime, London DJ, producer, and poet James Massiah showcases his references and creative influences

nts1.n: THE EXTENDED PLAY SESSIONS W/ MR PEDRO
nts1.d: Long time record collector and purveyor of fine musical flavours Mr. Pedro cooks up a banquet of aural delights every other week: afrobeat to disco and more, time to liven up.
A swift hour long excursion into the unknown. Expect the unexpected from host Tom Cathcart as almost anything goes: techno, post-punk, minimal synth, dub and new wave. Don’t be a prude.
Sweet selections from director and photographer Alfie Whiteman.
Babak Ganjei's monthly grunge and garage hangover rolls through with classics old and new, from Memphis to London, interspersed, of course, with totally irrelevant jokes and chat.
Cherrie Flava, the boss of MIC Records, the London label behind releases from LAPS, Mike Collins, Lord Tusk & more, joins us once a month.

Eazy-breezy dancefloor eclecticism from the DC wonder duo…
From the host: "Beautiful Swimmers start in the club, and then float down the hallway, and then lift-off out front into space. Pass it this way, please."

Coined by DRB LasGidi in 2014, alté fuses afrobeats, pop, rap, R&B, soul and dancehall into a DIY, genre‑bending sound from Lagos’ creative fringes. The term - slang for “alternative” - celebrates freedom of expression, emerging as a youth‑led reaction to conservative Naija culture. Catch our guide featuring trailblazers Cruel Santino, Odunsi (The Engine), Amaarae, Tems, and more.
Pattern Sounds co-founder Selassie TBC delivers an hour of straight jams every month.
Slowed Down, sun kissed journeys through dub, synth, cosmic & balearic beats. Brought to you from LA each month.

Sketchbook was the LA beat night started by Kutmah back in 2004 and was the breeding ground for artists like Daedelus, Ras G, Dibiase, Georgia Anne Muldrow, Teebs and Flying Lotus. Now it's radio reincarnation is on this side of the Atlantic. Lock in.

Helen Folasade Adu CBE, known professionally as Sade Adu or simply Sade, is an English singer, songwriter, and actress. She is best known as the lead vocalist of the English band Sade. This episode of In Focus looks at her career.
The Cleaners From Venus frontman presents an eclectic mix of pop music, comedy, and more.
Odda Recordings label owner Thea HD unearths the lesser known common sounds. from old soundtracks, library music to experimental electronic music…
Organic soundscapes, field recordings and melodic ambience from composer Sophia Zhuravkova.
Rory Bowens heads The Slip on Sunday mornings every other week - squeezing an hour of hazy dub off-shoots, pupil-dilating shoegaze and uncanny dream music.

In Iceland, Thomas Stankiewicz contemplates the constant flux of the elements. A composer & musician in his own right, he selectively weaves in between soundtracks, classical music, and evocative ambient sounds, symphonically unveiling untold stories from past and future…
@tommmasi
Minus Kendal trods the outer reaches foraging for aural morsels, slinging you a monthly smorgasbord of sultry sonix from trenchcoat folk to silicone synthwave.

Kit Grill presents a monthly excursion into music from around the world. Sunday, monthly, 4-5pm GMT. kitgrill.com
From the host: "This is a special ambient focus show ahead of the launch of my new album Andøya at Ormside on April 9, 2026."
The man behind the various Emotional labels jumps in the NTS cockpit for a chance to show off his expansive taste.
Janie Jones delivers an hour a month of kraut, psych and other treats.

London Perfect Lives shopkeepers Bruno and Danny share an hour of discoveries each month, Dipping into charming private press releases, cozy dub & dancehall, way out of left-field synth pop & postpunk, among other surprises..

An exploration into obscure, underground, and lesser known music from Hawaii, bound not by genre or generation but by a connection to the islands. Hosted by Roger Bong of Aloha Got Soul, a record label and record store in Honolulu.
From the host: "A soundtrack for leisurely drives along the coast, preferably Windward side."

nts1.n: MIDNIGHT MARAUDERS W/ MARSHMELLO & SUKI QUASIMODO
nts1.d: Marsha by name and mellow by nature, MarshmeLLo takes you on a musical journey of all her influences plus a little extra. The first show ever broadcast on the station, Marsha continues to find her groove…

A prolific & singular figure in jungle & drum and bass, Dillinja produced a body of work in the 1990s and 2000s that set a high water mark for club production finesse. Even now, most top producers in the genre are sure to have some secret weapon samples titled "Dillinja bass". 
DJ Persuasion and Dev/Null pull together two hours of his finest tracks, including anthems, rarities, well worn favourites and unreleased dubs on this In Focus.

Mosca runs underground club music inspired by tunes from all over: dub, house, techno, grime and jungle, as well as East coast hip hop, South African house, Shaolin soul, indie and post-rock from his youth, first-wave electro, stripped down ragga and dancehall, a little minimal ghetto stuff, and more. On top of that he's racked up some pretty big guest mixes in past shows.
Elena Colombi helps to keep nights weird - expect a diverse range of up-and-at-'em records: from techno to post-punk, through EBM, industrial and -wave…
Omar Hraib presents The Witching Hour; a passage through the realms of the slow, the heavy and the strange.

nts1.n: Perfect Lives w/ Bruno & Daniel Lichtenstein - Ethiopian Pop Special (R)
nts1.d: London Perfect Lives shopkeepers Bruno and Danny share an hour of discoveries each month, Dipping into charming private press releases, cozy dub & dancehall, way out of left-field synth pop & postpunk, among other surprises..

nts1.d: Singer-songwriter Carla Dal Forno takes over early morning duties from Melbourne with soft folk, indie and alternative selections.

nts1.n: Soup To Nuts w/ John Gómez
nts1.d: Collector John Gómez brightens up Mondays with some music from different times and different places.

Tropical rhythms, devotional boogie and soulful shenanigans with a heavy jazz undertone, Patrick Forge gathers it all up for your listening pleasure.
From the host:"Always curated and sequenced with love, another excursion of jazz infused wonders, ancient and modern."

Vinyl and acetate from Brazil and beyond.
Instagram.com/Dactylian
Instagram.com/Palo Santo Discos
Drakeford in the drivetime slot. A gentle cruise through dream pop, vintage folk, lushed-out electronics and more.
Welcome to Club Coco: a place where globe-trotting vinyl hunter Coco María shares her joyous discoveries - jazz, fusion, international pop, vintage latin music of all flavours…
Tune in to the sound of SUNCUT, boasting a heartfelt selection of some favourite 45’s through to classic bonafide soulful jams.

nts1.n: ALL FRUITS RIPE W/ JACK SAPSED
nts1.d: Ease in to delectations from our host, Jack Sapsed. A sea of sideways sounds: ambient tranquility and psychedelic folk; experimental dub to techno… ALL FRUITS RIPE

http://lungd.art/
Torn Hawk, also known as Luke Wyatt, brings his comedy talk show to the NTS airwaves.

nts1.d:
Drowned By Locals in collaboration with Rhizome Parking Garage presents music made to be listened to in parking garages. Inspired by the parking garage as a symbol of “the inferno of the same” and the agony of uniformity.
From the host: _"WHAT HAPPENS WHEN IT’S OVER AND I CAN’T SPEND TIME WITH YOU ANYMORE W/ CHEREE TRUE: A birthday mix drawn from ten years of conversations with L
Drowned By Locals in collaboration with Rhizome Parking Garage presents music made to be listened to in parking garages. Inspired by the parking garage as"_

nts1.d: A story by Tarisai Ngangura and a score by Immanuel Wilkins in response to 'Vertigo' (2024) by Turiya Adkins.

Jupiter Presents: Familiar Music by Caleb Azumah Nelson w/ anaiis

nts1.d: RVNG Intl. presents Friends & Fiends, a monthly guest hosted show spotlighting resident artists and heads orbiting the label’s universe of forward-reaching, free, and (generally) fried music.
Sonic diaries from Fine…
Rose-tinted hallucinogenic sounds from Astral Vibes' Acid Memories. Illicit dealings in psychedelia of all persuasions. Sit back and enjoy…

nts1.n: The Early Bird Show w/ TROTH
nts1.d: The Early Bird Show - our radio offering for morning people, hosted by a rotating cast of NTS friends and family.

nts1.d: Breakfast, in Flo motion - served by Flo Dill, every Monday to Thursday.
Let NTS brighten up your lunchtime hours.

London-based DJ and producer Nabihah Iqbal (formerly known as Throwing Shade) brings you an ethnomusicological perspective on weird and wonderful music from around the world. Expect regular themed special episode; a good dose of history, and above all, good music to broaden your horizons. Every other Tuesday, midday-1pm. You can listen back to previous Throwing Shade episodes here
From the host: "2 hours, all vinyl, sharing recent finds from digging in record shops all around the world, from Melbourne to Miami…!"
Settle in for weekly shows from Margeaux aka @marg.mp3, soothing with an hour of selections, offering an eclectic range of sounds old and new.
Record collector, artist, and mystic, Millie McKee shares organic spirtualized oddities and stories, dancing between rare variations of jazz, psych, dub and rock and more.

Jennifer Walton presents an hour of eclectic picks from research-driven internet crate digging. Following threads of Cosmic Americana, psychedelic timbres and experimental recordings to find through lines between the past and present.

nts1.n: Bump City w/ Billy Goods, Bobby Soul, Jas (R)
nts1.d: Funkmosphere resident Billy Goods heads up a 2 hour slot with James Yamagucci - all vinyl, all live and direct from our LA studio.

Veronica Vasicka presents a monthly 2-hour show featuring the lost synth-wave treasures she has archived from the late 70s and early 80s, along with other iconic underground electronic music drawn from over four decades. Expect to hear exclusive tracks from releases on her seminal Minimal Wave and Cititrax labels plus cassette-only and private press rarities.
Connections in electronic music: the history of house and techno as presented to you by the wonderful Olly Chubb.

Midnite Madness endeavours to unearth the spirit of the ancient metal gods as well as the genre's new flesh, taking in most styles—heavy, thrash, black, speed, death, punk, doom, etc. There will be some exclusion: no nu-metal, no triggered drums, no posers and no requests. Only metal is real!

Artist and audio maker, Alicia Matthews, takes over the NTS airwaves once a month for a rhythm ’n’ gloom showdown.
Past projects include SUE ZUKI (Domestic Exile/ NTS), LAPS (MIC Records/ DFA Records) and Organs of  Love (Optimo Music).
All flavours of acoustic & historical nuggets from Andras - Melbourne production wiz, composer & sleuth of musical ephemera…

nts1.d: Long time record collector and purveyor of fine musical flavours Mr. Pedro cooks up a banquet of aural delights every other week: afrobeat to disco and more, time to liven up.

nts1.d: The man behind Tokyo's revered Organic Music record store takes to the airwaves for a monthly 60 minute journey through his extensive collection…

nts1.d: Dawn moods for the early birds, courtesy of Sleepier & Impey aka Spirit Blue.

nts1.n: SOUP TO NUTS W/ SHY ONE
nts1.d: DJ & producer Shy One oversees the chatroom nonsense and brings that necessary get-up-and-go vibe every single hump day…

Radio Jiro is the two hour show of artist Jiro Bevis, every 4 weeks, Monday 2-4pm. The show features a wide variety of sounds from across the globe with previous specials covering themes such as Meditation, Italian, UK punk, Japanese Techno-Pop, Library and French.
Parisian party instigator Louise Chen pulls from a timeless well of classic & modern house, disco, soul, pop, & more.
Hip-hop and soul-inflected sounds from Richmond's Ohbliv.
The snack enthusiast and London based producer and DJ takes on the NTS airwaves. Stay tuned for mixes spanning the breadth of dance and club music.

All the biggest bangers from around the world with Mina and guests every month.
From the host: "This month I've got lots of new and unreleased music from myself + Styn, Bryte, Roska, L-Vis 1990 & Surreal Sessions. Plus a guestmix from Maganda Shakul, a producer and percussionist from Uganda, who is pioneering his own Bakisimbatronics sound."

Record maker, player and mixer Martelo - not Martello - joins the musical dots with sonic heat from across the globe. Taking us to hip-hop, RnB, electronica and elsewhere, Martelo keeps it tight and keeps the heat: non-stop classics.

EndgamE’s Precious Metals lines up the latest in the sounds of the underbelly: drill, auto-tune serenades and dancehall. 808 snares rattle like uzis, deep synths sing out like a foghorn through the spray – both grime and hip-hop, tunes for the club and tunes for the car. Lock in. therealendgame@gmail.com

Monthly inter-dimensional techno and rhythm transmissions from GiGi FM.
From the host: "This month GiGi FM welcomes Shoal. A full production ambient journey through motion, growth and tension from the Dutch sound artist and ISOTOOP core member ~ sculptural sound design and inner movement for Aries season’s shifting skies."

nts1.n: A LONG STRANGE DREAM W/ BATU
nts1.d:
A Long Strange Dream is Batu's latest series which allows him to showcase a selection of music not limited to peak time dancefloors. Expect a deep dive into the crates for an eclectic mix of influences and oddities as you transcend into a collective long strange dream.
From the host: "loads of new tracks this month! shouts to everyone sending me dubs!"

Uruguay DJ, producer and sound designer Lechuga Zafiro presents “La Mano” (The Hand). It is an ode to the creative hands of the world. Hands that translate ideas and modify sound: hit the drum, touch the knobs, excite the strings. ��La Mano” is always recorded live on CDJS and DJMs. Five fingers for rhythm, texture, weirdness, acoustic phenomena and sabor. Five fingers for each stroke of “La Clave”.
From the host: "La Mano comes back to club music. Heavy intervention on the 3 decks and the mixer. Sabrosa dance music with a pinch of weirdness. Unreleased stuff and upcoming releases from friends + some not so oldies."

nts1.n: Jupiter Presents: The Saturday Man by Aaliyah Bilal w/ Angel Dat Dawid & D-Composed
nts1.d: A story Aaliyah Bilal and a score by Angel Bat Dawid & D-Composed in response to 'And to Us is the return' (2025) by Armina Mussa Howada (Featured D-Composed Ensemble Members: Caitlin Edwards (Violin), Khelsey Zarraga (Violin), Seth Pae (Viola), Tahirah Whittington (Cello), Recording: The Land School, Recording & Post-Production Engineer: Dave Vettraino).

nts1.n: Jupiter Presents: they'll talk about us plenty by Tembe Denton-Hurst w/ Ivy Sole
Jupiter Presents: Ballistics by Briana Pickens w/ BSA Gold

nts1.d: Irish avant pop artist Maria Somerville traverses heady concepts through classic pop, ambient, experimental, spoken word, & field recordings - now hosted ever morning for the NTS Breakfast Show here.

Veteran NTS host Hampus brings you Doing Time: a genre-agnostic monthly trip through quality records from around the world’s past and present, featuring regular appearances from musical friends and family.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ PAM
nts1.d: PAM, previously host of beloved NTS show Okonkole Y Trompa, joins you for a dawn chorus every Thursday. Traversing obscure rarities, globetrotting vintage pop and beyond.

nts1.d: Breakfast, in Flo motion - served by Flo Dill, every Monday to Thursday.
Low, slow and sleazy sounds with babyschön, in the studio for the early afternoon shift…

Shanti is a veteran and expert in four to the floor dance music - tune in once a month for a considered two-hour selection of the best house and techno around, transmitting direct from the NTS studio.
Zurich-based Jenny Cara brings the heat with a blend of cheerful and hard-hitting house, creating a space that celebrates community, history, and pure energy.
Monthly missives from a London club selector par excellence - expect anything from finger-on-the-pulse UK rap, to deep house and techno records, to jungle & UK funky.

Andalusian, Berlin-based producer and DJ Chico Blanco drops in monthly with a mix of his top picks — shades of house, underground Spanish pop, dreamy romantic melodies, plus guest spots from longtime friends sharing their favourites.
From the host: "Chico Blanco invites Juguete"
International party starters from Huntleys + Palmers label head.
Hip-hop scholar, esteemed illustrator and all round head Ted Draws holds down a killer 2 hour monthly slot on Thursday afternoons.

nts1.n: THE TIM & BARRY SHOW w/ Tim b2b DJ spinn & Traxman
nts1.d:
Tim & Barry have been documenting underground music since 2000. Journey from the latest UK drill back in time to classic grime and garage, all the way to afrobeats, dancehall and RnB. Expect the unexpected.
From the host: "In this special mix recorded live on Tim's Farm,  Tim Goes B2B with 2 Chicago legends for 3 sets firstly footwork vs Dennery segment with DJ spinn for the first 40 mins a theme continued in the second B2B with Traxman. The final 40 mins is a multigenre excursion with the final B2B with Corky Strong."

nts1.n: RADIO SUPER W/ ROSA PISTOLA - Tributo a EROS
nts1.d:
RADIO SUPER is Rosa Pistola’s show on NTS where she dives deep into the Latin American underground. Each episode zooms into different genres, scenes, and vibes, tracing their roots and evolution. It’s all about the sounds, the stories, and the pillars of the underground.
From the host: "This mixtape is a tribute to Eros. Eros is a young Colombian producer who specializes in BALA, a subgenre of guaracha with a denser, darker sound."

In an era of everything at once, Gabrielle Kwarteng is a DJ who weaves stories that often look to the past, while always focusing on possible futures. Dip in to hear a range of dance music from downtempo to deep house to techno rollers - and everything in between.

MOBBS chopping up whatever he finds 
ohm.mobbs@protonmail.com

Cutting edge East African club sounds from Kampala's finest party starter.
From the host: "Masaka Masaka is a Ugandan Producer, DJ and Artist, his work features heavily mangled sound designs, percussion, Jazz influence, deconstructed sonics juxtaposed with chopped vocals and occasional basslines; His EP "Barely Making Much was released on Hakuna Kulala"
Classic Jamaican records from the "Rocksteady Daddy" Asher G.
London & Istanbul based record label Zel Zele takes us on a monthly ride to earthly wonders and cosmic adventures. Bite the lip take the trip!
Organic soundscapes, field recordings and melodic ambience from composer Sophia Zhuravkova.

Regular techno, electro, and house missives from the Against Interpretation labelhead and powerhouse-club selector Courtesy…
Skee's insight through his daily listening habits, from guilty pleasures to casual recommendations and in between…

nts1.n: UP YA ARCHIVES w/ Chinese Daughter
nts1.d: Star UK producer Nia Archives takes over the NTS airwaves in a weekly residency, celebrating the launch of Up Ya Archives, the new record label playing host to the best of new gen junglism.

Martha is a DJ and broadcaster from South London with roots in community radio and documentary making. Each of Martha’s thoughtfully curated radio shows navigate the fringes of electronic music, with a clear narrative that is grounded in community and storytelling.
Serving up monthly discoveries in clubland and beyond.

Jakarta trio Ali and Lebanese multi-instrumentalist Charif Megarbane present a medley of music representing the longstanding cultural exchange between Indonesia and the Arab world.

nts1.n: Highlife & Sunshine Show w/ Tryb & ZENA
nts1.d:
Music of the sun, a.k.a the tracks your dad used to play on a Saturday afternoon… 
Join TrYb as she explores West African pop, high life, soukous and more.

nts1.n: NTS Guide to: Jazz In Murakami Novels (R)
nts1.d:
'Somewhere along the line Coltrane’s soprano sax runs out of steam. Now it’s McCoy Tyner’s piano solo I hear, the left hand carving out a repetitious rhythm and the right layering on thick, forbidding chords. Like some mythic scene, the music portrays somebody’s - a nameless, faceless somebody’s - dim past, all the details laid out as clearly as entrails being dragged out of the darkness. Or at least that’s how it sounds to me. The patient, repeating music ever so slowly breaks apart the real, rearranging the pieces. It has a hypnotic, menacing smell, just like the forest' - Kafka On The Shore
Music, and specifically jazz, has always featured heavily in the literary imagination of Haruki Murakami. In this radio special, NTS lays down two hours of jazz records as featured throughout Murakami's corpus.
Cuban record collector, ethnomusicologist researcher, picks up a hour of global vinyl via Havana every months.

Bolero was once termed the 'quintessential Latin American romantic song of the twentieth century', a Cuban genre originating in the late 19th century out of the trova tradition, pioneered by troubadour Pepe Sánchez. Boleros are defined by their love-themed lyrics, but musically can take a variety of forms; as such it has such been fused with a variety of other genres over time such as rumba, mambo, and chachachá - giving the genre such an enduring and timeless quality.
The boleros were originally sung by single trovadores playing guitar, but it soon became popular for trovadores to play in groups as dúos, tríos, cuartetos. After gaining global popularity, boleros paved the way for the filin movement in Havana - a word derived from feeling, describing a style of post-microphone jazz-influenced romantic song.
This Guide To covers the most romantic exitos of the genres bolero and filin, crooners for the month of love.
The Cleaners From Venus frontman presents an eclectic mix of pop music, comedy, and more.

Time Is Away and somewhere else every month on NTS. Each hour-long show uses music, alongside snippets of spoken word, to produce something that is part soundscape, part essay for the radio. Host portraits by Robin Silas Christian (@makinabooks)

nts1.n: NTS GUIDE TO: INDIAN CLASSICAL INFLUENCE ON JAZZ (R)
nts1.d: In the mid-1950s musicians such as Yusuf Lateef and John Coltrane started to use instruments, structure and patterns based on traditional Indian music.  Artists sought to expand the boundaries of jazz by incorporating the modal structures, improvisational depth, and spiritual ethos of Indian music. Listen to two hours of Jazz inspired by Indian Classical.

nts1.n: WAVE FORM W/ AURÉLIEN ARBET & PIERRE ROUSSEAU (R)
nts1.d:
During 'Wave Form', Aurélien Arbet and Pierre Rousseau explore an interpolation of various recordings, often at the margins of traditional formats and conventional styles of music. During an hour, a long oscillation is formed out of a selection of interviews, spoken word, poetry, music for dance or art, marginal pop attempts and various field recordings.
Aurélien Arbet is an art director, co-founder of the clothing brand and publishing house Études. Pierre Rousseau is a composer, producer and sound designer, crafting sounds for records, films, and art projects.

A relatively new arrival in the ever-evolving lineage of internet-native musical microgenres, barber beats takes its name from the semi-serious genre label given to genre originator Haircuts For Men by their label Aloe City Wrld. The barber beats sound takes multiple cues from vaporwave, pulling similar sample material – lounge pop, muzak, digital 1980s productions – and condensing vaporwave's sedative haziness into sleekly mastered downtempo trip hop and hip hop instrumentals.

nts1.d:
Wire are an English rock band, formed in London in October 1976. by Colin Newman (vocals, guitar), Graham Lewis (bass, vocals), Bruce Gilbert (guitar) and Robert Gotobed (drums). This edition of In Focus takes a look at their career and other projects they were involved in, including the work of Dome, AC Marias, P'O, He Said and Colin Newman's solo material.

“Aquecimento" dives into Brazil’s widely eclectic and regional Electronic / Baile Funk music scene with Producer/DJ VHOOR. The show connects the past and present while providing hints to the future, highlighting the bubbling underground community, and providing a platform for a more diverse array of artists to be exposed to a worldwide audience.
From the host: "Aquecimento is back with a special guest mix from DJ/Producer holandês from Recife, Brazil.  Also, my new album "De Keke!" is out on April 8th and I play a couple of tracks from the album.  Hope you enjoy it!"

Uruguay DJ, producer and sound designer Lechuga Zafiro presents “La Mano” (The Hand). It is an ode to the creative hands of the world. Hands that translate ideas and modify sound: hit the drum, touch the knobs, excite the strings. “La Mano” is always recorded live on CDJS and DJMs. Five fingers for rhythm, texture, weirdness, acoustic phenomena and sabor. Five fingers for each stroke of “La Clave”.

nts1.n: Plastic Language w/ Florence Shaw (Dry Cleaning)

nts1.d:
For a band whose identity in pop culture is often subject to oversimplified definitions and reductive jokes, The Grateful Dead's music frequently evades concise pigeonholing. Formed in California during the high watermark of post-war US counterculture, the Dead weave a psychedelic tapestry of folk, jazz, bluegrass, country, gospel, reggae, rock and blues, where the beginnings and ends of each influence disappear. 
Their trademark marathon live performances, loose improvisational style and famously loyal fans make The Grateful Dead arguably a one of a kind phenomenon in popular music. 
Selected and mixed by Jennifer Walton.

nts1.n: NTS Guide to: Romanian electronic music at the turn of the millennium
nts1.d:
In the years following Romania's violent revolution in 1989, the country – like many other former Eastern Bloc nations at the time – went through a period of dizzying new cultural influence. New Western ideas, alongside the growth of the internet saw a particular explosion in electronic music Romania. Unshackled by pre-existing traditions and established forms, the music was hopeful, forward-thinking, and completely sui generis. 
"The music at that time was not segregated by commercial or underground appeal; there wasn’t even much gender segregation, and everything was built on a foundation of naive club music made with rudimentary studio equipment and angelic, almost infantile pop vocals that spoke of an idealistic world of love."
Selected and mixed by Dragoș Munteanu.
nts1.j: Romanian electronic music at the turn of the millennium

8ULENTINA is an interdisciplinary artist, music producer and DJ based in Brooklyn, New York. Known for his dynamic DJ sets and music composition synthesizing a range of genres, geographies and tempos. He co-founded CLUB CHAI (2016-2021), a record label and curatorial platform with Lara Sarkissian and currently runs a SILLAGE, a platform for contemporary sonic artifacts. His NTS show explores the sounds that inspire his production process, focusing on experimentation, percussive intensity, and non linear storytelling through sound.

nts1.n: Fifth World w/ Ian Kim Judd (R)
nts1.d: A riff on Jon Hassell's Fourth World series. Where Hassell focused on creating ritual music of potential societies that didn't exist; Ian Kim Judd seeks to evoke the notion of potential utopian societies that could exist. In these times of global instability and uncertainty, Fifth World seeks to posit an alternative, with scenes from an optimistic future.

Takashi Yoshimatsu, born in Tokyo in 1953, is one of Japan's most renowned living classical composers. First gaining recognition in the early 1980s with atonal, serialist compositions like "Threnody to Toki" (1981). Yoshimatsu later became disillusioned with atonal music, opting to compose lush neo-romantic sounds taking influence from popular rock and jazz. Yoshimatsu also notably composed the soundtrack for the 2003 film adaption of Astro Boy.
Image: Naoki Hashimoto
