Producer and DJ Jay Donaldson, also known as Palms Trax, brings his selections to NTS' Channel 1 for a two-hour session every other month.

Artist, producer, label owner and DJ Lee Gamble bringing an oneiric bi-monthly show of current sound and music from his hallucinated musical continuum. Expect anything from Drill to Dream pop, Conceptual Sound and Jazz to bleached out rave memories..
Nick Malkin hosts Post-Geography, featuring ambient and atmospheric selections, live from the NTS studio in L.A.

Your attentive participation, thru listening with your ears and your heart, will allow you to enjoy fully this exchange of ideas, to sense these various moods, and to reap the full therapeutic rewards that good music always brings to a tired, disturbed soul and all who dig the sounds. – Mary Lou Williams, 1964

nts1.n: Nāda Sonic w/ Sway Of The Verses
nts1.d: Take a journey into Raga and Tala based music, and find out the intricacies of this ever evolving ancient musical tradition as we weave our way though musicians, instruments, ragas and time-cycles.

nts1.n: THE ONE GLOVE BREAKFAST SHOW W/ MACCA
nts1.d: Stay left and take a trip with Macca's Saturday breakfast show on NTS. Weekend musings and eclectic cuts from all over the place, it's a low-key affair and you're all invited. ONE GLOVE!!!

British-Nigerian DJ Dare Balogun traces the journey of African rhythm across time and space, exploring how traditional genres like highlife, juju, soukous and makossa laid the foundation for modern dance music.

nts1.n: THE GREAT AFRICAN DISCO W/ TEMI KOGBE (R)
nts1.d: Lagos based collector and DJ Temi Kogbe traces connections in African popular culture through vinyl.

Music of the sun, a.k.a the tracks your dad used to play on a Saturday afternoon… 
Join TrYb as she explores West African pop, high life, soukous and more.
DJ, Run Dem Crew Founder, and community builder Charlie Dark MBE spins gems from his record collection once a month.
Music will be played once a month.
Jabu and Andy Payback bring us Music 4 Lovers once a month.

Journeyman of the LA music scene since the 1960s, multi-instrumentalist and songwriter Tommy Peltier shares an hour of original recordings and inspirations ahead of the release of “Echo Park: The ‘70s Sessions” out now on Drag City.

Experimental and intercepted transmissions every month with Field Work friends and family. Accompanied by GIFs at http://fieldworklondon.tumblr.com.
From the host: "Two hours of skewed chamber music, for April the cruellest month."
Music to dent the canister
Nitetrax presents a monthly exploration of classic and contemporary club and experimental music.
Sounds of the Dawn is a monthly two hour session through new age, ambient, environmental, meditation and relaxation cassettes from the seventies to the nineties. Tune in and zone out.
Visual artist and founder and editor-in-chief of The Editorial Magazine Claire Milbrath joins us for an hour of ambient, classical, and italian library music.

nts1.n: NTS Guide to: Jazz In Murakami Novels (R)
nts1.d:
'Somewhere along the line Coltrane’s soprano sax runs out of steam. Now it’s McCoy Tyner’s piano solo I hear, the left hand carving out a repetitious rhythm and the right layering on thick, forbidding chords. Like some mythic scene, the music portrays somebody’s - a nameless, faceless somebody’s - dim past, all the details laid out as clearly as entrails being dragged out of the darkness. Or at least that’s how it sounds to me. The patient, repeating music ever so slowly breaks apart the real, rearranging the pieces. It has a hypnotic, menacing smell, just like the forest' - Kafka On The Shore
Music, and specifically jazz, has always featured heavily in the literary imagination of Haruki Murakami. In this radio special, NTS lays down two hours of jazz records as featured throughout Murakami's corpus.

nts1.n: NTS GUIDE TO: JAZZ FROM THE EASTERN BLOC (R)
nts1.d: A selection of jazz recordings, produced by artists from across the former Eastern Bloc – Polish fusion, Bulgarian jazz-rock, Czech post-bop and more.
The Cleaners From Venus frontman presents an eclectic mix of pop music, comedy, and more.

nts1.n: TIME IS AWAY - DoE (Can a Tape Machine Improvise?)
nts1.d:
Time Is Away and somewhere else every month on NTS. Each hour-long show uses music, alongside snippets of spoken word, to produce something that is part soundscape, part essay for the radio. Host portraits by Robin Silas Christian (@makinabooks)
From the host: "This recording is the only surviving long-form tape collage made by Kurt Rapp during his time at the the Department of Euterpe (DoE). Efforts to identify its constituent parts suggest it cannot have been made any earlier than 1987. Research is ongoing."
Get radiant with Ruby Savage's mixed bag of goodies.

Heavy vinyl records collector Dr. Kruger digs deep every other Sunday to bring soulful, funky and odd tunes from his crates straight to your home. Sometimes joined by a fellow crate digger/DJ of his choice, just tune in and let the doctor treat you.

Veteran NTS host Hampus brings you Doing Time: a genre-agnostic monthly trip through quality records from around the world’s past and present, featuring regular appearances from musical friends and family.

Veteran NTS host Hampus brings you Doing Time: a genre-agnostic monthly trip through quality records from around the world’s past and present, featuring regular appearances from musical friends and family.
Basma shares songs and stories from passings through Khartoum and beyond.

nts1.n: A Month of Sundays w/ Bill Spencer
nts1.d:
Bill Spencer shares weekend recordings from his living room studio in Detroit, Michigan, from where he brings a range of soulful jazz rock, and ambient sounds, spanning to electronica and psychedelic dub.
From the host: "A focus on reggae, dub, and roots"
Music Supervisor and DJ Dhamirah Coombes brings a monthly multi-genre curation of m00d-bending tracks intended for a deep listening experience.

nts1.n: THE GREAT AFRICAN DISCO W/ TEMI KOGBE
nts1.d:
Lagos based collector and DJ Temi Kogbe traces connections in African popular culture through vinyl.
From the host: "This is a sample mix of 80s-90s reggae, mostly digital. A lot of these artistes were considered fringe but they captured the zeitgeist like no other. Translating into sound what many mainstream artistes couldn't pick up. Radio signals transmitting from the heart and soul. Big up to Chris de Souza PT"
French-Mongolian singer, songwriter and guitarist, harpist and zither player Céline Dessberg stops by the London studio, playing jazz, soundtracks, Mongolian sounds, leftfield pop and more.

nts1.n: A COLOURFUL STORM - A Day Without Rain
nts1.d: A Colourful Storm from label head Moopie, featuring contemplative ambience, folksong and indie-pop melancholia, contemporary electronics and beyond.
Ziggy Devriendt is the DJ behind Nosedrip. Tune in once a month for an expert selection of any or all of the following: new age, new wave, French psych, minimal synth, post-punk and a whole lot else…
Australian duo Otologic traverse wave, post punk, outsider club & dubwise meanderings in constant delay.

“I start associating your voice with ….”, for a while ML has been part of the NTS community, jingling around, airing his monthly 150 Session out of Berlin’s North, where all seasons give reasons. For clarification, creation, inspiration. One hour of poetic and musical explorations, questions, and curiosities. Circling around daring electronics, ethno-sonics, jazz-constructivism, minimalism, open structured digital dopamine, risky dub excitement and other sounds, that still need to be written. No country, no flag – outernational without a cause!

Your attentive participation, thru listening with your ears and your heart, will allow you to enjoy fully this exchange of ideas, to sense these various moods, and to reap the full therapeutic rewards that good music always brings to a tired, disturbed soul and all who dig the sounds. – Mary Lou Williams, 1964

Irish indie songwriter Nashpaints jumps on for a guest show ahead of the release of his new record "Everyone Good Is Called Molly", playing original material, music from friends and inspirations. 
Opening for Maria Somerville across Europe this winter, dates here.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ MARIA SOMERVILLE
nts1.d:
Drifting into a new day with Maria Somerville, live from Ireland's wild west coast. 
Touring across UK, Europe and USA this winter, tickets here.

nts1.n: THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
nts1.d: Breakfast, in Flo motion - served by Flo Dill, every Monday to Thursday.

nts1.n: Soup To Nuts w/ John Gómez
nts1.d: Collector John Gómez brightens up Mondays with some music from different times and different places.
The London songwriter, producer and musician shares a selection of favourites, influences, newly discovered music, and some of her own tunes, joined occasionally by friends and guests.

Sink into some downtempo sounds; tease nostalgia with obscure, experimental and retro blends of 80s synth-pop, wave and post punk; or simply immerse yourself in some 'real bathtub trippers’ with Bristol-based selector Tilly.
The Shane Lavers-fronted NYC indie group return to the NTS airwaves.

Mississippi Records - Excavating the future since 2005.
From the host: "Wild and free music that kept us going the last few months. Hear the ever-radiant Toody Cole in 1980 and in 2025, and songs from new and upcoming records by Dragging an Ox Thru Water, MerKaBa Brotherhood, Ata Ratu, Bulayo, etc etc…"

Naomi Asa on the NTS airwaves, delivering soothing soul and spiritual jazz.
From the host: "Naomi Asa interviews Sage Elsesser (aka Navy Blue) at Electric Lady Studios in NYC.
Navy Blue stands as one of underground hip-hop’s most visionary artists, approaching his craft with the literary precision of a novelist and the narrative ambition of a filmmaker."
Welcome to Club Coco: a place where globe-trotting vinyl hunter Coco María shares her joyous discoveries - jazz, fusion, international pop, vintage latin music of all flavours…
Access Bitter Babe's personal vault.
Another missive from the outer reaches of ambient and downtempo production courtesy of mu tate.

Cherrystones - musician, international DJ, soundtrack composer, compiler and multi-genre music fiend - within his longstanding NTS shows Cherrystones showcases his inspirations, his passions and his listening habits as a sonic moodboard pooled from his revered archive encompassing the past, present and future sounds to soothe and challenge in equal measure.
From the host: "Music to inspire, enlighten, confuse and enjoy!"
Life on wide mode.
2 hour bi-monthly excursions into sound and dissonance. Music label with John T. Gast. Regular guest selections from friends and affiliates.

nts1.n: WAVE FORM W/ AURÉLIEN ARBET & PIERRE ROUSSEAU (R)
nts1.d:
During 'Wave Form', Aurélien Arbet and Pierre Rousseau explore an interpolation of various recordings, often at the margins of traditional formats and conventional styles of music. During an hour, a long oscillation is formed out of a selection of interviews, spoken word, poetry, music for dance or art, marginal pop attempts and various field recordings.
Aurélien Arbet is an art director, co-founder of the clothing brand and publishing house Études. Pierre Rousseau is a composer, producer and sound designer, crafting sounds for records, films, and art projects.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ MARIA SOMERVILLE
nts1.d:
Drifting into a new day with Maria Somerville, live from Ireland's wild west coast. 
Touring across UK, Europe and USA this winter, tickets here.

nts1.n: THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
nts1.d: Breakfast, in Flo motion - served by Flo Dill, every Monday to Thursday.
Listen in for positivity and light as Anu broadcasts through the afternoon.

nts1.n: Low Life Loves You w/ Bill Brewster
Exploring Earth Through Sound w/ Machi -  Argentine Rock (60s-90s)
nts1.d: From the host: "The evolution of Argentine Rock through some of my favorite artists, bands, and tracks. In chronological order, from 1965 - 2000. Focuses mainly on Luis Alberto Spinetta (Almendra, Pescado Rabioso, Invisible), Charly García (Serú Girán, Sui Generis), Gustavo Cerati (Soda Stereo)."

nts1.n: QUEST NO MORE W/ ELHEIST & Ib Kamara
nts1.d: Elheist steers deep‑cuts, left‑field electronica, obscure grooves and sounds from the fam into the fold, where worlds collide, textures shift and surprises hit. check your quest, leave the rest.
Sounds from the grass roots of jungle and drum & bass with London scene heroes Rupture - featuring selections from residents Mantra & Double O.
Bristol based Londoner and Soft Raw label owner Danielle, takes over for an hour a month, running through a broad spectrum of dance music - from ambient to leftfield, techno, electro & beyond.
Melbourne based producer YL HOOI share an hour of dub, electronics, postpunk and more every month…
Elena Colombi helps to keep nights weird - expect a diverse range of up-and-at-'em records: from techno to post-punk, through EBM, industrial and -wave…
Sample excavator, music historian, and compilation curator DJ Amir selects a few influences each month - from house to disco, hip-hop to jazz.

Artist, graphic designer and DJ Paul Camo sets out to push the boundaries: free of all restrictions. Jump in the chat and listen as Paul Camo's WE ARE brings you on a voyage through the spectrum of jazz fusion, spiritual jazz, experimental jazz and everything in between.
Sounds of the Dawn is a monthly two hour session through new age, ambient, environmental, meditation and relaxation cassettes from the seventies to the nineties. Tune in and zone out.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ SPIRIT BLUE & Peach
nts1.d: Dawn moods for the early birds, courtesy of Sleepier & Impey aka Spirit Blue.

nts1.n: THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
nts1.d: Breakfast, in Flo motion - served by Flo Dill, every Monday to Thursday. Today Flo is joined by 85 year old Dutch musician Ronald Laangestraat: having spent his life playing music in lounges and lobbies, as well as writing and recording music privately, in the 2010s Ronald's music was discovered and reissued by label South of North. Since then, he's been releasing more brilliant, off kilter songs and playing shows in Europe - not in lounges and lobbies this time, but to paying customers! He and his band are in the studio to play three tracks live for us.

nts1.n: SOUP TO NUTS W/ SHY ONE
nts1.d: DJ & producer Shy One oversees the chatroom nonsense and brings that necessary get-up-and-go vibe every single hump day…
Dance music with feeling, lunch time with Finn

Rhythm Section Radio was founded in 2009 and moved to NTS in 2012, and has since pulled out the stops breaking new music, promoting independent labels and paying homage to the greatest music from the past, through a perhaps unhealthy obsession with record digging.

nts1.n: Moxie W/ Ariane V (Nervous Records feature)
nts1.d: Part of the NTS family since day dot, Moxie has held her Wednesday show since the stations first broadcasts. Inviting esteemed guests for interviews & mixes and breaking new, unreleased music, she’s got your Wednesday afternoons covered… Tune in to hear anything from Soulful House, Garage, Afro beats and all the way through to rolling techno.
After work fun and global tunes with Tash LC and guests every other Wednesday

nts1.n: TOUCHING BASS W/ ERROL & Maxwell Owin
nts1.d: Join Errol and the Touching Bass family every month for regular voyages into inner-city, concrete jungle productions plus astral-facing funk, jazz, broken beat and anything else with a soulful pulse.
NTS's own Giulia Tess pulls us leftfield once a month.
Unrelenting and cutting edge funk, electro and club sounds from Brazil’s rising star Lucas Kid.
Fraternal party starters The Twins takeover for regular trips into electroclash, experimental electronics, and Desi sounds, inviting on boundary pushing collaborators and friends.

Croatian DIY label Low Income $quad showcase their catalogue and extended family of producers - Expect neo trance sounds, reworked Memphis rap acapellas, 90s-inspired fast techno and noisy experimentalism.
Dutch-Argentinian producer Jasmín looks ahead to a big summer after a breakthrough Hessle Audio release and a new project on fabric Originals with an hour of unconventional dancefloor rhythms.

nts1.n: Rắn Cạp Đuôi w/ ريما نوباني Reema Nubani
nts1.d:
Traversing East and South East Asian contemporary music with Ho Chi Minh City crew Rắn Cạp Đuôi.
From the host: "Reema Nubani, a Palestinian multidisciplinary artist born in occupied upper Galilee, currently based in Geneva. Her practice is rooted in poetic writing, research of language and materials constructing a vocabulary expressing the life under occupation."

nts1.n: Women of Video Game Music: Yuka Kitamura (R)
nts1.d: As lead composer at FromSoftware, the Japanese developer deeply beloved by hardcore gamers across the world, Yuka Kitamura has soundtracked the virtual deaths of millions of players. Her music, which soundtrack the tough as nails, worlds of Dark Souls, Elden Ring, and Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice, is as tense and foreboding as the worlds themselves.

nts1.n: THE EARLY BIRD SHOW W/ PAM & Rocheman
nts1.d: PAM, previously host of beloved NTS show Okonkole Y Trompa, joins you for a dawn chorus every Thursday. Traversing obscure rarities, globetrotting vintage pop and beyond.

nts1.n: THE NTS BREAKFAST SHOW W/ FLO
nts1.d: Breakfast, in Flo motion - served by Flo Dill, every Monday to Thursday.
Low, slow and sleazy sounds with babyschön, in the studio for the early afternoon shift…
Musical peregrinations from Gabi, guiding listeners through new wave, post punk and synth.

Longtime NTS resident, producer, vocalist and DJ Fauzia guides us through the multiplicity of her influences and tastes for an hour each month. Expect everything from frenetic club productions to classical composition, experimental electronics and beyond.
Deep, soulful, rootsy flavas across house, hip hop, boogie, soul and broken beat from the Secretsundaze founder who is now doing his own thing.
A power hour of classic jungle records from one of the genre's most consistently quality record labels in the 1990s, Joker Records.
Monthly missives from a London club selector par excellence - expect anything from finger-on-the-pulse UK rap, to deep house and techno records, to jungle & UK funky.
Friday club energy from LDN producer, DJ & More Time label head Ahadadream.

nts1.n: GHOST NOTES WORLDWIDE W/ Impey & Sonice
nts1.d: Tune into Ghost Notes Worldwide for that post-twilight sound. Night time music of all strains, with blunted R&B and hip hop instrumentals, neo-downtempo productions, slow jams, and hazy dancefloor sounds.

The cult post-rock-electronic band Seefeel return this year with “Sol.Hz”, their first album in 15 years, out Friday May 1st via Warp Records. Sarah Peacock picks out an hour of inspirations in a special guest show.
friends&, the Toronto producers behind the sprawling, relentless 116-track album “folx” present Brainrot Psychedelia.
Somewhere between banging your head against a brick wall and holding your breath under water.
Danish label Posh Isolation join us for an hour a month.

nts1.n: CHANNELING W/ IVAN SMAGGHE & NATHAN GREGORY WILKINS (R)
nts1.d: Old friends and hapless mavericks Ivan Smagghe and Nathan Gregory Wilkins present a fortnightly window into their ramshackle musical world. A show with absolutely no rules (as they'd only break them). We love the unmixable, old & new. We are oddballs and we love you.

nts2.n: NTS GUIDE TO: INDIAN CLASSICAL INFLUENCE ON JAZZ (R)
nts2.d: In the mid-1950s musicians such as Yusuf Lateef and John Coltrane started to use instruments, structure and patterns based on traditional Indian music.  Artists sought to expand the boundaries of jazz by incorporating the modal structures, improvisational depth, and spiritual ethos of Indian music. Listen to two hours of Jazz inspired by Indian Classical.

Julien Dechery and David Pinhas set out to capture the meditative beauty and poetry of Malayalam filmmaker Aravindan Govindan’s craft, exploring his film scores and sound design.
Featuring music by Aravindan Govindan himself, along with Hariprasad Chaurasia, Salil Chowdhury, Rajeev Taranath, K. Raghavan, M.B. Sreenivasan, Janardhan, M.G. Radhakrishnan, G. Devarajan, Mohan Sithra, and K.J. Yesudas.

nts2.n: RŌNIN SHUTSUGEN W/ SLOWERPACE 音楽 (R)
nts2.d: 60 minutes of dreamscapes from the Slowerpace’s vaporwave lab.

Theory Therapy is a label, mix series and radio show run by Low End Theorists, broadcasting from Sydney. Expect sun-drenched ambient and downtempo-adjacent sounds, inspired by late ’90s/early ’00s labels like Instinct Ambient and Elektrolux, plus unreleased music from artists like Ike Zwanikken, Slowfoam and Ben Bondy.

nts2.n: THE WONDER CABINET w/ Mothanna Hussein
nts2.d:
Sounds and recordings from resident artists at the Wonder Cabinet space in Cremisan Valley, Bethlehem.
From the host: "Wonder Cabinet on NTS  invites Mothanna Hussein for a 1 hour section of music."

Broken club, dubbed out sounds, experimental electronics and atonal drifts: Gio’s monthly broadcasts are high contrast assemblages that move at a slow pace.
From the host: "This month my friend Mika joins me with a wonderful guest mix, as usual in the second half of the show."
Kaifeng based DJ & producer Yu Su cooks up an hour of warm meanderings once a month.
Monthly mixes and "Bad behaviour" from the crew behind Berlin's acclaimed Mala Junta parties. Expect throwback trance, souped up '90s basslines and party music of a high tempo flavour.
A blast of new dancefloor styles from Wamena, Indonesia from Asep Nayak, the producer sharing uptempo electronic club sounds rooted in the traditional music of the Paupan Highlands.

Chester, PA’s DJ FINNAHITALICK (Blackwealth in the club) is a seasoned sound archivist of Underground Hip-hop. Starting Hit A Lick Radio in 2016, DJ FINNAHITALICK explores rap’s extensive subgenres and regional epicenters, ranging from DMV crank and Atlanta Plugg to Milwaukee low-end, Michigan punch-rap and beyond. His fast-paced mixes encapsulate Underground Hip-hop’s diverse landscape, and are not for the weak-hearted.
Native Sound System join us once a month, breaking the latest sounds from Lagos

HD features a vast variety of guests spanning across hip hop, RnB and electronic music to talk about music, culture and life aswell as share their favorite selections of any genre.
From the host: "This episode is pure energy in the mix with HD including techno, guaracha, dembow, freestyle + more."
Head of Grimy Edits, Zernell Gillie's style can range from the heart of disco to the soul of Chicago house - he heads up a 2 hour slot once a month from our LA studio.

nts2.n: KRISTIN KONTROL PRESENTS: OUT OF VOGUE
nts2.d:
Former frontwoman of rock outfit Dum Dum Girls eschews the pseudonym with her new solo project, Kristin Kontrol. Newly relocated to LA, Kristin delivers a monthly mix of expansive and hypnotic sounds: one part Durutti Column to two parts Alice Coltrane.
From the host: "gentle luvin"
Every month Jonah Rabb brings you Weekend Read, an hour of funk, house, contemporary R&B, and more.

nts2.n: Lunar New Year: Mahjong Special w/ Straight Honey
nts2.d: NTS marks Lunar New Year 2025, ushering in the Year of the Wood Snake with a series of broadcasts �� featuring guest hosts representing the range of East Asian countries that celebrate every year.

An hour a month from LA label Total Stasis, responsible for releases by Ramzi, Aquarian Foundation, Anom Vitruv, Elysia Crampton, Bobo Eyes & Plush Throw, CS + Kreme, Fmvee, Second Language, Regularfantasy, Private Lesson, Alexi Baris, Laila Sakini, and Sebastiano Carghini.
Get to know Akito Hearnshaw - chief of the minimal club tracks. Tune in for his selections of burnt out, distorted, rhythmic movers. Bitcrushed, resonant snares clap and kicks punch���

A genre-spanning show focusing on rare grooves, classics, new productions and obscurities with a worldwide perspective and a spotlight on soul, synth pop and leftfield house. All curated with love by Javybz - Anya and Julia.
Vinyl only selections from TOPS’ personal collections
Low, slow, and sleazy sounds for your Friday afternoon. Expect post-punk, synth pop, -wave and more.
BUDGIE – serving up jazz, hip-hop, soul, beats & more from his sprawling record collection, once a month from our LA studio.
6O minutes of timeless jazz from Ogun Records, the London label formed by South African bassist Harry Miller, his wife Hazel Miller, and sound engineer Keith Beal. Selected by NTS.

nts2.n: Imagine Soundtracks w/ redkoffee: Michiko to Hatchin (R)
nts2.d:
Words, illustrations, and images all possess atmospheres. 
Imagine Soundtracks depicts the musical emotions that animations, literature, manga, movies, or video games inspire. This series, curated by Paris-based poet and DJ Clarisse Prévost, aka redkoffee, is presented with interpreted dialogues from the explored selection.
Geneva's DJ Fine plays piano-centered pieces, often accompanying compositions with spoken world and poems.

Monthly enqiuries into encounters in harmony, forms of hearing, and occasionally responding.
From the host: "Quyếch's debut album Quyển Trời, reordered from a storytelling perspective."
Contemporary British AV artist, collagist, and NTS host Mark Leckey takes to the airwaves to play oddities from his collection. Everything from doo-wop to gabber.

North London native and Hyperdub signee Loraine James is an electronic producer who effortlessly blends Electronica and IDM with whispers of jazz and ambience all wrapped up in the artists key avant-garde aesthetic and sensibility.

Loops, remixes, edits and reinterpretations from DJ and programmer miss sheitana, oscillating between post-club, rap, baltimore club, bass, ambient and remixes from the limbos of the internet.
From the host: "postclub ° stolen loops ° radical bass ° romantic sometimes �� classics ° collapsed tempo ° footwork ° misfit emotions ° emoraï ° remixs from internet limbos ° haunted rhythms ° magic mashups only
hectic +++++++"
A missive from the CPH underground with the curator of Unearth, the city’s new club night and “hyper-rhythmic realm”.

Optimo is JG Wilkes & JD Twitch; record label, and longstanding Glaswegian party. You can expect a lot from their new monthly slot, sure to dig up some obscurities from post-punk, new wave, EBM, house and techno. Bags of unreleased stuff, too.

nts2.n: THE LUNACY OF FLOWERS W/ DAVE ID
nts2.d: Dave I.D takes us through industrial, and techno – melodic and dark. New and classic goth, his selection sets him apart from most, branching decades and scenes.

Japanese hardcore punk records from the nation's first explosion of the genre. 
Selected by Los Hitters.

Over the last few years, the London based imprint, Hegoa, has taken great strides toward illuminating the shadowy world of contemporary music emerging from the Basque region, an autonomous community in northern Spain with its own distinct cultural traditions and language.

Los Hitters is the collective partnership of Joe Cristando (Couple Two T’ree) and Rene Lopez (Suavecito Souldies). A collaboration that links the two from Brooklyn, NY to Oakland, CA. Their mixes focus on soul sounds comprised of their vinyl collection.

nts2.n: FUSION BATCHES W/ DJ MORSE CODE
nts2.d: February Batch for the lovers!
New releases and current inspirations from your hosts Jonnine Standish and Nigel Yang aka HTRK.
Lex Records artist Boom Bip scrapes beneath the surface of the status quo by loosely basing his mixes on electronics and the new waves they produce.

nts2.n: The Last Playlist w/ Luis Felipe Farfán

nts2.d: The sound of Ukraine's independent music scene. Presented by Схема, a Kyiv-based collective that moves forward rave culture in Ukraine.
Another mixtape, another mistake. Come get broken with Noa Kurzweil (from VOICE ACTOR).

Lung Dart take over the NTS airwaves every fourth Monday at 10pm. Playing chopped & screwed, ambient, pop, experimental and more.
http://lungd.art/
i love music

Saddle up and ride out for two hours of western soundtracks, covering music from classics and contemporary takes on the genre.
Selected and mixed by Florence Scott-Anderton.

Transmissions from the Outer Rim/Inner Edge.
“you know the gates themselves are made of flood.”
-Nadia Marcus
“Explorations” is an attempt to shed light on offbeat music from India and around the world. Digging In India aka Nishant Mittal is a music archivist, DJ and record store owner based in New Delhi.
Maghreb K7 Club explores North African cassette culture with synth-heavy Raï, Chaoui, and Staifi from 1985–1997.

"Shakshouka" is a dialectal arabic word used in North Africa to reflect an unexpected mixture of elements that wouldn’t naturally go together.
From the host: "Ramadan Kareem everyone! This show is inspired by a recent escapade to the Sahara where I was supposed to play for Nomad Man Festival in Tunisia, Unfortunately I got caught in a desert storm and couldn't make it but those 7 hours stuck in the Sahara were definitely special!"
Prolific London based DIY & lo-fi pop producer Keel Her heads to the studio for a show once a month.

Lamin Fofana is an artist and musician currently located in New York. His music contrasts the reality of our world with what’s beyond, and explores questions of movement, migration, alienation and belonging. His releases include Works In Metal, Here Lies Universality, Unsettling Scores, and The Open Boat. Fofana is the founder of the imprint Black Studies and phase4.zip
From the host: _"A one-hour selection of closing tracks and exit music from Prince to Lubomyr Melnyk; from a somber reflection on black refusal and resisting closure to a triumphant and utterly sublime solo piano work, this show observes the lost art of the last song with pairings and contributions from Toumani Diabaté and Kayhan Kalhor, Rokia Traoré and Oumou Sangaré, Amina Alaoui and Abdullah Ibrahim, Shabaka and James Newton.
The show concludes with a live acoustic rendition of "Reflection" by Prince, accompanied by Wendy Melvoin of the Revolution, a particularly poignant track from his late career landmark album Musicology. Below is a link to a video of the performance on The Tavis Smiley Show from February 2004"_

nts2.n: THE BADDIS SOUND W/ DANNIELLA DEE - Live from Vinyl Club Jamaica
nts2.d: Inspired by the iconic reggae & ska sounds of her home, singer & producer Danniella Dee shares a monthly crate of dub-wise selections, soul-soothing rocksteady, and heavyweight roots to the studio.
Tune into Martyn's monthly show on 2 for some delectable jazz treats from over the Atlantic.

nts2.n: NTS GUIDE TO: SOULFUL SÉGA & ELECTRIC MALOYA FROM THE INDIAN OCEAN (R)
nts2.d:
In the 1970s, artists from Réunion and Mauritius began fusing island styles - Maloya and Séga - with psychedelic rock, funk, and soul. 
Séga, rooted in Afro-Creole rhythms and often sung in Mauritian Creole, developed as a celebratory dance music drawing from European, African, and Indian influences. Maloya, native to Réunion, originated in enslaved Malagasy and African communities - a slower, percussive style traditionally performed with vocals, kayamb and roulér, and associated with ritual, resistance, and later, political activism.
nts2.j: SOULFUL SÉGA & ELECTRIC MALOYA FROM THE INDIAN OCEAN

nts2.n: Saywite w/ Emperatriz Plácido San Martín
nts2.d:
Una hora seleccionada por Camila Palomino…un mapa de encuentros—entre voces y territorios.
From the host: _"Saywite invites Emperatriz Plácido San Martín, a Peruvian trans artist based in Brussels, to create a diaristic mix inspired by house parties in Lima—a meeting between the music of Afro‑Peruvians and migrants from the North, the Andes and the Amazon, tracing the lineages of our friends and families through salsa, cumbia, hip-hop, huayno and more." _
"Emperatriz shares: ¿Qué música sonaba mientras el vapor se escapaba por las ollas en la cocina de tu casa? Se me hace imposible no recordar a quienes extraño con canciones. Cada canción es una invocación a mis muertos y cada vida que yo misma tuve. Sabiduría profunda que cala con cada melodía. Refranes que despiertan nuestra memoria corporal y nos recuerdan que todo es pasajero. Letras que hablan del dolor para saber qué hacer la próxima vez que volvamos a sentirlo. Estos tiempos que miran al futuro olvidan que todo es cíclico. Mientras les comparto invocaciones a tiempos donde el dolor se superó con el deseo, les imagino bailando, escuchando las emociones en cada letra, exorcizando la tristeza de sus cuerpos."

Alice "Turiya" Coltrane was a visionary pianist, composer, and bandleader who spent the majority of her life seeking spiritually in both music and her private life. Curator and DJ Edna Martinez explores the champeta, an underground musical genre and dance that originated in the Caribbean coast of Colombia in the early 1980s. The rhythms and influences are said to have arrived with the sailors from West Africa in the 1960s and 70s. We will get to know in each episode its specificities and the relevance of this scene.
From the host: "A tribute to a legendary Cartagena picó El Parrandero since 1978, championing soca and Caribbean rhythms. The pure euphoria of Colombian caribbean sound system culture."

French vocalist & songwriter Isabelle Antena initially found success as a member of Antena, famous for birthing a quietly influential electro bossa nova sound on their debut "Camino Del Sol". Isabelle further explored latin styles including samba in her solo career, which continued into the early 2000s. Her work has remained a favourite of DJs whose selections skew relaxed and sunny, with Antena and solo tracks continually surfacing on compilations and remix albums. 
This In Focus is an hour exploring the best of Isabelle Antena, created by Pepsi of Bruits de la Passion.

Inspired by Quince��era parties in the hills of Mexico and beyond & rooted in partying as a form of immigrant resistance. La Cosecha was started by XOLO. Playing all Spanish, all vinyl norteñias, corridos, rancheras, cumbia and banda classics. Xolo's aim is to bring this music to a new generation.

Based in Cotonou, Benin, which at the time was referred to as Dahomey, Albarika Records was founded by the French-born, Benin-based producer and entrepreneur, Emmanuel "Manu" Dibango, who had a significant influence on the local music scene. The label was instrumental in releasing records from some of the region's most prominent musicians during the golden age of African music.
Many of the artists signed to Albarika Records were known for blending indigenous African rhythms with Western jazz, funk, and soul influences. This made their sound distinctive and part of a broader movement across West Africa during the 60s and 70s, as music became a form of social and political expression.

A unique dip into the musical history of Ukraine with archival label Shukai, journeying back to the ‘90s, playing music from documentaries, sci-fi films, obscure cartoons and late-night music programming, curated by Dmytro Nikolaienko, Dmytro Prutkin and Alec Tsapenko.
From the host: "Step inside the sonic laboratory of Soviet Ukraine’s Kyivnaukfilm (Eng: Kyiv Science Film, active years 1941-1993) studio — a workshop where composers and sound designers crafted scores for documentary, science, education, and animation films. From eerie synth textures to inventive tape collages, these rare recordings (1967���1990) reveal a secret world of avant-garde music created under censorship yet brimming with experiments."

In an time of almost surveillance-like coverage of popular musicians, where artists are expected to be 100% available and known to their fans, there stands Burial. Antithetical to the social media era, William Bevan continues to be (mostly) a figure of mystery to his legion of fans.  
His signature sound transmutes a postmodern patchwork of samples – R&B vocals, video game soundtracks, field-recorded ephemera, and organic percussive elements – into a club melancholia that could only come from London. 
Photo: Georgina Cooke

DJ, artist and techno impresario, Word of Command welds fevered collage and high-octane techno into a hallucinatory dreamscape. Inspired by the Richard Brautigan poem, Midwife’s curated playlists explore the idea of “a cybernetic ecology;” acoustic and electronic sources reflect on our humanity as well as our animalistic nature in the context of a digital landscape.

nts2.d:
Ono is a Rīga based program that was originally started by Michael Holland and Paddy Shine in the Manchester studios but has since developed a nomadic exploratory based structure highlighting experimental rhythmic based music made mostly by outsiders.
From the host: "During weeks living off-grid with a colony of Atlantic Grey seals on Wales's Porthdinllaen Peninsula, an extensive archive of seal vocalizations was recorded. A responsive system was then developed allowing a performer to activate the recordings through gestures of pitch, timbre, rhythm and presence"

A two hour journey through the utterly singular sound of Broadcast. Fronted by the late Trish Keenan, the Birmingham band melded the novel soundscaping of library music and early electronic experiments with their own sensitive take on indie rock and pop. Keenan's untimely passing in 2011 effectively ended the project - but a limited amount of previously unheard material has surfaced in the following years. 
This mix features classics and obscurities from across Broadcast's discography, including exclusive tracks featured in upcoming compilation "Distant Call - Collected Demos 2000-2006".

nts2.d: Quiet purveyor of hip-hop and beyond, DJ Luh delivers a power hour of the best in hip-hop
NYC-based rapper & producer 454 presents Florida Man FM, coming through with an hour of under the radar US rap & club sounds every month.

nts2.n: El Sabor De La Ciudad w/ REGAL86 (R)

nts2.d: High Jazz* is an independent record label specializing in 70s & 80s soul, jazz & funk. ill Dubio takes the reigns for the monthly High Jazz* show rustling up an hour of choice selections on a Wednesday.

A two hour journey through the the intricate and epic electronic productions of William Orbit - featuring both his instrumental and experimental modes and his world-beating pop hits from All Saints, Madonna and more.
Selected and mixed by The Pilotwings.
"Shakshouka" is a dialectal arabic word used in North Africa to reflect an unexpected mixture of elements that wouldn’t naturally go together.

nts2.n: NTS Guide to: Giorno Poetry Systems (R)
nts2.d:
An hour of selections from Giorno Poetry Systems, the platform launched by NYC poet and performance artist John Giorno in 1972.
As well as engaging in live performances and performance art projects – including his famous "Dial-A-Poem" project – the record label wing of the platform released over 40 records featuring music and poetry from Laurie Anderson, Philip Glass, Meredith Monk, Allen Ginsberg, John Cage, and William S. Burroughs and more.  
Selected by ML of 150 Session.
Occult tapes plundered by two low level scavengers: the finest dungeon synth, medieval dark ambient & black metal.
Plug in and zone out.

Bi-monthly radio show by Tokyo-based DJ CENTERFOLD and sudden star. Since 2021, they have co-run the event series ether, which sprawls across the city’s landscape���blending electronic and live music, fashion, and art. Adults Play Radio is a sonic love letter to the people they’ve met and the places they find themselves in at each exact moment of delivery.
A monthly sonic journey from Lukid a.k.a Luke Blair. Expect a slow-burning, cinematic hour of hip-hop, epic psych, dub and synth soundscapes.
A lovers selection strictly for the adultz, courtesy of Norman Francis - the man behind lovers rock archive The Revival Master.

nts2.n: YOU'LL SOON KNOW W/ TIM PARKER
nts2.d: Tim Parker explores the experimental corners of beats and hip hop, weaving together jazz, underground cuts and global sounds - live once a month from London & Amsterdam.
A feel-good hour of deep grooves and soulful selections served up by Tone.

The Utrecht native from the Netherlands Carista, joins us to share music of all kinds going from Soul, Jazz, House and everything in between.
From the host: "MALLAURY turns it up a notch with a 60 minute guestmix for United Identities"

nts2.n: The Big Happy Show w/ FREDFADES
nts2.d: Mr. Big Happy is a graphic designer, artist, DJ, and music archivist from San Francisco, California. While he is an accomplished designer and artist, over the years he has always had a love for music and spends time searching for rare and obscure vinyl. Big Happy’s focus is the late 70s into the late 80s - modern soul, funk, boogie and disco.
Visual artist and founder and editor-in-chief of The Editorial Magazine Claire Milbrath joins us for an hour of ambient, classical, and italian library music.
One hour of music all across the board, naturally, with Jonny Mons.

nts2.n: NTS Guide to: Golden Age of Malian Music - 1970s
nts2.d: Selections from the "golden age" of Malian music from the late 1960s into the early 1980s. This was the era following Mali's independence in 1960s. President Modibo Keïta, eager to make music a key part of a newly independent national spirit, funded and encouraged the forming of musical communities and orchestras across the country. Many of these groups fused the generations-old griot story telling tradition with modern instrumentation.

Quarterly transmissions from the New York-based composer/producer Alexis Georgopoulos, exploring the unexpected connections between disparate genres & traditions, from Modern Classical to the club-adjacent, experimental to folk, ambient to spiritual jazz and beyond.

8ULENTINA is an interdisciplinary artist, music producer and DJ based in Brooklyn, New York. Known for his dynamic DJ sets and music composition synthesizing a range of genres, geographies and tempos. He co-founded CLUB CHAI (2016-2021), a record label and curatorial platform with Lara Sarkissian and currently runs a SILLAGE, a platform for contemporary sonic artifacts. His NTS show explores the sounds that inspire his production process, focusing on experimentation, percussive intensity, and non linear storytelling through sound.

Cold winds, empty street. Diamondstein, member of the band Melted Bodies, presents Night Shift - ambient techno, sinister industrial sounds, and moody experimental music for night time noir moments.
From the host: "H, aka HEVX, aka ANOTHERREUR is one of my longest running creative collaborators, having done the artwork for every episode of Night Shift. This show is dedicated to what is coming up on ten years of creative partnership and collaboration. Her photography brings Night Shift to life."

Dark Entries reissues archival and contemporary gems that prove to be crucial to our modern idea of bodily music that exists out of time. Electro, italo, cold wave, new beat, house, techno from a one-of-a-kind digger who visits dusty basements to rescue lost tapes.

nts2.n: Low Life Loves You w/ Bill Brewster (R)
nts2.d: Writer, historian and DJ Bill Brewster showcasing two hours of love, laughter, and music.
Horsegirl Sounds Radio Hour is a long running playlist curated by Penelope, Gigi, and Nora. Now they bring their monthly selections to the NTS airwaves.
Canadian record label, publisher, and distributors Séance Centre conjure timeless music from the past, present, and future.
Death Is Not The End digs up the oldest of the old, serving up your gospel, blues and folk with a crackle. Hear the dust.
The underground and experimental hero, composer, and guitar abstractionist Oren Ambarchi invites us to regular eclectic listening sessions, exploring the breadth of his extensive record collection.
Regular transmissions from the Japanese songwriter & composer Eiko Ishibashi, sharing favourites traversing folk, jazz, avant-garde composition and more.

Rose-tinted hallucinogenic sounds from Astral Vibes' Acid Memories. Illicit dealings in psychedelia of all persuasions. Sit back and enjoy…
From the host: "This mix is dedicated to vintage synths, cosmic library sounds, and rose-tinted frequencies.
A slow, sensual orbit through forgotten studio experiments and psychedelic grooves.
Floating somewhere between the body, memory, and deep space.
Sit back… and enjoy the journey.
this is Acid Memories !"

A unique dip into the musical history of Ukraine with archival label Shukai, journeying back to the ‘90s, playing music from documentaries, sci-fi films, obscure cartoons and late-night music programming, curated by Dmytro Nikolaienko, Dmytro Prutkin and Alec Tsapenko.
From the host: "A journey through Estonian film music (1967–1986) — from orchestral scores and jazz-infused soundtracks to early electronic experiments. Featuring works by Sven Grünberg, Raimo Kangro, Rein Rannap, and others, written for Estonian, Ukrainian, and Polish films, animation, and television."

80's Rampwalk delivers an hour of cold wave, minimal synth, industrial, post-punk and music made in Berlin basements.
From the host: "Avian society / perfumed bordello / a stranger under a lamp post in a trench coat and borsalino hat / "She drank from a boiling kettle" / Distributed Denial of Service Attack / jilted at the altar (a dead cert at the hounds) / a pale shape floats across an otherwise empty dance floor."
universal collage

nts2.n: FIFTH WORLD W/ IAN KIM JUDD
nts2.d:
A riff on Jon Hassell's Fourth World series. Where Hassell focused on creating ritual music of potential societies that didn't exist; Ian Kim Judd seeks to evoke the notion of potential utopian societies that could exist. In these times of global instability and uncertainty, Fifth World seeks to posit an alternative, with scenes from an optimistic future.
From the host: "Thaw tracks with IKJ. First solo mix of the year, marking the ninth (!?) year of the show. Thanks as always for listening."
Jen Monroe writes the album download blog Listen To This!, which focuses on Japanese and new age records: Listen to This

Chime into a world of noise and melancholia. Ranging from ambient to 70s Italian film scores. Perfect time for a long long walk.
From the host: "An exploration into mid-century Black operatic sopranos and contraltos."
LA native Bianca Lexis showcases a monthly, genre-blending hour, exploring the different facets of pop, rock, synth and house music. all in panoramic view, for you.

nts2.n: HEAVEN AND EARTH MAGIC W/ Barbarelle
nts2.d:
Exploring inner and outer worlds, cosmic and pastoral sounds, and the intersection between reality and dreams once a month with Jocelyn Romo's mix of international and private press folk, instrumental guitar, dream country, ambient, new age, kosmiche, psych and beyond
From the host: "Barbarelle is an artist, DJ, curator, and creative director from Buenos Aires. Her debut ambient album Celesta was released on Hidden Harmony in 2025. She founded ATLAS, a platform for deep listening through events, listening sessions, and a radio show on Dublab."
Soft-touch home listening selections live from LA every month with Luke Mele. Traversing ambient, jazz, guitar tones and more.

nts2.n: Beyond The Clouds w/ Nevena Jeremic
nts2.d: Slowed Down, sun kissed journeys through dub, synth, cosmic & balearic beats. Brought to you from LA each month.

Formed in the febrile community of NYC's 90s underground scene, Blonde Redhead – comprised of Kazu Makino and twin brothers Amedeo & Simone Pace – are the masters of indie rock atmospherics. From their early noise rock and no wave inspired sounds, they progressed into cinematic and lushly orchestrated leftfield pop, with Makino's ethereal vocals as the one constant. Compiled and mixed by Sophie Nicole Ellison.

Civilization is an evolving project about New York City by Lucas Mascatello and Richard Turley.
From the host: "Fine art, time travel, motivational speeches, Thai ingredients, restaurant reviews, fairytales, bad genetics, good experiences and the never-ending pursuit of excellence."
for you and me

nts2.n: Club Aerobics w/ Bianca Oblivion & AOBEATS

nts2.d: Awe is something you feel when confronted with forces beyond your control: nature, the cosmos, chaos, human error, hallucinations.
Anadol's selection for NTS.

Heavy vinyl records collector Dr. Kruger digs deep every other Sunday to bring soulful, funky and odd tunes from his crates straight to your home. Sometimes joined by a fellow crate digger/DJ of his choice, just tune in and let the doctor treat you.

nts2.n: NTS GUIDE TO: MODERN ETHIOPIAN ORTHODOX BEGENA (R)
nts2.d:
The begena is a sacred Ethiopian instrument: a ten string lyre, commonly known as the "Harp of David' and central to spiritual music. The deep, devotional tone is most often played unmetered, inspiring its listeners with thoughts of repentance.   
This episode of The NTS Guide To offers a glimpse into a droning, introspective strain of Ethiopian religious music, as you may hear on the streets of Addis Ababa during Lent.
Take a Kokonut Trip with Tim Koh, playing vintage pop, kaleidoscopic rock & folk, and whatever else he feels.
Japanese musician and video game composer June Chikuma, known for her work on the Bomberman series, plays selections from her wide and diverse musical tastes in regular transmissions.

nts2.n: On the Road w/ Adelina S - Gris-gris tour pt. 1
nts2.d:
Traveler and DIY documentarian Adelina S traverses the savannahs and deserts of the Sahel region, recording griots who travel from remote village to village performing to make a living. Through storytelling and field recordings, Adelina shares the winding paths - and the choices - that led to growing close to and be welcomed by this community.
From the host: "Radio show featuring field recordings taken during a road trip with a Fulani Griot band performing for spiritual healers around Fouta-Toro region in Senegal. Through sounds and storytelling the show wil travel to the Sahelian Savanna where centuries old music tradition blends with amplified guitars."
The co-founder of the legendary Saxon Sound System, Lloyd 'Musclehead' Saxon steps up for a regular session, playing ska, reggae, rocksteady and beyond.
Explorations in music from Ruth Tafébé a.k.a The Afrosoulmessenger.

nts2.n: THE GREAT AFRICAN DISCO W/ TEMI KOGBE (R)
nts2.d: Lagos based collector and DJ Temi Kogbe traces connections in African popular culture through vinyl.

Alessio Natalizia is Not Waving. Take the plunge with his monthly hour of fucked-up left techno, leftfield electronics, brain-twisting oddball pop and post-punk, dub offshoots and other diversions. 
ecstaticrecordings.com
Oakland songwriter Maria BC selects an hour of tracks serving as inspiration for their new LP “Marathon”, featuring lo-fi pop, folk, ambient and more.

Malibu is a French electronic musician whose work sails between ambient and ethereal music.
Forever inspired by soft reverbed vocals and melodious chord progressions, Malibu��s music is an immersive nostalgic journey in a sea of synthetic strings and choirs.

Where the wall meets the floor, Temporal Cove is a body of sound exploring the ethereal and ephemeral. Smilegoth dives into shoegaze, post-rock, house and more to articulate a flow of reverberations that can be felt on every surface.
From the host: "smilegoth invites dear friends and Americana band Dutch Interior onto Temporal Cove for a cutty country and folk mix."
Sematary (fka Grave Man), a key figure in cult new wave drill and horrorcore collective Haunted Mound presents his monthly NTS Show.

Self proclaimed “Underground and Black”, Detroit native Ash Lauryn leads her musical charge with a candid love for the historical roots of dance music, and the power of reclamation in modern day America.
Cheeky slacker selections from everyone's favourite - Homeshake. Kick back.

nts2.n: NTS Guide to: Vietnamese Cloud Rap Vol. II (R)
nts2.d: A deep dive into scenes, record labels and genres from around the world.

The Bones-led independent hip hop collective and label TeamSESH take the NTS airwaves for a monthly one hour transmission live from our LA studio.
From the host: "As it has become somewhat of a tradition and a rite of my own for the  March episode of our TeamSesh NTS show: Here is my annual Airtaster Special, with its usual focus on all things ambient, calm, drone-y and drum-less. - HNRK"
Label head Michael Lundy explores Rap Vacation’s experimental influences, with guests and exclusive cuts from the thriving LA beat scene.
Prestonian singer songwriter Rainy Miller presents the progress check, a bi-monthly show playing an hour of archived experiments and influences.

nts2.n: THEY ARE GUTTING A BODY OF WATER (R)
nts2.d: Philadelphia cult property and experimental shoegazers They Are Gutting A Body Of Water bring their hazy sound to NTS once a month.

Welcome to LXXXVIII – the ninth Actress album to be created by Darren Cunningham and the very first presentation of Actress’ voyage into luxury sonics.
A lifetime in the making, LXXXVIII is the culmination of 25 years’ honing mind-shorting, soul-igniting audio
infusions for dance floors, rave dens, festivals, and concert hall, and the inaugural vessel for Actress’ cruise into novel strata of artistic influence: the executive suites, yacht decks and private jets of our planet’s ultimate power players.
100% Yegorka material, selected by label founder Why Be.

nts2.n: Under Sleepy Moon w/ Yawning Portal (R)
nts2.d: Zzzzzzzzzzzz… Shhhh… There's a baby sleeping in the house!

nts2.n: VIBRATIONS FOR A BETTER WORLD W/ AOKI TAKAMASA (R)
nts2.d: Sticky Groove for Every Direction.
A deeper look into the back catalogs of various artists.

Get in your feelings with Brussels party starters HE4RTBROKEN - the beloved Belgian collective that aren't scared of a few tears on the dance floor. Resident djs Liyo, Steff, Munix and ssaliva explore the full range of cathartic music listening together with some kindred-spirited emotive guests.
Spanish alt pop songwriter Lorea selects a deep hour of influences, including contemporary chamber compositions, Spanish folk, as well as unreleased original music.
Danish label Posh Isolation join us for an hour a month.
Leaf through songwriter Calvin LeCompte��s monthly selection via The Uline Catalog…

nts2.n: CREEP ZONE W/ JAMES PANTS & MARC SCHALLER
nts2.d: Stones Throw's James Pants joins forces with Marc Schaller for an hour-long dive into dreamy electronics, wired sounds and experimentation.

nts2.n: world of pain w/ dagmar zuniga (R)
nts2.d: the meek shall inherit the girth
James McNew of Hoboken indie rock royalty Yo La Tengo shares an hour of picks from his collection each month..
Leftfield Pop, Bedroom Z-sides and Music Box Covers…

This month’s journey winds upward through the deserts of North Africa, across Sudan and Palestine, and northward still into Lebanon. Along the way, I’ve gathered sounds from both Eastern and Western Europe, with echoes reaching as far as Japan, Jamaica and Brazil. The pace runs a little faster here, the BPM is bit higher in this one to carry me through these final weeks of summer, sweet and fleeting, filled with moonlit gatherings and the first whispers of cooler winds.

nts2.n: AXCESS AMNESIA W/ JENNY SAYAKA NONO
nts2.d: Filmmaker Jenny Sayaka Nono brings her manic VHS hued style to the audio airwaves, with steely coldwave, industrial, synth pop & more.

Yesterday's News: a thorough re-cap of the finest in punk, noise, garage and other ratty guitar sounds from the past few decades.
From the host: "100% Japanese Punk/Hardcore/Garage Rock"

nts2.n: Trapdoor w/ Neil Krug - Window Pane Jane
nts2.d: Neil Krug is an American visual artist, director, and photographer based in Los Angeles. Tune in for a sonic lysergic tale of love, heartbreak, and redemption.

nts2.n: BORN FOR BURNING W/ PAINKILLER KIM
nts2.d:
Devoted to the metal underground - black, thrash, heavy, death and speed
From the host: "Darkness prevails this Friday the 13th"

nts2.n: Hit Factory W/ DJ Wawa - City Pop Special (R)
nts2.d: From the host: "Post-punk in Peru emerged mainly within the framework of Rock Subterráneo, a movement that took shape in the mid-1980s on the margins of official circuits. Bands inspired by post-punk currents circulating globally forged, from their own social realities, a language that combined introspection, experimentation, and social critique. More than a mood, Peruvian post-punk expressed a collective sensibility shaped by the violence and precariousness that afflicted the country, turning that unrest into a form of creation and affirmation within the local underground."
Los Angeles native & synth pioneer Benedek blends ambient, boogie, funk & more, once a month from our LA studio.

"I could compare my music to white light which contains all colours. Only a prism can divide the colours and make them appear; this prism could be the spirit of the listener." - Arvo Pärt
A figurehead in European minimalism, Arvo Pärt's compositions are slow and meditative, owing to the composer's own mystical experiences with chant music following a conversion to Orthodox Christianity. Now one of the world's most widely performed classical composers, immerse yourself in the output of Estonia's most celebrated musician in a three-hour In Focus special.
An pensive hour of music in translation from Bradfordian's finest export aka UNDERTHEJAGUAR. Spanning across time, geographic, and lexicon borders, this mix takes listeners on a linguistic adventure.
The revered Japanese noise artist stops by for a regular NTS slot, playing some surprising selections from his diverse & deep record collection.
A mix of love songs spanning desert reggae, country, soul, rock n' roll & more from various Australian indigenous artists. This mix includes english & a handful of other indigenous languages.
Mikey Young of Total Control and Eddie Current Suppression Ring plays a selection of 60's and 70's soft rock�������
Ethiopian pop & jazz from Mulatu Astatke, Alemayehu Eshete, Mahmoud Ahmed & more.

Francesco Fusaro, the man behind MFZ Records and classical music recording series 19'40'', brings a considered and varied selection of baroque, classical and contemporary music for a full two hours each month.
From the host: "A two-hour tribute to music pioneer Éliane Radigue, who passed away in Paris on 23 February 2026 at the age of 94. The programme traces her singular path from early tape and feedback pieces to the immersive electronic works of the ARP 2500 years, and nd beyond to the later acoustic cycles."

nts2.n: THE OPERA SHOW w/ Daniel Oduntan, Seyi Adelekun & Raheel Khan
nts2.d:
Hannah Catherine Jones splits things open for the Opera Show: an exploration of the best in opera old and new, as well as an unearthing of the operatic in other records from soul, jazz or further afield.
From the host: "This month I'm excited to welcome guest hosts Daniel Oduntan, Seyi Adelekun & Raheel Khan - all amazing interdisciplinary artist-musicians who are currently preparing for live events as part of Somerset House's experimental sound and music series Assembly on March 26-28th (see links for more info)."

“I start associating your voice with ….”, for a while ML has been part of the NTS community, jingling around, airing his monthly 150 Session out of Berlin’s North, where all seasons give reasons. For clarification, creation, inspiration. One hour of poetic and musical explorations, questions, and curiosities. Circling around daring electronics, ethno-sonics, jazz-constructivism, minimalism, open structured digital dopamine, risky dub excitement and other sounds, that still need to be written. No country, no flag – outernational without a cause!
From the host: "Today 150 Session drifts into a quieter space: Ambient currents. Slow drifts. Sounds for contemplation. Music to open a small pocket of calm for our troubled times. Let the frequencies soften the noise."

Lucia Kagramanyan digs through the archives of the Republic Radio of Armenia, presenting lost compositions, featuring everything from classical & opera, to folk & pop in a special hour long show.
From the host: "Lucia Kagramanyan takes on a one hour journey across Armenian dance, it's tradition, origins, spirituality, types and practice."

for this one I'm making a 'So Low' show; this is a club night (and short lived label/ sub label of Optimo Music) which has ran for 11 years, music policy is > Dark Waves / Cold Waves / Minimal Synthesis / Maximal Industrial / Forgotten Cumbernauld Goth Bands and Belgian Apocalyptic Christmas Beats past, present and future +++  
thinking of our friend, inspiration and collaborator at this time

nts2.n: RACHEL GRACE ALMEIDA: LATIN EBM & WAVE SPECIAL
nts2.d:
Venezuelan writer Rachel Grace Almeida spends an hour dipping into the expansive universe of Latin music every month.
From the host: "Tropigoths! Bien darks! This is a deep dive into the freakzone of Latin EBM & wave of all forms, connecting Belgium to Buenos Aires, Germany to Guadalajara."

LABOUR is the duo of Colin Hacklander and Farahnaz Hatam. Tune in each month for their hour-long show featuring rotating guests and friends from Dakar, Senegal. Expect a mixture of progressive electronic music with heavy sabar drum music, classic & current Mbalax, and timeless Sufi praises.
From the host: "This month’s show features the great Senegalese singer and bandleader, Momo Dieng, a popular mbalax artist on the Prince Arts label, featuring tracks spanning the last 10 years such as Saf Na Ba Nopi and Doom Gorel."

nts2.n: Dance Music Show w/ Bell Towers - Italo Disco Special (R)
nts2.d: Prolific producer + DJ from Melbourne, Australia - Bell Towers presents his Dance Music Show.
High speed electronics and dance soundtracks from the Chicago footwork auteur.
Special guest shows from around the world.

nts2.n: Halloween on NTS: Parallel Lines - Death Rock (R)
nts2.d:
The music… it's coming from inside the house… 
NTS gets spooky with this year's Halloween programming, featuring video game scores, road horror soundtracks. & other frights.
Renegade Soundwaves by Pandora's Jukebox: sonic assemblages with hypnotic noir-dreamscapes and a multi-textured take on electronic music.
Todmorden's finest, Kim Lana joins us in the Manchester studio once a month for 2 hours of bumping and pumping golden era house & techno.

Cultural virtuoso and creative luminary SHABAZZ, aka DJ Auntie Spice, illuminates obscure nostalgic treasures, crafting a rich, diverse auditory experience of soul, funk, jazz and beyond with her sound art initiative, Auntie·ism.
Musical peregrinations…

Diving into the catalogues of Gene and Dean Ween.
Mix by Brian Coney.
Bi-monthly emissions from Conrad Standish and Sam Karmel, aka CS + Kreme - keeping it loose, deep and free.

Paulina and Janek Ufnal from Warsaw's Mondoj label share a guest mix guided by enigmatic storytelling, lightheartedness, and play. Sounds from Mondoj associates Olli Aarni, Patrick Shiroishi & Piotr Kurek, and Mike Cooper & Jason Kolàr interweave with recent finds and old favourites.

nts2.n: NTS Guide to: Italian Library, Soundtracks and OSTS (R)
nts2.d: Living la dolce vita with two hours of stylish '60s and '70s Italian Library, film scores and OSTs.

In Iceland, Thomas Stankiewicz contemplates the constant flux of the elements. A composer & musician in his own right, he selectively weaves in between soundtracks, classical music, and evocative ambient sounds, symphonically unveiling untold stories from past and future…
@tommmasi
Visual artist and founder and editor-in-chief of The Editorial Magazine Claire Milbrath joins us for an hour of ambient, classical, and italian library music.

nts2.n: Stereolab w/ Tim Gane - ITALIAN SPECIAL (R)
nts2.d: Stereolab co-founder Tim Gane pulls together a special episode of exclusively Italian library music from film soundtracks.

nts2.n: NTS x Strata-East Records Special (Mixed By Jean-Claude) (R)
nts2.d: Jean-Claude searches through his extensive mountain of records to bring out some of that new new, and some new old as well. It always bangs.

nts2.n: Peking Spring presents: Danny Bushes 'Implicated' Mixtape (R)
nts2.d: ‘Implicated’ moves in/with a conversation between John Akomfrah, Tina Campt, Saidiya Hartman and Ekow Eshun - discussing BAFC’s Handsworth Songs, statues and monuments, the inseparability of racism and capitalism, burning police stations and implication.
Aylu shares an hour of Chilean protest music, and sounds of solidarity, marking 50 years since the country's violent military coup, which ousted democratic leader Salvador Allende.

Two hours of music from the lauded German jazz imprint Enja Records, responsible for releasing the cutting edge of European, Japanese and US jazz, including artists like Mal Waldon, Freddie Hubbard, Yōsuke Yamashita, Alexander von Schlippenbach and many more.
Music from absurdist Swiss electronic pioneers fills this month's In Focus slot. Selected and mixed by Alex Egan.
Música sentimental for all the sad girls and sad boys.

nts2.n: NTS Guide to: Women of 60s & 70s Folk (R)
nts2.d: Anne Briggs, Sibylle Bayer, Karen Dalton and more shepherds of the folk revival in this hour long guide to.

High Jazz* is an independent record label specializing in 70s & 80s soul, jazz & funk. ill Dubio takes the reigns for the monthly High Jazz* show rustling up an hour of choice selections on a Wednesday.
The stage is set for a spiritual Spring sojourn in the foothills. We listen to devotional joy on cassettes from the '80s and '90s, Germany, India, and California.

nts2.n: The Head Zone w/ Ripley Johnson (R)
nts2.d: Step into The Head Zone - one hour of musical selections from the mind of Moon Duo's Ripley Johnson.
Sounds and recordings from resident artists at the Wonder Cabinet space in Cremisan Valley, Bethlehem.

Broken club, dubbed out sounds, experimental electronics and atonal drifts: Gio’s monthly broadcasts are high contrast assemblages that move at a slow pace.
From the host: "Taking it slow: just twelve really long tunes in this episode, long blends and all. A pretty chilled vibe this Monday, enough stimuli already…"

nts2.n: AO RADIO PRESENTS NIGERIAN UNDERGROUND W/ RACHEL, ROBERTO, ONA & EBUBE
nts2.d:
Paxslim's Africa Only Radio explores the Nigerian underground scene. The first episode features Rachel, Roberto, Ona and Ebube, each selecting tracks by emerging artists from across the scene. 
Rachel runs The Process Africa documenting young African creatives, based in Lagos. Roberto runs Soundgasm, a Toronto-based platform covering music from Nigeria and the diaspora. Ona is an artist manager and A&R, whilst Ebube is a video creator and commentator. Mixed by Haram, Paxslim's longtime producer and collaborator.

nts2.n: ENERGY RESERVE SYSTEMS W/ KITTY CA$H (R)
nts2.d:
Live from Ibiza.
The latest installment of Energy Reserve Systems is a sonic postcard from Ibiza — a raw, sun-drenched mix capturing the spirit of a girls trip through deep house, underground edits, and soulful rhythms.
Woven with real voice notes recorded on Kitty’s TP-7 field recorder, this set lives at the intersection of movement and memory. It’s part dancefloor transmission, part emotional archive — designed to recharge the spirit and keep the energy in motion.

nts2.n: HIT A LICK RADIO w/ VVS.JAH
nts2.d: Chester, PA’s DJ FINNAHITALICK (Blackwealth in the club) is a seasoned sound archivist of Underground Hip-hop. Starting Hit A Lick Radio in 2016, DJ FINNAHITALICK explores rap’s extensive subgenres and regional epicenters, ranging from DMV crank and Atlanta Plugg to Milwaukee low-end, Michigan punch-rap and beyond. His fast-paced mixes encapsulate Underground Hip-hop’s diverse landscape, and are not for the weak-hearted.
Native Sound System join us once a month, breaking the latest sounds from Lagos
Head of Grimy Edits, Zernell Gillie's style can range from the heart of disco to the soul of Chicago house - he heads up a 2 hour slot once a month from our LA studio.

nts2.n: Kristin Kontrol Presents: Out of Vogue
nts2.d:
Former frontwoman of rock outfit Dum Dum Girls eschews the pseudonym with her new solo project, Kristin Kontrol. Newly relocated to LA, Kristin delivers a monthly mix of expansive and hypnotic sounds: one part Durutti Column to two parts Alice Coltrane.
From the host: "Tune in to hear the new Daughn Gibson song, "Sacred Life.""

Take a moment and sit in the Astral Garden with harpist and composer Nailah Hunter, summoning healing frequencies you can float off to. 
Image: Claudia Gödke

Get the party started with DJ Wawa & Clay Wilson's Hit Factory Records.

Imagine Soundtracks depicts the musical emotions that animations, literature, manga, movies, or video games inspire. This series, curated by Paris-based poet and DJ Clarisse Prévost, aka redkoffee, is presented with interpreted dialogues from the explored selection.

Katsuhiro Otomo is a defining figure in modern Japanese animation and manga. Best known as the creator of Akira, he helped popularise the cyberpunk aesthetic and influence a global wave across film, science fiction and music. This episode of Otaku traces the sonic worlds around Otomo’s films, with selections from the Akira score alongside music from Robot Carnival and Memories: atmospheric synths, ritualistic chants and an uncanny near-future soundscape.

He blessed us with an all Japanese jazz-funk mix!"

Kita is a resident DJ and co curator of LA’s premiere black and queer underground party called Hood Rave. She has toured both nationally and internationally.

nts2.d:
"The Last Playlist" explores mortality via songs people want at their funerals. Guests share stories ranging from hilarious to heartbreaking, discussing their musical choices while exploring death, grief, and what makes life worth living. Each episode weaves together music and conversation to celebrate life's complexities and honor its inevitable end.
From the host: "In this special episode, we sit down with Maustetytöt to explore the intersection of Finnish identity and art. From the complexities of working as sisters to the songs they want played at their funerals."

Ritmo Santanero is an all-vinyl cumbia DJ Crew based out of Santa Ana, CA consisting of DJ’s Rigo Suave, Gary Ventura, LX Ramirez, and Kevin Hernandez. The revered cumbia connoisseurs locally dubbed “Los Reyes Cumbieros” are each seasoned vinyl collectors in their own right. Their highly-curated selection ranges from porros, gaitas, rebajadas, to rare tracks and beloved classics - all on 45s.
Ritmo Santanero stands out as an artistic contender in the Southern California sonidero scene as the trio expands out of Santa Ana and into Long Beach, and Los Angeles - bringing the best all-vinyl cumbia night to a town near you. Spinning a mix of cumbia genres from of all corners of the world, Ritmo Santanero is reviving the once-dormant sonidero culture of the 90’s, yet deviating from the announcement-heavy tradition by letting the music do the talking.

nts2.n: Funk Freaks Radio w/ DJ Zee (R)

nts2.d:
On today's Radio Discostan we present an hour of music from Bangkok's Arab-Malay scene assembled by Peter Doolan of Monrakplengthai. In these songs, we hear Thai pop/rock sounds layered among rhythms, melodies and textures from throughout the Muslim world. Influences from Malaysia, Indonesia, Egypt, Lebanon, and India are all discernable in the mix.
Thai Arab-Malay acts had their heyday in the '70s and '80s entertaining cosmopolitan visitors from the ummah on "Soi Arab" (Sukhumvit Lane 3/1), and popular nightlife bands of the era have remained a staple at mosque fairs and other cultural events around the central provinces ever since.
The selections in this set are primarily digitized from cassettes that were for sale at Phrakhanong's Yanavy Tape & Video or from various second-hand vendors around the capital.Peter Doolan covers the world of Thai music on cassette at monrakplengthai.blogspot.com.

nts2.n: Post Punk Britain: Primitive Painters - Jangle Pop & Indie Rock from the UK Post Punk Era (R)
nts2.d: Featuring melodic post punk bands from the "C86" wave - named after the famous NME Magazine compilation of the same name. The title of the compilation became shorthand for the sound, which would become the blueprint for contemporary "indie" as a genre: guitar-based music characterised by jangly tones and power pop song structures, taking healthy inspiration from sunny 1960s pop music.

A special physical tape I made in 2019, entitled 'Bordi az Yadam' and released on Bunny Jr tapes. It is dedicated to Iran and is a soundscape of childhood memories; channeled through an oral abstraction of living in America while Iranian programs played via satellite, on bootlegged VHS tapes, and music from my father's massive tape collection filled the house. Songs, poems and excerpts from Iranian films pre-revolution.

Heading to wartime Sài Gòn, specifically the venue Quán Văn, where two young performers – Trịnh Công Sơn, singer-song writer, and Khánh Ly, singer, would perform regularly for an intimate audience in the midst of a devastating conflict. 
One of the few venues the artist could perform at, due to government's persecution, the duo performed folk music, evoking themes of love, loss, filial piety, the spiritual world, homeland, and anti-war sentiment.

Based in Cotonou, Benin, which at the time was referred to as Dahomey, Albarika Records was founded by the French-born, Benin-based producer and entrepreneur, Emmanuel "Manu" Dibango, who had a significant influence on the local music scene. The label was instrumental in releasing records from some of the region's most prominent musicians during the golden age of African music.
Many of the artists signed to Albarika Records were known for blending indigenous African rhythms with Western jazz, funk, and soul influences. This made their sound distinctive and part of a broader movement across West Africa during the 60s and 70s, as music became a form of social and political expression.

A unique dip into the musical history of Ukraine with archival label Shukai, journeying back to the ‘90s, playing music from documentaries, sci-fi films, obscure cartoons and late-night music programming, curated by Dmytro Nikolaienko, Dmytro Prutkin and Alec Tsapenko.
From the host: "Step inside the sonic laboratory of Soviet Ukraine’s Kyivnaukfilm (Eng: Kyiv Science Film, active years 1941-1993) studio — a workshop where composers and sound designers crafted scores for documentary, science, education, and animation films. From eerie synth textures to inventive tape collages, these rare recordings (1967–1990) reveal a secret world of avant-garde music created under censorship yet brimming with experiments."

Juana Molina is one of South America's most remarkable and exploratory popular musicians. For over three decades, the Argentine-born artist has approached songwriting and music production with a childlike curiosity and sage open-mindednes, garnering a beloved following in Argentina and beyond. 
Her music, which often compared to Björk, is a patchwork of folk, ambient, electronic and indie rock, and avant-garde in a way that is truly her own. Listening to Molina's music, which is often suffused with humour and oddity, it perhaps comes as no surprise that she first rose to prominence as a comedian, featuring on Artgentinian sketch comedy shows in the early 1990s.

DJ: “… I don’t know, I didn’t… I didn’t think I’d be the one holding the air, I never spoke live before the event consumed the many. I’ve no news for the desperate survivors and… and I can’t… well, I can hope that this next song might help us survive a little longer…"
From the host: "DJ: “On the theme of love, caller on line 8. Tell us a small joy.”

nts2.n: EDGE OF THE FOREST: Fish String Theory
nts2.d:
Antoine Bertin weaves together field recordings and sonifications of data he collects around the world. Wandering in its own meditative way on the edge of science, technology and sound, the radio show explores where forests begin and when they end. From listening to the DNA of plants in Japan, to the seismic data of the Stromboli volcano or the movements of rivers in Alaska, The Edge of the Forest seeks to share sensations and ideas that inform our understanding of the natural world, humans included.
From the host: "Edge of the Forest takes you across the horizon, to listen with the forests, seas and caves of Aotearoa. Follow the guidance of resonating strings, connect to the origin of voices, read the future in the structure of harmonics, translate yourself into otherness."

His cheek was cold when I kissed him goodbye. He had the skin of an angel and I could feel his warm heart beating underneath. I don't know the difference between a goodbye and a farewell so I didn't say anything. Once he was gone I felt his light caress me and I started to cry. I knew at that moment that he was inside me forever.

nts2.n: Sounds on Screen: Paul Thomas Anderson
nts2.d: A trip through the sonic worlds of Paul Thomas Anderson – one of the greatest living American film directors. The breadth of humanity Anderson depicts spans 19th Century oil prospectors, cult leaders, pornstars, and a miscellany of lost and broken souls – all of their stories suffused with the subtly supernatural and the gravity of fate.

nts2.j: A SONG IS A NAKED PERSON

nts2.d:
Born in Bristol, England, Robert Wyatt joined the Soft Machine during the exciting, slightly post-psychedelic Canterbury Scene of the mid-'60s that produced bands like Gong and Pink Floyd. Swerving the typical theatrical excess of progressive rock, the Soft Machine preferred a more spartan rock setup, experimenting in jazz-esque improvisation. Following a traumatic accident, falling out of a window, Wyatt became wheelchair-bound. His solo work became contemplative, trance-like & lush. Musically active until very recently, Wyatt was unrestricted by the machinations of the music industry, creating and releasing music only when he felt like it.
This In Focus is a deep dive into the meditative & emotionally potent music of Robert Wyatt, compiled by Knee Deep's Mattis.

nts2.n: Estação Primeira de Mangueira w/ Montif Clare

Nigerian musician King Sunny Ade enjoys royal status from two monarchies – firstly due to his birth into Yoruba royalty in Southwestern Nigeria, and secondly, from his eventual rise to prominence as the King of Juju, the Nigerian popular sound that fused Christian choral performance with traditional Yoruba vocals and rhythms, as well as incorporating Western rock and pop influence. 
With albums like 1977's Sound Vibration and 1979's The Royal Sound, Ade ushered his distinctive juju sound onto the global stage, also helping popularise African music around the world more broadly.

contemporary french + international rap presented by Stephane.
