But the sun will always rise again.

nts2.n: A Kalimantan Listening Session w/ neko.ea
nts2.d:
This one-hour listening session is an exploration of the Austronesian Expansion, a migration that began over 4,000 years ago from the indigenous tribes of Taiwan, eventually rooting itself in the heart of Borneo. In West Kalimantan, I grew up surrounded by the sonic remnants of this history, sounds that serve as a living map of our journey across the seas. While often under-researched or overshadowed by the royal court traditions of neighboring islands, the music of the Dayak/ Kenyah people contains the raw, "proto-Austronesian" frequencies that have survived for millennia within the rainforest canopy.
This session highlights the instrument’s specific pentatonic scales and meditative drone strings, elements that were historically used in rituals to maintain the cosmic balance between the human and spirit realms. A contemporary experimental interpretation is also included, featuring a digitally refracted field recording that sits in dialogue with these traditions.

In the post-internet world, there is accessibility in creation ; anyone with a laptop and internet access can make and distribute music. In this mix, we will explore how niche online identities paired with distortion and saturated production have infiltrated sound from UK rap to Korean softrock.

1986 wow it's forty years ago. It's always been my favourite year for music, as much as I love all of that decade. It was after disco, hiphop was growing and it was a few years before house and dance music caught on. What should we make of this chaotic era? It was when punk, post-punk, synths and drum machines were all in the mix for DIY songwriters and tapes were passed around. So it's time for an '86 in '26 mix – going deep into minimal wave and coldwave.

As the biggest record label in former Yugoslavia, Jugoton bridged the country and the Western world. This mix showcases Yugoslav artistry released by Jugoton during the 1970s and 1980s, blending sounds from new wave electro, funk, reggae, disco, and so much more.

During 'Wave Form', Aurélien Arbet and Pierre Rousseau explore an interpolation of various recordings, often at the margins of traditional formats and conventional styles of music. During an hour, a long oscillation is formed out of a selection of interviews, spoken word, poetry, music for dance or art, marginal pop attempts and various field recordings.
Aurélien Arbet is an art director, co-founder of the clothing brand and publishing house Études. Pierre Rousseau is a composer, producer and sound designer, crafting sounds for records, films, and art projects.

nts2.n: Despertar de un ángel argentino W/ Fabián Sarinelli

This is a mix of obscure indietronica/folktronica released between 1999 and 2007, inspired by hyper-contemporary releases from artists like Worldpeace DMT, Ideasforconversations, Chanel Beads, Cicada, Josephine's Next Million Miles, Ear, b7lanket, Her story and Horse Vision. 
About a month after I first started exploring/obsessing over the above bands, I came across the Internet Archive’s Netlabel Collection, a library of independently released mp3’s from the early to mid 00’s. I was immediately struck by the similarities I heard between some of these releases found on the Netlabel archive, and the newly coined genres of "Cloud Rock" and "Laptop Twee" (created by Eli Enis and JC of the band friends& respectively), both of which focus on a chopped, glitched, electroacoustic, and most importantly digital sound.
Those odd moments where vintage and contemporary blur together always feel very special to me, so I spent the next few months chasing that high, digging between the couch cushions of the world wide web, combining my findings from the Netlabel archive with tracks pulled from extensive searches on Discogs, Youtube and Soulseek, until I compiled a satisfactory list of extremely obscure folktronica/indietronica/glitch pop that managed fit in line with the contemporary music I’ve fallen in love with. I hope it sounds ok.

nts2.n: Las Mujeres De Cumbia w/ DJ Nandez

nts2.d: While traveling long treacherous and beautiful Himalayan mountain paths in the Kingdom of Bhutan, you need two things: the best driver in all the land and the tunes to get you in the zone. In this Bhutan special, we dive deep into the USB stick of one of the best driver’s in Bhutan - Tashi Dorji. While studying at Royal Thimphu College in the Summer of 2019, Tashi granted us safe passage on all of our excursions and in the end gifted me a copy of his USB tunes as a farewell. Please enjoy the ride!

A selection of music from the past and present connecting the Black and African diaspora through the specialist subject of Afrofuturism.
Taking inspiration from filmmakers, musicians and science fiction, Afrofuturism reimagines history, technology and identity through speculative possibility.
'Future Ancestry' is a constellation of experimental jazz, hyper-kinetic Singeli, dusty deep roots and digital acceleration, framing music as technology — a catalyst for imagination and transcendence toward a vision of tomorrow.

nts2.n: SEYYAN AMONG OTHERS W/ MINA BARUT
nts2.d: Inspired by the title of Seyyan Hanım ve Diğerleri by Toktamış Ateş, this mix centres Seyyan Oskay, the first woman to sing tango in Turkey. Rising in 1930s Istanbul, she recorded iconic works before leaving music at its peak to devote her life to her family. The selection places her alongside other women artists, tracing memory, absence and devotion.

Take a dive into the quiet but extremely rich Danish jazz scene. From collaborations with giants to intimate searches for new improvised sounds, creating dialogue between eras, with a particularly Danish deep care and focus on listening and leaving room for each other.

nts2.n: YU Special Education W/ DJ Bringiton
nts2.d:
We present you with a mix that brings together the best of sounds from across former Yugoslavia.
Rather than looking back nostalgically, this piece is a living conversation with tracks speaking to each other across cities, decades and styles.
Groove support provided by Milanche and Suhi.
Exploring the exciting period when the early 80's post-punk scene met dub and funk music.

Lost in the fields, found beneath the sky. This is a secret, sacred conversation. Industrial sounds meet angels in the wind, deconstruction flirts with the ethereal nature of ambient. Non-club club music, hardcore for the girls: this mixtape is a soundtrack to the inevitably emerging spring.
From Paris, an hour's interstellar journey among aliens and the fantaisies they inspire.

nts2.n: Wire & Solo Mix w/ Fabrizio Clemenza
nts2.d: A selection of songs exploring a lesser known side of the seminal british post-punk band Wire, including songs taken from singles, EPs, demos and solo/side projects of the band members Colin Newman, Graham Lewis and Bruce Gilbert. The songs included in this mix are from the period 1977-1991.

nts2.n: MADRID SIERRA & PERIFERIA RAP W/ TOMI FARIÑA
nts2.d: Light rain on commuter rail, frank rhymes on greyscale, tobacco notes. Fresh underground joints and certified barrio classics from Madrid's Sierra and overall periphery.

John Peel once said Leeds’ LS6 postcode had more bands than anywhere in the UK. This mixtape attempts to document some of the groups active in the early-2000s heyday of the Leeds DIY scene: self-organised gigs in sweaty pub rooms, social clubs, squats and house parties, with the Cops & Robbers listings zine as oracle of events. Compiled by Matthew Benn & Steven Nuttall.

Listening backward through my maternal lineage, from pre-Revolution wax recordings to corridos shaped by migration after the Mexican Revolution, then songs and artists my relatives loved, and finally the music my mother passed down. Some tracks are remembered firsthand, others imagined, piecing together what was lost through assimilation.

From the Mississippi River blues of Memphis, to the bright lights of Nashville honky tonkin’ and the Grand Ole Opry, to the Smoky Mountain folk and bluegrass – Tennessee isn't just a place, but an idea, a collective dream that is comprised of and has influenced the most uniquely American genres.

Flip phones flipping, neon lights buzzing and compact discs spinning. The era of Japanese hip-hop & R&B from the late 90s to mid 2000s is a time that holds near and dear to my heart. So here is a mix that captures a piece of that era and hopefully transports you back into a different time.
Rarities, bootlegs, and deep cuts from the enigmatic Scottish duo Boards of Canada.
A collage of spiritual sound–sacred pop, folks hymns, maritime storytelling, and gentle instrumentals for quiet Sunday's.

I firtsly immersed myself in ambient music, with works that include field recordings with wind sounds, distant echoes, subtle movements. Then I discovered the artists of the movement called "Kankyō Ongaku", who have been created delicate artificial environments using electronic instruments.
I wanted to gather artists who share this spirit and reinterpret it today, through younger labels such as Mizuha 罔象 or the artist Lamina.
To me, they push the boundaries of an imagined utopia between the natural and the artificial — a place where dreamy forests could be felt with synthesizers.
(This mix is an extract from a project combining a CD and a fanzine)

nts2.n: DARK SAMPLADELIA W/ DJ INTERIOR SEMIOTICS
nts2.d: the heavier and darker side of sampladelic music. trip-hop and jungle remixes of alt rock. gothic spoken word. pre-9/11 alternative culture. if shibuya-kei is austin powers, this mix is dr. evil. this is that shit they were smoking in the 90s that made them do the 1999 WTO protests.

Immerse yourself in a nocturnal tour through the post-Franco underground of Madrid.
Experience the sound of a city that had been holding its breath for forty years finally exhaling all at once, turning its repression into art, its anxiety into dance floors, and its freedom into something gloriously strange.
The best of the Balkans — spanning ’60s crooners, ’70s jazz-funk & rock, disco, ’80s synth-pop, and psych-rock.

nts2.n: BENEATH THE VEIL W/ MIKEY G

nts2.d: A left-of-the-dial drive through Connecticut’s underground sound.

nts2.n: BUZZY BRATTY KEYBOARD w/ NO PRESSURE SELECTIONS
nts2.d: Music from 2002 to 2008 featuring a certain lo-fi synth sound. Serves as a parallel history of the "indie sleaze" moment. Casiotone features prominently, while other melodies honk louder and bite harder. Styles from the jocular, insouciant, and twee to ponderous, moody, and strange.

nts2.n: Miami-Cuban Ex-Pat Funk, Rock, & Soul w/ DJ Blacksmith

nts2.d: Perceptual Calibration brings together Japanese environmental and related music from the 1980s to today. This mix gently lowers the sensory gain of city life, focusing on intimacy, artificial nature, and small shifts in sound and space.

Orphnoise traces Gamelan as a system of power, time, and cosmology. Centered on José Maceda’s radical deconstruction of court music, the mix follows Gamelan’s influence from ritual sound through Debussy and Messiaen to minimal process, rhythmic abstraction, and contemporary experimental practice.

nts2.n: Muddy Bless w/ Viki Steiri & Dario Papavassiliou

nts2.d: Asian international teenage angst

Boogaloo or bugalú, a short-lived genre that blended Afro-Cuban and Puerto Rican musical tradition with Black American jazz, soul and R&B. It beautifully crystallised the diversity of Harlem in 1960s. I hope this snippet of my vinyl collection transports you to how NYC may have sounded like then.

House mixed with live FM radio? You heard that right! I sample live radio while mixing multiple genres of House. Doing so pays homage to my roots: I fell in love with House listening to radio out of Tucson, Arizona and shows streamed over NTS, such as Steel City Dance Discs. The result are sets imbued with audio-terroir, improvised and meta in a new way.
Underground legend Bloodz Boi leaves untold emotions in sounds. Broadcast from Beijing on a Tuesday every month.
Bi-monthly random fandom.
Berlin-based artist Pavel Milyakov aka Buttechno joins us for an hour a month.
Danish label Posh Isolation join us for an hour a month.
Minneapolis five piece she’s green contribute an hour of selections covering their influences and personal favourites, from OPN to Led Zeppelin.
Henriette and Catharina of Smerz take over the NTS airwaves once a month with a selection of classical, folk, ambient, pop and more.
collab episode featuring special guest M Sage
Relaxing & spiritual sounds with Copenhagen-based composer & producer Sofie Birch.

nts2.n: Lost in the Bitrate w/ Lena Raine
nts2.d: This month's episode of Lost in the Bitrate covers a wide range of styles from gritty glitchy electronics, to synthy jazz fusion, to sweeping digital palettes. Settle in for another journey through the digital landscape.
Sarah Davachi serenades the LA airwaves once a month with Le Jardin - an amalgamation of progressive rock, acid folk, early music, experimental productions, & minimal sympathetic sounds.

nts2.n: ZEL ZELE W/ DEBORA IPEKEL & ECE DUZGIT (R)
nts2.d: London & Istanbul based record label Zel Zele takes us on a monthly ride to earthly wonders and cosmic adventures. Bite the lip take the trip!

nts2.n: Day Care w/ The Alaia & Randy Ellis: Boogie-Funk and House Vibes
nts2.d:
CQQL Records founders DJ Randy Ellis and The Alaia bring you Day Care, a 2-hour monthly afternoon curriculum of Boogie Funk, Disco, R&B, G-Funk, House, Modern Soul wax, and way way more. Expect to be powdered and pampered by these audio obsessives.
From the host: "Celebrating two new releases from CQQL Records: Walter Thomas - Chicago Knights LP (Artist Retrospective) and Jackie Gritness - Introducing Jackie Gritness 12" (Deep House / Acid House)"
Dam-Funk slides by the LA Studio for a special guest set.

nts2.n: WHAT MEANS THE WORLD 2 U W/ THEO BARK

nts2.d: Quiet purveyor of hip-hop and beyond, DJ Luh delivers a power hour of the best in hip-hop

Founders of Lagos’ new-age underground festival, FREEWATER, join NTS for a 6-show residency, diving deep into the next wave of Nigerian music. Each hour-long show explores our favorite jams from the city’s bubbling alternative scene, covering alt-afro, indie, electronic, and trap sounds shaping the future of Lagos’ underground.
From the host: "Lagos’ new-age underground festival, FREEWATER, join NTS for a 6-show residency, diving deep into the next wave of Nigerian music."
Memphis selector and radio host Nya presents an hour of rare soul, boogie, gospel, and funk records once a month.

nts2.n: Under the Ocean w/ Ciao Ciao
nts2.d: Aquatic dub, sub-nautical IDM, deep-sea drone, vaporous synths and nocturnal bass wash up at the shore above the scurrying of crabs trying to understand the rhythm of the tides. A mix for the most serenely languid dancefloor at the bottom of the sea. Endless, timeless oceanic drift.

nts2.n: Saudades de Verao w/ Mr. Chong
nts2.d:
This mix contains cd only tracks from most Lusophone (PALOP) countries, mainly the focus is Zouk & Kizomba from Angola & Cabo Verde, with a few oddballs from Mozambique and São Tomé 
please enjoy this mix while longing for sunnier times !

nts2.n: Suck Meh Soucouyant w/ Blood Hags
nts2.d: "She’ll suck your whole world dry!" This sharp-fanged session traces bloodthirsty desire and vampiric terror in the Caribbean. Political critiques pierce ‘70’s soca rhythms. Soucouyants and duppy haunt ‘80s calypso and ‘90s dub. Hear ghostly forewarnings preserved in percussion.

A dusk-lit journey through South African jazz, bridging elders and contemporary voices. From Abdullah Ibrahim to today’s torchbearers, this mix is less about nostalgia and more about continuity — music as memory, resistance and return. Press play, exhale, come home.

pop.soil is Lyra Pramuk’s bimonthly series exploring catharsis, transformation, and healing through sonic storytelling.
From the host: _"From the host: _"This mixtape is a tribute to my late grandfather, honoring my heritage and Sufi practices across Morocco, Egypt, Tunisia, Sudan, and Turkey. I named the set “Taking Off My Shoes” to echo the ritual of going barefoot when entering sacred space, inviting listeners to hear this with the same intention."_
A monthly sonic journey from Lukid a.k.a Luke Blair. Expect a slow-burning, cinematic hour of hip-hop, epic psych, dub and synth soundscapes.

nts2.n: You'll Soon Know w/ Tim Parker & Sonnyjim (15th Anniversary Show)
nts2.d: Tim Parker explores the experimental corners of beats and hip hop, weaving together jazz, underground cuts and global sounds - live once a month from London & Amsterdam.
A feel-good hour of deep grooves and soulful selections served up by Tone.
The Utrecht native from the Netherlands Carista, joins us to share music of all kinds going from Soul, Jazz, House and everything in between.

Mr. Big Happy is a graphic designer, artist, DJ, and music archivist from San Francisco, California. While he is an accomplished designer and artist, over the years he has always had a love for music and spends time searching for rare and obscure vinyl. Big Happy’s focus is the late 70s into the late 80s - modern soul, funk, boogie and disco.

1 hour of disco and italo from Mexico.
Made by Coco Maria.

nts2.n: MUSIC FOR A HEALING EARTH W/ THE COSMIC TONES RESEARCH TRIO
nts2.d: Healing and restorative sonic transmissions for all beings.

Phil Cho & Russell Alexander share monthly recordings from their hilltop soundsystem in Echo Park, California.

nts2.n: ABBY IMPERIAL w/ The Grouch DJ

The eclectic and diverse frequencies from a true East Bay Area native, Abby Imperial is ready to bring the soulful sounds of Rnb, g-funk, deep house, jungle & more..

nts2.n: NTS Guide to: Soulful Séga & Electric Maloya from the Indian Ocean (R)
nts2.d:
In the 1970s, artists from Réunion and Mauritius began fusing island styles - Maloya and Séga - with psychedelic rock, funk, and soul. 
Séga, rooted in Afro-Creole rhythms and often sung in Mauritian Creole, developed as a celebratory dance music drawing from European, African, and Indian influences. Maloya, native to Réunion, originated in enslaved Malagasy and African communities - a slower, percussive style traditionally performed with vocals, kayamb and roulér, and associated with ritual, resistance, and later, political activism.
nts2.j: Soulful Séga & Electric Maloya from the Indian Ocean

nts2.n: BTWN EARTH+SKY: LARA SARKISSIAN, THE FORGOTTEN USB VOL. 2 - STEPANAKERT'S MUSIC STORE 2016

nts2.d:
Cold winds, empty street. Diamondstein, member of the band Melted Bodies, presents Night Shift - ambient techno, sinister industrial sounds, and moody experimental music for night time noir moments.
From the host: "Robert Alfons' project TR/ST is a goth-pop staple, marked by his undeniable voice. It's the kind of music that you know was inspired, or written, the ghostly hours that Night Shift is all about. So this show has a touch more of the morbid, sullen lyrical tunes that you think about as much as feel."

nts2.n: Low Life Loves You w/ Bill Brewster (R)
nts2.d: Writer, historian and DJ Bill Brewster showcasing two hours of love, laughter, and music.

nts2.n: Thai Tom Yum Covers w/ 242 Records Store
nts2.d: All vinyl selection of Thai international covers. From late 70s to mid 90s when music copyrights were cloudy, Thai artists were running wild with soul, disco, reggae, soundtrack and pop with own arrangements and cheeky lyrics.

nts2.n: Sinophone Reworks of Cold War Global Hits w/ Loquat Covers
Wait it's all Neil Young? W/ Anatomy of Melancholy
nts2.d: Despair, heartbreak and open-road loneliness — 60 minutes of textural catharsis. Tracing Neil Young’s influence across slowcore, countrygaze and distorted Americana, where fragile songwriting meets blown-out amps and the beach is never far from the feedback.

During the first lockdown I became obsessed with finding and collecting Jamaican Dancehall 7"s. It seems like the late 90's and early 00's was a fertile period for experimentation, and I love the anything goes attitude to the productions of this era. Here is an hour of some B-sides - enjoy!

Songs by bands from the cultural movement formed in Dunedin, Otago, New Zealand, in the early 1980s and onwards. The Dunedin sound was marked by jangly guitars, reverb, and influenced by The Velvet Underground and the Stooges, with most of the music released through Flying Nun Records. One of the first lo-fi movements out there!
80's Rampwalk delivers an hour of cold wave, minimal synth, industrial, post-punk and music made in Berlin basements.
Rose-tinted hallucinogenic sounds from Astral Vibes' Acid Memories. Illicit dealings in psychedelia of all persuasions. Sit back and enjoy…

nts2.n: Sunn O))) w/ Stephen O’ Malley
nts2.d: Legendary Seattle drone summoners Sunn O))) grace the NTS airwaves in a special three part takeover.

nts2.n: FIFTH WORLD W/ IAN KIM JUDD
nts2.d:
A riff on Jon Hassell's Fourth World series. Where Hassell focused on creating ritual music of potential societies that didn't exist; Ian Kim Judd seeks to evoke the notion of potential utopian societies that could exist. In these times of global instability and uncertainty, Fifth World seeks to posit an alternative, with scenes from an optimistic future.
From the host: "From me to you.
Images by Kersti Jan Werdal"
Jen Monroe writes the album download blog Listen To This!, which focuses on Japanese and new age records: Listen to This
universal collage

Cheeky slacker selections from everyone's favourite - Homeshake. Kick back.
From the host: "ps goner special to celebrate the debut album "there's an atm inside" out now.  featuring inspirations alongside new ps goner tracks not found on the new album."
LA native Bianca Lexis showcases a monthly, genre-blending hour, exploring the different facets of pop, rock, synth and house music. all in panoramic view, for you.

nts2.d:
Exploring inner and outer worlds, cosmic and pastoral sounds, and the intersection between reality and dreams once a month with Jocelyn Romo's mix of international and private press folk, instrumental guitar, dream country, ambient, new age, kosmiche, psych and beyond
From the host: "Night-blooming jasmine through an open window on a spring night."
Soft-touch home listening selections live from LA every month with Luke Mele. Traversing ambient, jazz, guitar tones and more.

nts2.d:
Slowed Down, sun kissed journeys through dub, synth, cosmic & balearic beats. Brought to you from LA each month.
From the host: "feeling spiritual"

Athenian Marketplace takes the reigns of a 2 hour monthly slot from our LA studio. 
Soundcloud
Youtube

for you and me
From the host: "all for nothing"

nts2.n: Club Aerobics w/ Bianca Oblivion & Meg10
nts2.d:
LA-based DJ and producer Bianca Oblivion highlights dance floor gems from artists worldwide. Featuring special guests every month. Make ‘em sweat.
From the host: "In anticipation of their massive b2b for Hoemies at Berlin's Oxi Club, Bianca and Meg10 go in with some deep selections, sick blends, and of course, cute cunty club tracks."_

An exploration of sounds from an expanse of US and east Asian independent rock, all connected by Seam and Bitch Magnet founder, Korean-American musician Soo Young Park.
Selected by Smilegoth
For a late afternoon stroll, a mix of my favourite lost psychedelic treasures from the 60s

nts2.n: SOUNDS OF FIJI W/ MANA FRACTAL

nts2.d: I remember the thrill of experiencing my first Relief Records release. It was Paul Johnson’s ‘The Other Side of Me’ at Birmingham��s Hard To Find Records. Thirty years on, and Green Velvet’s Chicago-born label is still putting out jacking techno & house. This mix is a collection of largely seldom heard tracks with some pieces of well known bangers dropped in.

nts2.n: LA RUTA DEL BAKALAO W/ CAFÉ RIOT
nts2.d: 'La Ruta del Bakalao' was a massive clubbing and cultural movement centred in Valencia, Spain that flourished in the 80's and early 1990s. It pioneered marathon raving between clubs, and witnessed an exciting blend of club music, from industrial techno, to the early stages of Mákina, euro dance, and hardcore. This show looks at some of the music that spanned the 80s into the 90s that would have been played out on 'La Ruta', as well as other modern tracks that channel the energy of that era.

nts2.n: My Eyes Glaze Over: Mego's first phase - 1994-2005
nts2.d: A dive into the often beautiful, often abrasive (and frequently both) computer music of the influential Austrian label, Mego.

nts2.n: Sounds on Screen: HK Crime Movies
nts2.d: Gunfights, brotherhood and betrayal: this edition of Sounds on Screen traces threads through the soundtracks of Hong Kong’s golden age of crime film. From A Better Tomorrow to The Killer and Infernal Affairs, immerse yourself in a musical universe that sits alongside louche tailoring, faded colonial dives and bursts of stylised violence.

nts2.n: On the Road w/ Adelina S - Welcome to Hamarabr Pt. 2
nts2.d:
This episode delves into my love story with Fulani griot music and recounts my first encounters with different griot groups in Fouta-Toro.
From the host: "This episode delves into my love story with Fulani griot music and recounts my first encounters with different griot groups in Fouta-Toro."

Explorations in music from Ruth Tafébé a.k.a The Afrosoulmessenger.

Alessio Natalizia is Not Waving. Take the plunge with his monthly hour of fucked-up left techno, leftfield electronics, brain-twisting oddball pop and post-punk, dub offshoots and other diversions. 
ecstaticrecordings.com

Malibu is a French electronic musician whose work sails between ambient and ethereal music.
Forever inspired by soft reverbed vocals and melodious chord progressions, Malibu’s music is an immersive nostalgic journey in a sea of synthetic strings and choirs.

Where the wall meets the floor, Temporal Cove is a body of sound exploring the ethereal and ephemeral. Smilegoth dives into shoegaze, post-rock, house and more to articulate a flow of reverberations that can be felt on every surface.
From the host: "smilegoth mix featuring Robert Lester Folsom, xaviersobased, Mogwai and more ཐི༏����󠀮"
Sematary (aka Grave Man), a key figure in cult new wave drill and horrorcore collective Haunted Mound presents his monthly NTS Show.
Another transmission of dead mall energy from the later vaporwave era of the 2010s.

The sounds of the rap abyss and beyond, presented by the music blog No Bells. Hosted by Mano Sundaresan with special guests.
from the host: "Guest mix from Brooklyn artist/DJ/engineer extraordinaire Swaya, spanning the New York DIY circuit and beyond."
Label head Michael Lundy explores Rap Vacation’s experimental influences, with guests and exclusive cuts from the thriving LA beat scene.
Prestonian singer songwriter Rainy Miller presents the progress check, a bi-monthly show playing an hour of archived experiments and influences.

nts2.n: THEY ARE GUTTING A BODY OF WATER (R)
nts2.d: Philadelphia cult property and experimental shoegazers They Are Gutting A Body Of Water bring their hazy sound to NTS once a month.

I’m a huge Mr Oizo fan and have been listening to his music and growing with it since I was a teenager. I continue to fall in love with his music as I age. Some of his work that wasn’t really accessible to me as a young girl, because it sounded too obscure at the time, I’m now able to connect with as an adult, and it feels like I’ve discovered his music all over again.
I received my Flat Eric doll as a gift from a close friend when I was young, and he has been a huge part of my life ever since. Flat Eric has gone on many adventures with me - he’s been recognised from afar by strangers who are now good friends, they asked if they could take a photo with him. He has gone to festivals, had his portrait painted, sat on a very tall stack of milk crates, but most importantly, he has grown with me for the past 14 years.
Flat Eric aside, the Oizo/Ed Banger Records sound really shapes who I am musically. It marks the start of my musical journey, and while I don’t strictly play French Electronic today, it most definitely seeps through in the sounds I’m drawn to and play.
I love Mr Oizo’s entire artistic essence - from his album art, to his track titles, to the whole absurdist thing he has going on. There is something about his expression and work that truly resonates with me. My artist/DJ name is Flat Esoteric, a direct reference to Flat Eric. It only made sense for me to put together a mix that is an ode to Mr Oizo.

Inspired by moss, quiet, resilient, interconnected. this show weaves ambient textures, soft folk, and field recordings into a musical pause. No rush, no competition; just space to slow down, listen deeply, and embrace a gentler rhythm. Like moss, it grows, not advances. A moment to breathe.

nts2.n: On that 12 inch shyt w/ DJ Indulgence

collection of memphis rap classics released on vinyl, all killer no filler
my dearest style of hip-hop on my dearest medium

nts2.n: AMBIENT MUSIC FROM THE METROID PRIME TRILOGY W/ WUSKETTI
nts2.d: From ambient drones to hybrid electronic-orchestral textures, Kenji Yamamoto reshaped Metroid’s sound for 3D worlds. Blending classic motifs with synth atmospheres and subtle rhythms, his music captures solitude, tension and alien exploration, redefining unease in game soundtracks.

Edda Sabatini, better known as Edda Dell’Orso, was an Italian singer whose wordless voice became a defining feature of Ennio Morricone’s most iconic scores. Her soprano, simultaneously intimate and cinematic, runs through the sound of Italian film in the 1960s and ’70s.
This In Focus traces her solo work and collaborations across the wider world of Italian soundtrack and library music, with selections from Piero Piccioni, Bruno Battisti D’Amario, Temme Di Giuditta, and Armando Trovajoli.

God Colony are two producers and songwriters from Liverpool and the cofounders of the record label Crack Copies. Adam Curtis is the legendary BBC filmmaker known for his documentaries HyperNormalisation, Bitter Lake, Shifty, and more. `
Each NTS show will be collaborative and themed around a specific, unrevealed word, with that word acting as the hook for all song choices. Each show will be a different word. God Colony and Adam Curtis have known each other for a while and most research for these shows is done in the pub.

Occult tapes plundered by two low level scavengers: the finest dungeon synth, medieval dark ambient & black metal.
From the host: "Stricly Hellenic dungeon synth: cassettes and demo tapes from the fringes of the Greek & Cypriot metal scene, from 1994-1999."

nts2.n: The Uline Catalog w/ Calvin LeCompte
nts2.d: Leaf through songwriter Calvin LeCompte’s monthly selection via The Uline Catalog…

nts2.n: CREEP ZONE W/ JAMES PANTS & MARC SCHALLER
nts2.d: Stones Throw's James Pants joins forces with Marc Schaller for an hour-long dive into dreamy electronics, wired sounds and experimentation.
A monthly hour from Jane Records label-runner Braden Wells.

nts2.n: world of pain w/ ian collins

nts2.d: Filmmaker Jenny Sayaka Nono brings her manic VHS hued style to the audio airwaves, with steely coldwave, industrial, synth pop & more.
Yesterday's News: a thorough re-cap of the finest in punk, noise, garage and other ratty guitar sounds from the past few decades.

Local, regional, and unknown country singles shared from the dusty bins of America. https://dollarcountry.org/

Lex Records artist Boom Bip scrapes beneath the surface of the status quo by loosely basing his mixes on electronics and the new waves they produce.

nts2.n: NTS GUIDE TO: 60 YEARS OF PSYCHEDELIC POP - A BRIEF HISTORY (1963-2025) VOL II (R)
nts2.d: From the first budding of flower power to the present day – part two of NTS' journey through the history of psychedelic pop.
nts2.j: 60 YEARS OF PSYCHEDELIC POP - A BRIEF HISTORY (1963-2025) VOL II

nts2.n: All Squares In There w/ Oscurita
nts2.d: A (far) leftfield disco voyage of woozy synths, cosmic chug, and dubbed‑out grooves. Inspired by voyages outside of linear time, in the milky ether.

nts2.n: Midwest Fantasy Quest W/ Moral Compass
nts2.d: Some of the greatest hidden folklore can be found lurking throughout the Great Lakes where fantastical Midwestern tales are conjured by mystics who are subverting the modern mind. Evade your contemporary woes with tales of forgotten worlds and embark on a mythical journey of spellbinding sorcery.

It amazed me how good music Indonesia from the 70s and 80s. So around the year 2014-2015 I visited some vinyl shops in Jakarta, Bandung and Jogjakarta and bought around 40-50 records from that era.
This mix is me selecting my favourite tracks of that collection, recorded in my bedroom in Lichtenberg, Berlin.

Research into musical traditions across post-Soviet space — from Central Asia to the Caucasus, the Volga, the Urals and Siberia. This episode focuses on Shashmaqom from Uzbekistan and Tajikistan, tracing modal systems that survived empires and continue to evolve.

A collage of music made from or significantly featuring metal. Gongs, bells, wires, chimes, pipes, sheet metal. Traversing genres and times : from traditional Gamelan orchestras to the American avant garde via tool wielding industrialists from the 80s. Literally tracing the vibration from Bali to Berlin to beyond….
Mikey Young of Total Control and Eddie Current Suppression Ring plays a selection of 60's and 70's soft rock…

Francesco Fusaro, the man behind MFZ Records and classical music recording series 19'40'', brings a considered and varied selection of baroque, classical and contemporary music for a full two hours each month.
From the host: "HyperTurandot is a two-hour mix built to mark the 100th anniversary of the world premiere of Puccini’s Turandot at La Scala, Milan, on 25 April 1926. Rather than airing a single version, it cuts across several studio sets to let different Turandots collide: different voices, tempi, textures, colours"

nts2.n: THE OPERA SHOW w/ Bones Tan Jones
nts2.d: Hannah Catherine Jones splits things open for the Opera Show: an exploration of the best in opera old and new, as well as an unearthing of the operatic in other records from soul, jazz or further afield.

“I start associating your voice with ….”, for a while ML has been part of the NTS community, jingling around, airing his monthly 150 Session out of Berlin’s North, where all seasons give reasons. For clarification, creation, inspiration. One hour of poetic and musical explorations, questions, and curiosities. Circling around daring electronics, ethno-sonics, jazz-constructivism, minimalism, open structured digital dopamine, risky dub excitement and other sounds, that still need to be written. No country, no flag – outernational without a cause!
From the host: "150 Session brings a selection of contemporary musical compositions, featuring artists like Froid Dub, Läuten der Seele, Colombian Drone Mafia, Question Mark, Radio Hito, Mr Fingers, Spekki Webu, or Ricardo Villalobos."

nts2.n: Panorama Yerevan w/ Lucia Kagramanyan - Armenian Songs of Spring
nts2.d:
Lucia Kagramanyan digs through the archives of the Republic Radio of Armenia, presenting lost compositions, featuring everything from classical & opera, to folk & pop in a special hour long show.
From the host: "With spring unfolding, I've put together an hour of spring melodies and songs from folk to pop, classical, estradayin."

Visual artist and ceramicist Aaron Angell plays a vinyl-only monthly selection of music from his collection - usually focussing on guitar soli, privately pressed UK folk, psychedelia, and the occult new age.

Lucia Kagramanyan digs through the archives of the Republic Radio of Armenia, presenting lost compositions, featuring everything from classical & opera, to folk & pop in a special hour long show.
From the host: "With spring unfolding, I've put together an hour of spring melodies and songs from folk to pop, classical, estradayin."

LABOUR is the duo of Colin Hacklander and Farahnaz Hatam. Tune in each month for their hour-long show featuring rotating guests and friends from Dakar, Senegal. Expect a mixture of progressive electronic music with heavy sabar drum music, classic & current Mbalax, and timeless Sufi praises.
From the host: "This month’s show features the great Senegalese percussionist and bandleader, Pape Thiopet, aka Pape Kaolack Ndiaye, with his 2008 collab album "Avant Que Le Journal Passe" with Ass Seck, followed by tracks from previous guests of this show Amy Colle Dieng & Fallou Dieng, featuring his percussion."
Explorations in music from Ruth Tafébé a.k.a The Afrosoulmessenger.

LABOUR is the duo of Colin Hacklander and Farahnaz Hatam. Tune in each month for their hour-long show featuring rotating guests and friends from Dakar, Senegal. Expect a mixture of progressive electronic music with heavy sabar drum music, classic & current Mbalax, and timeless Sufi praises.
From the host: "This month’s show features the great Senegalese percussionist and bandleader, Pape Thiopet, aka Pape Kaolack Ndiaye, with his 2008 collab album "Avant Que Le Journal Passe" with Ass Seck, followed by tracks from previous guests of this show Amy Colle Dieng & Fallou Dieng, featuring his percussion."

nts2.n: Club Romantico Takeover w/ Florentino, dj g2g, Safety Trance, Phran
nts2.d: Club Romantico takes the London studio with a two hour session traversing the full flavour of the club night’s sounds – selection from Florentino and the whole Romantico gang.

nts2.n: The Mellow Sounds of Bolivian Folklore and Film Music
nts2.d: Sometimes when you live abroad is when you start realizing the wonderful aspects of your country and that's what has happened to me, specially with folkloric and film music from Bolivia. Bolivia is full of wonderful composers and musicians, so I just wanted to share some of my favorites songs that have been with me lately while I am far away.

nts2.n: From Hill and Holler W/ Ben Adams
nts2.d: From Hill and Holler explores early 20th century musical recordings from Appalachia. Defined largely by the use of the fiddle and banjo and techniques like shape-note singing, these folk songs are the product of European ancestry and influence from African rhythms.

nts2.n: WITH LOVE FROM CANADA W/ AAL
nts2.d:
This mix is inspired by all the music I have come across since moving to Montreal from Berlin in summer 2024. I spend most of my time going to shows and engaging in local music and art scenes in the places that I live, and that is what I would say I 'know best' - learning through my personal experience and by -being- right in the middle of it all. Live music always impacts me most, it's how I've come to love music and genres of music I would've otherwise never gotten to love in the same way. I wanted to share a bit of that and what I got to know and cherish so dearly since living here - a slice of Montreal, and Canada, for the NTS listeners across the world. 
(This mix is by no means exhaustive or fully representative, just a personal take and selection based on my experience and taste/+mood :)). 
The selection includes songs by bands I have seen live here in Montreal, whether these are local, from across Canada (e.g. Toronto, Vancouver or elsewhere), or from across the border in the U.S. Music influences are so fluid and unpredictable, and seeing how these influences shift and mingle is so exciting to experience! 
I am so appreciative of community radio as a medium. Always a pleasure sharing a bit of music and time with anybody interested and curious and in need :)<3. Thank you so much to you guys at NTS in any case for the openness and for taking the time! Big supporter of your work!

nts2.n: Midwest/East Coast Underground Post Punk w/ Aliza Shiah
Footwork 4 Ever & Ever w/ Metaverse Bitch
nts2.d: Movement, pressure, and feeling — dig into it.

An ode to pre and early 2020s underground female trap. Through abrasive samples, intoxicating hooks, and lush digital production, pluggnb and drill remains an unwavering energy soundtracking late nights in big cities by artists in their own lane. For the girls who Daydream.

nts2.n: Head Of Household w/ CIG MOM

nts2.d: This mix explores the raw and emotionally driven side of soul and funk recorded between 1969 and 1977. Bringing together some well-known cuts and others less familiar, it reflects the pure essence of the 1970s, where youthful harmonies, early group formations and heartfelt performances blend with grooves that evolve from rustic immediacy into growing sophistication.

nts2.n: Disco's Revenge w/ Gus De Silva
nts2.d: Post-disco, boogie, proto-house, however you classify it, all share elements that I can’t resist - deliciously juicy synths, soaring vocals, and punchy drums. Disco productions of 1980-86 were stripped back, used synthesisers and drum machines, and bridged to the future: house and electro and more.

nts2.n: yumemiru kikai (dream machine) w/ Finn den Hertog
nts2.d: Moving through hazy lullabies, cosmic dirges and intimate ephemera, this is a pilgrimage through Japan’s indie, psych, weird folk and experimental scenes; spanning several decades yet suspended outside of time. Merely skimming the surface of an imaginal sphere I would happily immerse myself in time and time again. A sonic dream machine.

Britain's best indietronica came from Brum. South Birmingham to be exact. This mix provides a glimpse into the wonderful melting pot of experimental art that was occuring in Moseley from the mid 90's and onwards.

« Gaur » means « today » in euskara. This mix aims to showcase a diversity of contemporary Basque artists, representing different genres and mainly based in Hegoalde (the southern part of Euskal Herria). Let yourselves be carried away by the musicality of a region and a language that are dear to me.

nts2.n: Contemporary Sounds of the Trautonium w/ Zar
nts2.d: It’s rare: an instrument where the number of composers who use it barely reach double digits. The Trautonium. Somewhat forgotten, but eternally ethereal, this mix represents the 1930’s synth’s incredible expressive capabilities across experimental electronic and contemporary classical music.

nts2.n: Folklore of North & West Africa w/ Afrodesi
nts2.d: A musical journey from Morocco to Nigeria through a selection of Folk and Traditional music of North and West Africa. We start in Morocco, travel to Mali via Algeria and Mauritania. From Mali we go to Burkina Faso via Guniea and Cte d'ivoire. Finally from Burkina Faso we go to Nigeria, our final stop via Benin.

Cyberpunk: neon bleeding into wet asphalt, flesh interfaced with chrome, corporations ruling from above while netrunners hack from the margins.
This mix skips most of the anthems. Instead, it dives into rare soundtrack cues, deep cuts from cult films and series, and musicians who absorbed cyberpunk's DNA into their own frequencies. Deck ready. Jack in.

nts2.n: On the Phone w/ Noah Thomas Miller
nts2.d: This program was recorded over the telephone, through voicemails, phone conversations, dial tones, and including a series of songs that incorporate a theme or subject all about the telephone. With songs, sound bites, and messages intermixed, this program traces lines around the world.

From basements, bedrooms, and bar rooms across the carnation state, LO-FI-O documents the scrappy, DIY sounds that define the Ohio underground from Robert Pollard's prolific projects through the Shitgaze wave of the early-2000s to the current crop of Ohioans carrying the fuzzed out, trashy torch.

nts2.n: A DOZEN STRINGS W/ JULES ANA
nts2.d: New and notable compositions featuring Asian zithers – koto, jūshichi-gen, gayageum, and geomungo.

A collection of songs taken from Gustavo Cerati's (RIP) entire catalogue including solo, side projects, & collaborations; curated by many friends through many years to be enjoyed under the blissful effects of cannabis.

nts2.n: The Sun Rises in the East w/ The Propaganda Site
nts2.d: Western Asia meets Eastern Europe in the mix of songs that underscore the profound, the absurd, the joyous, and the mournful, sometimes in slightly mind altering ways.

nts2.n: Songs for Ham the Astrochimp w/ Edie Le Blancq
nts2.d:
Songs dedicated to Ham the Astrochimp (1957-1983)
Miss you big man x

nts2.n: AT THE BRITRONICA FESTIVAL w/ DJ LOOSE
nts2.d: In the wake of Perestroika, the British underground descended upon Moscow, hungry and penniless. Only some even got the chance to perform in this confused clash of cultures, but fueled by paranoia and pent-up rage, 1994’s Britronica Festival let some lucky few dance away into their brave new world.

An ode to old school perreo–from its reggae roots in Panama’s working-class West Indian diasporas to its evolution in Puerto Rico and beyond, including collabs with US rappers. Spanning the 1990s to reggaeton’s golden age in the early-mid 2000s, this mix draws on bootleg compilations like Baile Reggaeton and Reggaeton Traxx.

More u meni (The Sea Within) moves through Bosnian sevdalinka, Yugoslav radio folk and Macedonian song. From suspended guitar to romantic swell, these voices trace longing as an inner sea – something remembered, repeated and carried within.

nts2.n: HIDDEN FLESHLITE MUSAK w/ DRONE FOREVER
nts2.d: Compiling freaky music for freaky people — a dose of heavy groove to carry you through the week without having to yell at your Boss, God or Loved ones, and to mentally set you up for a hell of a weekend. Grab a hold and ride that freight, be yourself, don't hesitate!
This mix is a collection of my favorite ethereal fem voices in dark gazey music. Listen for screams, drones, and fuzz.

nts2.n: a tour through twerknation w/ mykissera

nts2.d: A mixtape entirely made of interludes, intro and outro songs plucked out of classic and contemporary black and death metal albums. These in-between hiatuses, meant to ease listeners in and out of blastbeat-driven tracks, now take center stage. A warm folksy voyage through cold otherwordly soundscapes.

nts2.n: Torch Songs and Aftermath Ballads w/ Sinziana Velicescu
nts2.d: Pour something strong and sit with the devastation of unrequited love. Torch songs and aftermath ballads for those who stayed too long and felt too deeply, from our greatest queens of sorrow and the occasional lovesick gentleman.
A fixation on the voice – shifting between whispered intimacy, devotional singing, fractured language and stretched vocal harmonies.

Drift along the Aether guided by deep hypnotic grooves and mind-bending polyrhythms. A spiritual journey through spacetime - an exploration of music as ancient magic ritual, a way of communing with the invisible world, of connecting the body to greater cosmic currents, to enter the world of feeling.
I've always been an avid reader and keen on wordplay so I would like to pay homage to spoken music.

Broken club, dubbed out sounds, experimental electronics and atonal drifts: Gio’s monthly broadcasts are high contrast assemblages that move at a slow pace.
From the host: 'This month's show features a guest mix in the second half by Brusselles based artist maya dhondt, who's put together an hour of music featuring pianos used in interesting ways. The file she sent me is called "what to do with the piano mix" and I find it to be the most appropriate title for it.'

Ahead of Wounder’s NTS15 live show, where he will be joined by Isaac Maxwell on improvised electronics. The two present a selection of Metal, Ambient, and Electronic that have shaped their upcoming performance.

Monthly mixes and "Bad behaviour" from the crew behind Berlin's acclaimed Mala Junta parties. Expect throwback trance, souped up '90s basslines and party music of a high tempo flavour.
From the host: "EVEHIVE kicks off spring  fever with a mix full of percussions and explosions of rhythm."

nts2.n: AO Radio presents South African Underground w/ mfanatouchline, kae.woe, rrapid444, thandrich888
nts2.d: Special guest shows from around the world.

Chester, PA’s DJ FINNAHITALICK (Blackwealth in the club) is a seasoned sound archivist of Underground Hip-hop. Starting Hit A Lick Radio in 2016, DJ FINNAHITALICK explores rap’s extensive subgenres and regional epicenters, ranging from DMV crank and Atlanta Plugg to Milwaukee low-end, Michigan punch-rap and beyond. His fast-paced mixes encapsulate Underground Hip-hop’s diverse landscape, and are not for the weak-hearted.
From the host: "Guest mix from Daysafterodeo out of Atlanta, Georgia."
Native Sound System join us once a month, breaking the latest sounds from Lagos

HD features a vast variety of guests spanning across hip hop, RnB and electronic music to talk about music, culture and life aswell as share their favorite selections of any genre.
From the host: 'DJ Guari is one of the most exciting producers in NYC right now.  Hailing from The Bronx, Guari is pioneering a sound all his own, fusing Drum & Bass with Perreo and Techno, with hints of Reggaeton and NY Latin house, Latincore, and more. "

nts2.n: UNITED IN FLAMES W/ TORUS & MALIBU (R)
nts2.d:
Malibu is a French electronic musician whose work sails between ambient and ethereal music.
Forever inspired by soft reverbed vocals and melodious chord progressions, Malibu’s music is an immersive nostalgic journey in a sea of synthetic strings and choirs.
Visual artist and founder and editor-in-chief of The Editorial Magazine Claire Milbrath joins us for an hour of ambient, classical, and italian library music.

nts2.n: KRISTIN KONTROL PRESENTS: OUT OF VOGUE
nts2.d:
Former frontwoman of rock outfit Dum Dum Girls eschews the pseudonym with her new solo project, Kristin Kontrol. Newly relocated to LA, Kristin delivers a monthly mix of expansive and hypnotic sounds: one part Durutti Column to two parts Alice Coltrane.
From the host: 'Gentle Spring Musing Music + closing out with the debut of new single "Cocoa Beach" by Daughn Gibson'
Every month Jonah Rabb brings you Weekend Read, an hour of funk, house, contemporary R&B, and more.

Cultural virtuoso and creative luminary SHABAZZ, aka DJ Auntie Spice, illuminates obscure nostalgic treasures, crafting a rich, diverse auditory experience of soul, funk, jazz and beyond with her sound art initiative, Auntie·ism.

An hour a month from LA label Total Stasis, responsible for releases by Ramzi, Aquarian Foundation, Anom Vitruv, Elysia Crampton, Bobo Eyes & Plush Throw, CS + Kreme, Fmvee, Second Language, Regularfantasy, Private Lesson, Alexi Baris, Laila Sakini, and Sebastiano Carghini.
Delivery presents: 'HAAN', live for an hour a month, straight from our LA studio.

A genre-spanning show focusing on rare grooves, classics, new productions and obscurities with a worldwide perspective and a spotlight on soul, synth pop and leftfield house. All curated with love by Javybz - Anya and Julia.
A narrow but luminous collection of nineteenth century baroque piano transcriptions offering a glimpse behind the veil, a taste of the empyreal and beyond…

A journey through the many forms of the arpeggiator as an emotional and atmospheric force across genres. From mystical videogame soundtracks to French pop and modern electronica, these sequenced patterns shape space, movement and quiet intensity. Nostalgic, dream-dazed and cosmic, where repetition becomes feeling and arpeggios breathe like memory.

SCANZONATISSIMA turns the DJ set into a narrative device, using selection as a form of sonic archaeology. FROUGE's research explores Italian culture as a collective emotional archive, drawing on sources such as the national Italian television archives and moving through her Neapolitan funk roots, disco, library music and forgotten or overlooked songs suspended between TV shows, cinema and the hidden balere, the traditional social dancefloors of Southern Italy.

Piper’s Lament
warlocks and witches in a dance.
nae cotillion brent new frae France,
but hornpipes, jigs, strathspeys, and reels,
put life and mettle in their heels.
a winnock-bunker in the east,
there sat auld Nick, in shape o’ beast;
a towzie tyke, black, grim, and large,
to gie them music was his charge:
he screw’d the pipes and gart them skirl,
till roof and rafters a’ did dirl.
from “Tam o’ Shanter” by Robert Burns
Devoted to Elizabeth Dobie-Sarsam and her ancestors. May their memory live on in the pipe’s drone.

Geneva's DJ Fine plays piano-centered pieces, often accompanying compositions with spoken world and poems.
From the host: "This episode features an original sound-piece / poem written and performed by Elise Amal Connor."

Monthly enqiuries into encounters in harmony, forms of hearing, and occasionally responding.
From the host: "The singer isn't important as the listener - an hour of silk fiddles across East and Southeast Asia."

North London native and Hyperdub signee Loraine James is an electronic producer who effortlessly blends Electronica and IDM with whispers of jazz and ambience all wrapped up in the artists key avant-garde aesthetic and sensibility.

Loops, remixes, edits and reinterpretations from DJ and programmer miss sheitana, oscillating between post-club, rap, baltimore club, bass, ambient and remixes from the limbos of the internet.
From the host: "just recovered a part of my teens / early twenties music library from an old usb….
° you wanna be one of them ° F#m ° nostalgic spring ° guilty indie ° smells like ghettoteck ° s/o asmara s/o totalfreedom ° rip bella ° 100% coming of age"
Deep-cut house and techno sessions from vet club selector Wes Baggaley.

Optimo is JG Wilkes & JD Twitch; record label, and longstanding Glaswegian party. You can expect a lot from their new monthly slot, sure to dig up some obscurities from post-punk, new wave, EBM, house and techno. Bags of unreleased stuff, too.

nts2.n: THE LUNACY OF FLOWERS W/ DAVE ID
nts2.d: Dave I.D takes us through industrial, and techno – melodic and dark. New and classic goth, his selection sets him apart from most, branching decades and scenes.
Manchester musician and game developer Murlo returns to the NTS airwaves to present the soundtrack and musical inspirations behind his game “Something Beautiful”.

nts2.n: Cosmic Soundz - Bolivian Garage & Psych Rock ’60s–’70s
nts2.d:
Lima, Peru vinyl obsessive Salvatore Dellepiane shares selections from his treasure trove of rare and obscure boogie and funk from all across the world.
From the host: "A journey through Bolivian rock from the ‘60s to ‘70s, capturing a lesser-known yet deeply rich scene shaped by global rock influences and local identity. These records were sourced during my time in Bolivia, where finding clean copies is rare. From garage/psych to raw, punky takes on classic cuts."

Los Hitters is the collective partnership of Joe Cristando (Couple Two T’ree) and Rene Lopez (Suavecito Souldies). A collaboration that links the two from Brooklyn, NY to Oakland, CA. Their mixes focus on soul sounds comprised of their vinyl collection.

nts2.n: FUSION BATCHES W/ DJ MORSE CODE
nts2.d:
DJ / Producer Morse Code brings us a monthly all vinyl mix of jazz-funk, jazz fusion, and rare groove in the tradition of his Fusion Batches mixes from the mid 00's, which championed dollar bin heat as well as the rares, all in the name of the hunt for the best grooves!
From the host: "a nice fresh batch for Spring! enjoy."

DJ Kita is a Los Angeles based DJ most known for her energetic sets and dynamic hip hop selections. Her sets are fun and unpredictable, and capture the youthful essence of hip hop culture. Kita is a resident DJ and co curator of LA’s premiere black and queer underground party called Hood Rave. She has toured both nationally and internationally.

Lex Records artist Boom Bip scrapes beneath the surface of the status quo by loosely basing his mixes on electronics and the new waves they produce.
From the host: "Ambient Textures, Drum and Bass and Early Electronics"

nts2.n: The Last Playlist: Iranian Special w/ Luis Felipe Farfán
nts2.d:
"The Last Playlist" explores mortality via songs people want at their funerals. Guests share stories ranging from hilarious to heartbreaking, discussing their musical choices while exploring death, grief, and what makes life worth living. Each episode weaves together music and conversation to celebrate life's complexities and honor its inevitable end.
From the host: "This is a guide to Iranian bangers from the 60's - a special episode, dedicated to all the Iranian friends."
Another mixtape, another mistake. Come get broken with Noa Kurzweil (from VOICE ACTOR).

nts2.n: imaginative architecture of utopia w/ solemnly sage
nts2.d: The ambient and experimental worldbuilding of Black artists that play in digital dreamscapes, sonic oceans of static, and glittering drones… re: a new world crafted of digital radiance and texture, inspired in part by Legacy Russell's Glitch Feminism

nts2.n: Berebere In My Eye w/ Nacho Kenji

nts2.d: Our friend Matt has been sending us field recordings from Cambodia made on his first ever, begrudgingly accepted phone for a year and a half while he's been based in the casino border town of Poipet. Along with the youtube links he's sent to music heard on busses and out of shops and bars, we've made this mix, because he wouldn't have the first clue how to.

nts2.n: NUESTRO LUGAR W/ FROM AND CARLOS BARREDO
nts2.d: Nuestro Lugar is a careful selection of hidden gems from Spain ranging from as early as 1952 to the present. With both of us living abroad, the mix gave us a chance to dig into lesser-known tracks, reminding us of home in different ways.

nts2.n: SICHUAN TEA HOUSE w/ JORDAN PAUL ROUSSEAU
nts2.d: An hour of guqin music by Wang Hua-de, tracing sounds born in Chengdu’s Sichuan teahouses. Sparse, intimate, and unhurried—this mixtape sits with tradition, daily ritual, and the quiet power of strings resonating through time.

“Explorations” is an attempt to shed light on offbeat music from India and around the world. Digging In India aka Nishant Mittal is a music archivist, DJ and record store owner based in New Delhi.
From the host: "A journey through hypnotic corners of global music, from Balkan and mediterranean sounds to French medieval drone, Turkish disco, Indian film music and beyond present by Axel Moon from Ko Shin Moon"

Special thanks to Xabier Erkizia for facilitating all the materials on this episode, which are unreleased.
First episode in a series going through ERTZ: Other Music Festival’s archive.
Formative and unparalleled curatorial events & publishing house merging sound art, video art, experimental music, dance and performance that happened in the Basque Country (mostly in Bera Navarre) between 2000-2018.
Some of the international artists that visited the small town throughout the years include Amy Denio, Mark Cunningham, Charlemagne Palestine, AMM, Stan Brakhage, Ken Jacobs, Oren Ambarchi, Toshimaru Nakamura or Phil Niblock, amongst many others, as well as the local scene including Cafe Teatro, Voodoo Muzak, Xabier Erkizia, Iñigo Telletxea, Alex Mendizabal or Mattin.
Milesker entzuteagatik!

nts2.n: KEEL HER w/ Daniel O'Sullivan, Frank Maston & Greg Foat
nts2.d:
Prolific London based DIY & lo-fi pop producer Keel Her heads to the studio for a show once a month.
From the host: "Deep library music selections from composers Frank Maston and Greg Foat, who celebrate the release of their debut collab, Moving Images, on Magic Hollow - out April 17th!"
nts2.j: Daniel O'Sullivan, Frank Maston & Greg Foat

nts2.d: Legendary Seattle drone summoners Sunn O))) grace the NTS airwaves in a special three part takeover.

nts2.n: THE BADDIS SOUND W/ DANNIELLA DEE - Dee's Version Excursion
nts2.d:
Inspired by the iconic reggae & ska sounds of her home, singer & producer Danniella Dee shares a monthly crate of dub-wise selections, soul-soothing rocksteady, and heavyweight roots to the studio.
From the host: "An exploration into some of my favourite sounds from the B side. Rocksteady, Reggae, Steppers & Roots."

Tune into Martyn's monthly show on 2 for some delectable jazz treats from over the Atlantic.
From the host: "Previewing my new album "Music for Existing" and the music that inspired it."

nts2.n: SUBJECT TO CHANGE W/ TIMMHOTEP & Yaya Bey in High Fidelity
nts2.d:
Cultural critic and curator Timmhotep Aku explores the intersections of black music, life, metaphor, and meaning through songs and found audio footage.
From the host: "In this special episode Timmhotep is joined by singer-songwriter and performer Yaya Bey to discuss their album 'Fidelity' and its themes of diaspora, devotion, and love."

nts2.n: THE WINDMILLS OF YOUR MIND w/ Taylor Rowley
nts2.d: DJ & music supervisor Taylor Rowley forages for the forgotten musical past every month, uncovering soundtracks, psych rock & other treasures.

nts2.n: SEA TO SEA w/ Diana Ratsamee
nts2.d:
Seattle DJ and collector Diana Ratsamee digs out musical discoveries from across the world, notably sharing music from Lao and Thailand and Southeast Asia - airing rock, disco, soul and psychedelic records, as well as diving into present day musical movements in the region.
From the host: "Dee Pi Mai! Today's show features a mix of folk-fusion from contemporary Southeast Asian artists, as well as classics from the crate to celebrate the new year. Enjoy!"

The narrative is bursting with color and impossible to fit into a 60 minute timespan - but here is an effort to both timehop a personal story and nod to a timeless musical vignette of influences and inspiration that spans intensely amongst all corners of the artistic globe: shining bright into film + design + literature + circumstance as much as the sonic palette itself presents. Here’s a little exploration and adventure celebrating both the underbelly of the unknown with all that is beautifully pop.

nts2.n: BLACK STUDIES w/ Lamin Fofana & Mohammed Tatour
nts2.d:
Lamin Fofana is an artist and musician currently located in New York. His music contrasts the reality of our world with what’s beyond, and explores questions of movement, migration, alienation and belonging. His releases include Works In Metal, Here Lies Universality, Unsettling Scores, and The Open Boat. Fofana is the founder of the imprint Black Studies and phase4.zip
From the host: "On this episode, I’m joined by Mohammed Tatour, an Amsterdam-based Palestinian artist. We contemplate Adania Shibli’s Minor Detail, language, and untranslatability. Featuring music by Taymour x Bareetlblad, Kiss Facility, HAAi with James Massiah, Wadada Leo Smith, Surya Botofasina, and more."
Eddie Junior & friends trading sounds for 60 minutes monthly, committed to their own rhythm & tempo.

56 DJs & Stones Throw Records employees, David Valencia, Kota Yoshioka, Nic Cabrera, Dain Valdes, Shane Sakanoi & Tim Nable bring you “Whodis?” twice a month on NTS Los Angeles. Expect to hear sounds ranging from Hip Hop, House, Jazz, Soul, Funk, Disco, Electro and more.
The notorious Orange County Funk Freaks crew take over the LA studio once a month with their signature brand of boogie, italo, and lowrider funk.
Past, present and future soundtracks from Beirut to Bangkok via Bombay.
A scrappy, femme-fronted road trip through lesser known 70s/80s new wave, post-punk, and 90s indie pop.

nts2.n: It Came From the New Age Bins W/ Jonas Reinhardt
Early Hamra Project W/ Milan Rivet Biennès
nts2.d: This mix is about Lebanese kids growing up at the same time as i did. It focuses on early 2000s Lebanese pop music and was made exclusively for dancing at the dawn of the millennium.
Seductive and suspicious, these Italian b-film scores provide the soundtrack to your wildest daydreams.

Another journey through the spectrums of emotional ambient music. Thank you for sharing this space with me. I hope these sounds hold you gently and help you feel whatever needs to be felt. 
Deep gratitude to the incredible artists in the mix - and to you for listening. Lots of love, Lloyd (pirouette) ✨

nts2.d: A trek into the mythical mountains of Fairy Tale Music… It's moonlit adventure time! Or, a haunted storybook sitting, beckoning sirens from the ever-loving looking glass. Softly stitched, mystery-laden lullabies for the deepest reaches of your wild, melancholy heart… Sweet dreams, Leslie

I research Southern Italian folk music, specifically songs linked to water. I'm interested in the significance of different water sources as powerful symbols that mediate political, cultural, mythical or social narratives. This is a selection of some favourites (traditional&new), hope you enjoy :)

an hour of cartoon music : theme tunes, soundtracks, special songs sang by the characters themselves, from the 60s until today. focusing mainly on synth-driven delightful melodies from stop-motion and hand drawn gems, whether niche oddities or well-known family favourites.

nts2.n: SWISS UNDERGROUND RITOURNELLES w/ WAGNER LOVE
nts2.d: Songs in the fields, on mountain ridges, along rivers, in cellars and caves. In the shade of arid surfaces, where the wild things and our rich swiss underground ritournelles are  <3

The musician and early music historian David Munrow was only 33 years old when he passed away. Yet his legacy befits a man more than twice his age. A staggeringly prolific musician and educator, Munrow travelled the world soaking up the music of different cultures. He learned new musical systems and obscure instruments in the span days or weeks. 
He is most famous as the figurehead of the Early Music movement, performing and teaching music from the early Medieval period onward. His BBC radio show "Pied Piper" - was a centuries spanning, 600+ episode education in the history of music – from Monteverdi to Pentangle.
As well as his radio and television work, Munrow also released well over 50 albums in his short lifetime, a body of work which has since altered our perception of music history.
Amsterdam selector Orpheu The Wizard connects oddball and straight forward jams from different decades and different record shelves in a monthly show.
Monthly sessions from Zaltan, the mind behind Paris-based leftfield dance, electronic & downtempo imprint Antinote, with occasional appearances from friends and artists on the label.

Themed mixes, hidden gems, bedroom demos, interviews and other odds and ends once a month. Email: animalimagesearch@gmail.com

nts2.d: A monthly broadcasts from London-based curator Cynthia, taking you through a selection of her ongoing music research and personal favourites, blending sounds that move across time and genre.

Started in 1995 as a home-recorded cassette tape label. Since then, the label has released a wide variety of artists including Twee Pop, Garage, Shoegaze, and Toy-pop, such as Kactus, 800 cherries, red go-cart, Pervenche, Smiley and Daffodil-19. The label has a uniquely Japanese sense of style that sets it apart from Shibuya-kei.

From the host: "Ambulance Blues collects the bleed and tunes into what’s left. Dead-air hymns, warped voices, and dust-choked tracks traveled too long. Songs that feel weathered, wandering, slightly out of time. A monthly transmission from Mike Martinez."  https://www.mixcloud.com/ambulanceblues/ IG

nts2.d:

_"A mix of Japanese jazz funk anchored by artists like Casiopea, Ryo Fukui, and Yuji Ohno. Romantic and cosmic, enjoy this mix on sunny seaside drives or late night whiskey hangs." _

nts2.n: HOOD RAVE X UNDERGROUND RESISTANCE w/ BAE BAE

Dark Entries reissues archival and contemporary gems that prove to be crucial to our modern idea of bodily music that exists out of time. Electro, italo, cold wave, new beat, house, techno from a one-of-a-kind digger who visits dusty basements to rescue lost tapes.
From host: "Tribute to Keith McIvor"
Sam Shepherd a.k.a Floating Points barely needs introduction. Cataloguing depths uncharted to expose some of the best jazz, soul, rare groove and disco around.
Popstar Benny, DJ & producer based in Atlanta, Georgia

some time ago i came across the idea that ambient music didn’t truly begin with artists like brian eno, but can be traced back to classical music and its power to shift listeners into different states of mind. guided by that thought, i’ve been exploring and collecting contemporary works that use classical instruments while pushing further into ambient territories. This is a selection of pieces released between 2010 and 2025.

nts2.n: I WALK YOU DRIVE w/ KONSTANTINA YAN
nts2.d: Walking is falling deep into dreams, floating through fantasies; it doesn't matter where you step. A mix for a submersive journey through a sonic, flowing, earthy palette.

nts2.n: Yugoslav New Wave Classics ’70s–’80s w/ Johana
nts2.d: A love letter to “Novi talas/Novi val”: Yugoslav new wave and post-punk from the late ’70s–’80s. Political wit, art-school cool, and DIY urgency - music that still feels dangerously alive. I grew up with these records playing at home, so putting this together feels like coming back to them.

nts2.d:
88 white and black keys to another world
A homage to one of my favourite instruments taking us through modern day jazz arpeggiating into legends of mystical harmony through key changes into wild euphoric explorations, unreleased gems culminating in a finale of some truly timeless classical.

A portrait of Sicily through sound: devotional laments, canti popolari, field recordings and modern reinterpretations woven into 1hr of Mediterranean memory from an island shaped by migration and ritual. Artwork courtesy of Roberto di Alicudi, whose glass paintings embody Sicily’s luminous spirit.

Oldham, UK composer producer and performance artist plus44Kaligula presents a reimagining of the 1950s BBC radio series "Listen With Mother", attempting to seek order in chaos, tracing how stories persist and distort.

Hosted by Bob Holmes, member of SUSS, and host of the popular Ambient Country podcast, this unique program invites you into a realm where sound becomes landscape, and music transcends genre, inviting listeners to wander through vast, uncharted spaces of sonic possibility.
From the host: "Bob Holmes from SUSS plays music at the intersectrion of instrumental, ambient, experimental and Americana, featuring artists and tracks that have recently appeared on his Across the Horizon podcast series."

nts2.n: West Coast DIY Pop 2006-2015 w/ Matty Terrones
nts2.d: If you were a participant of the West Coast DIY touring circuit between the years of 2006 and 2015, chances are you would have come across these bands playing in some decrepit basement in Seattle or a collectively-run anarchist cafe in San Diego. This music is for tender hearts with pop sensibilities. This music is soft. This music is as free as the roads that take us from place to place, drifting along from rest stop to rest stop as the coastal breeze washes against the car.

Spain during the 80’s had a vibrant underground scene with a number of groups who experimented with synths and drum machines. In a Post-Franco society, the nation dove head first into hedonism.  “Elektro” arises from this, offering memorable cult releases and forgotten gems ranging from New Wave to Industrial sounds.

An immersion in the blue of the Ligurian Sea, in the music and poetry that have inspired -and continue to inspire- the entire Italian peninsula, starting from the city of Genova, also known as La Superba.

Selected choice of different instruments solo pieces in various musical genres… Searching for emotions within the sound of each instrument in these stripped orchestrations. How composers and / or interprets throw their soul through their instrument ? How with only the « timbre » or sound can many layers of emotions be developped ? Classical, jazz, pop, noise, the artist face his listener alone and the sound goes from one to one ! No matter the difference in style the intensity remains the same.

Hi, music nerds!
I'm DJ Mihajlo, music lover and DJ from Belgrade, Serbia. This mix is a glimpse of vivid Yugoslavian music scene, consisting mostly of music being made in the 90s. Here you can hear artists from Serbia, Montenegro and Croatia. Some of them are really famous, others forgotten. I wanted to introduce these musicians to wider audience and make you dive into the Yugoslavian music scene. 
Hope you'll enjoy it!

nts2.n: Neo-Medieval Folk in Sweden w/ Vox Vulgaris
Sunset in the Maghreb w/ Mike.H
nts2.d:
Sunset in the Maghreb (1959 – 1983)
I have a long-standing obsession with old music from the Maghreb region in North Africa. For this mix, I’ve chosen favorites from Algeria and Morocco, spanning three decades and various folk and pop genres and their crossovers. I feel deeply connected to the music, even though the lyrics remain beyond my understanding.

nts2.n: I Live in A Mumblecore Film in the Early 2000s W/ Yuki
nts2.d: I live in a early 2000s mumblecore film: Mumblecore is low budget, lo-fi, natural and awkward, sometimes actors sound like they are mumbling. Whimsical American indie songs - heartwarming, cozy, nostalgic. Sit back, relax, teleport into an early 2000s mumblecore film. Honourable mentions - my favorite mumblecore films that inspired this mix: Pieces of April (Peter Hedges, 2003), The Puffy Chair (Duplass Brothers, 2005), But I’m a Cheerleader (Jamie Babbit, 1999), and Ha Ha Funny (Andrew Bujalski, 2002).

nts2.n: MOTHER2ギーグの逆襲 OST MIX W/ CAPÙS

Mix of the original soundtrack of Earthbound (Mother 2) by Keiichi Suzuki and Hirokazu Tanaka. 
100% Earthbound, 50% joy, 51% trauma (with a 1% margin of error).

nts2.n: Champeta Global Vol.2 w/ Edna Martinez
nts2.d:
Curator and DJ Edna Martinez explores the champeta, an underground musical genre and dance that originated in the Caribbean coast of Colombia in the early 1980s. The rhythms and influences are said to have arrived with the sailors from West Africa in the 1960s and 70s. We will get to know in each episode its specificities and the relevance of this scene.
From the host: "Global Champeta presents a curated selection of tracks highlighting their sonic connection to picó sound system culture, from which champeta emerges, featuring artists from across different scenes and regions around the world."

Paying homage with mixes blending the sounds of yesteryears’ cumbia labels and the sounds of the future in cumbia happening now.

Inspired by Quinceñera parties in the hills of Mexico and beyond & rooted in partying as a form of immigrant resistance. La Cosecha was started by XOLO. Playing all Spanish, all vinyl norteñias, corridos, rancheras, cumbia and banda classics. Xolo's aim is to bring this music to a new generation.
From host: "Rolas Perras Que Rifan En Todos Los Barrios Y Colonias Malandras."

nts2.n: OPEN HAND REAL FLAMES - NINA SIMONE SPECIAL (R)
nts2.d:
OPEN HAND REAL FLAMES is a monthly show presented by Bass Clef. Each episode has a theme - usually a specific musical instrument or process, perhaps an artist or composer, sometimes a record label. Ranging wide over centuries and genres, looking for connections and joy. Always welcomes contributions, music, suggestions.
For old Floating World Music shows click here

An ectotherm is an organism reliant on environmental sources of heat to regulate body temperature. Ectotherm is also a Copenhagen-based record label founded by Courtesy and Mama Snake, home to releases from Schacke, Rune Bagge and Ibon.
Cassette rips and the others.

nts2.n: Dmitri Shostakovich Op.87 Preludes & Fugues w/ Monocarpics

nts2.d: This mix is my finest selection of Yugoslavian darkwave, industrial, experimental and dark disco, produced between 1980-1988 in former Yugoslavian countries. It is a result of my long-standing obsession with East European 80s wave music.
a selection of tracks that highlight the influence and elements of electronic dance music subgenres such as 2-step, breakbeat and glitch hop in late 90s/early 00s mainstream R&B and pop.

Here is a walk through a city, made up of the changing rhythm of your footsteps. You want to look at everything around you. You need to lose yourself, don't you? 
Like a heart surgeon, Minatch breaks down and reassembles on Audacity bits of sound, traces of memories and emotions from this walk.
Curio earworms and other obsessions from Krakow's Natalia Panzer.

Srinivas (brought the electric mandolin to Carnatic), plays Raga Hindolam; Hindustani violin duet on pure Basant ft. N. Rajam and Zakir Hussain; closing dhrupad from Ustad Faiyaz Wasifuddin Dagar"

Yakuzas, folk spirits, punk bodies, drifters, water, air, subways, and late nights under a full moon.

Tom Furse is a musician and compiler - searching out the spaces in between genres - tune in for all things wonky; an education in spacious sound and dusted soul.
NYC folktronica and indie duo Cicada hop on for an hour – presenting some of their current favourite music.

nts2.n: TWISTED DREAM DIARY W/ OKO DJ

OKO DJ, shares an hour of music inspired by dreams – a realm of musical introspection, endless imagination and non sense compositions…

From the host: _"smilegoth invites Cole from Wallows for a guest mix." _
"On tour I am taking lots of baths at hotels. These songs are all lifted off of my bathcore playlist. Enjoy <3 - Cole"

nts2.n: Slow Curiosities for Fast Worlds w/ Hill Men

nts2.d:
As automation and robotic technologies constantly reshape the world around us, rapidly becoming part of our everyday life, we find ourselves gazing at the future with equal parts fascination and dread. Rooted in my deep fascination for the electronic sounds of the late 70s and 80s, this mix explores the remarkable ways in which artists from this era( with the exception of Codachrom, from 2013, yet deeply echoing the spirit and sound of those years) used technology to create genuinely innovative music: synthetic landscapes, processed voices, cold minimal textures, rigid mechanical beats, and entirely new instruments made by connecting circuits and electronic components.
At a time when genuine musical creativity feels increasingly threatened by the rise of automated music generation tools, this exploration serves as a powerful reminder of how artists once confronted the machine — not to surrender to it, but to use it in service of their own vision. Those tools unlocked an incredible explosion of creativity and genuinity — pushing artists to explore the unknown, break their own boundaries, and produce some of the most revolutionary works that only the human mind and inspiration can create.

nts2.n: In Focus: Emahoy Tsege Mariam Gebru (R)
nts2.d:
An hour of spellbinding solo piano recordings from one of the most fascinating musicians of the past century. Born Yewubdar Guèbrou in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, into an upper class family, she was classically trained in piano and violin, notably even performing for Emperor Haile Selassie. 
She was invited to study at the Royal College of Music in London, but her admission was blocked by the Ethiopian government. Dismayed by this news, she turned to religion, becoming an Orthodox nun, taking the title "Emahoy" and changing to the religious name Tsegué Mariam.
She spent the rest of her years living out these holy orders, first in Addis, and eventually in Jerusalem, where she continued to write and record music – a truly singular style of piano composition, as indebted to the proto-ambience of Erik Satie as it is to the Ethiopian tradition. 
This mix, selected by NTS, features recordings from "Church of Kidane Mehret", a new archival release from Mississippi Records, including previously unheard material.
