nts2.n: Tapes Of Vietnam w/ Jordan Obarzanek
nts2.d:
This mix is an amalgamation of tapes collected from small antique stores, markets and music shops in Vietnam - focussing mostly on the genre of Vọng cổ, a style invented in the early 20th Century by Cao Văn Lầu which was used primarily in Cải Lương (or “Reformed Opera) theatre. When you hear the kind of melodic dialogue with fast plucked guitar-like instruments (traditionally a Đàn Nguyệt or “Moon Lute”), semi-improvising around specific scale structures - that’s Vọng Cổ. 
I had the idea to make this mix after a conversation I had with a Vietnamese lady running the bungalow I was staying in by the mountains just south of Hanoi last year. She’d overheard me listening to some of these tapes in the courtyard by the pool on my portable cassette player and came over to speak to me through Google translate. She regarded the music with familiarity and fondness and what ensued was an hour long speech-to-text conversation about music, the past, identity, belonging, and the TikTok-guided Vietnamese youth of today who don’t listen to this kind of music anymore. She often spoke with a hint of sadness or melancholy but was incredibly witty and clever and could identity many of the artists and songs I was listening to by ear (I wish I had her with me when I was writing the tracklist). 
I thought it was such a fitting conversation for the music we were listening to as Vọng Cổ’s direct translation is “Longing For The Past”.

nts2.n: The Year Punk broke ... in Spain
nts2.d: Take a step back to a turning point in time. Just as Sonic Youth was unveiling their iconic documentary from that whirlwind tour, a parallel musical landscape was beginning to bloom in Spain. It was a scene born from the same restless spirit: a mix of rebellion and trash culture that followed a path already being blazed in the UK and the US. The spark was kept alive by MTV, which filled our screens with the sights and sounds of inspiring new bands, alongside standout local shows like Plastic on public television…. It was also the era that witnessed the first stirrings of festival culture, a movement previously unknown in Spain. The Spanish "juventud sónica" took it all in, finding their own voice and embarking on a journey that would define their own unique path.

nts2.n: Ìbàṣẹ Yèyé Ọ̀ṣun w/ DJ Junior Star
nts2.d:
Òrìṣà Ọ��ṣun is known in West Afrika and throughout the Diaspora as the Goddess of fertility, wealth, prosperity, sensuality, music, dance, and is most commonly recognized as the Spirit of the River and cool, fresh, healing waters. Celebrated throughout the world, Yèyé Ọ̀ṣun is also a fierce warrior and protector of her people, making her one of the principal figures in the Ifá-Yoruba Òrìṣà pantheon. Her presence and energy have been known to bring forth balance and diplomacy to unstable situations. 
I invite you to dance, sing, and join me as I share just a bit of the celebration of Ọ̀ṣun's beauty and blessings through sound across the Diaspora.
ORE YEYE OOOOOO!!

nts2.n: Mexico Soft Future w/ Andres H. Covarrubias

Un-recognized, I fear most acutely when viewed in the mirror. Heavens escape it, earth not the same for it. You leave, you go, but you carry it more than you know.

nts2.n: A Love Letter to Stickfigure Recordings w/ Jalen Jenkins
nts2.d: Stickfigure Recordings was started in 1992 in Atlanta, GA. Throughout the labels history, it has put out records for Portait, Deerhunter, I Would Set Myself on Fire for You, and more. As an Atlanta resident, Stickfigure is my specialty, and this mix is my choice picks from the catalog.

nts2.n: SACRED HARP AND BEYOND W/ STEVIE FELICIANO

nts2.n: ON THE MOUNTAIN W/ TANNER MCCRARY
PSYCHEDELIC LEANING TURKISH ARABESQUE W/ SİNAN ÖZER
nts2.d:
Arabesque music is an often overlooked gem from my native land. I am equally excited and anxious to submit my first mix of music that are so near and dear to me
Turkish arabesque started gaining popularity in the 60's as a fusion of Arabic and Anatolian sounds. The lyrical themes are almost always unrequited love. When looking at the genre as a whole, deeper sentiments of rebellion against state, life, and sometimes even god are revealed. It is no surprise that the genre was banned by the state during the chaotic political climate of the 70s. The ban didn't last long but it's effects resonated into late 90s with mass media shunning the re-emergence of the genre. I remember our elementary school teacher lecturing us, a group of 10 year old students, about the destructive path arabesque fans take, as if she was giving a lecture on drug abuse. A silly exaggeration from sillier times, but also a testament to the power of this music
With the mix, I tried to find a niche within the niche to hopefully peak listener interest to dig deeper into the genre. Odd time signatures driven by darbukas, melodies of high pitched traditional wind instruments backed by violins, shifting vocal pitches that mimic crying, and lyrics sung from the heart rather than the diaphragm are the central musical themes on this mix. To lean into the psychedelic aspect, I included some Arabesque leaning good ol' psych Anatolian rock. To round of the trip we visit neighbouring Azerbaijani Gitara, a prime example of Turkic Psychedelia with a tinge of Arabic influence.
I hope you enjoy!

nts2.n: for the lovers (abridged version) w/ p.a.r.t.i.d.a
nts2.d: love is a universal language as is music. this is p.a.r.t.i.d.a's take on celebrating love in the face of oppression. these 12 tracks are a small snippet of a larger 10 hour playlist of love centric bangers from the 70s to present day.

"SPIRITUAL" 
MHYSA + chuki
Show title credit: Hannah Black
Crossover Sydney hardcore punk quintet Speed bring a slice of chaos to the London studio.

nts2.n: Tears in the Club w/ Jeune Pouce (R)
Don't Know Diddley W/ Adam Mario

nts2.d: Rap and videogames have always spoken the same language they are both obsessed with world building, both rewarding the obsessive. This selection pulls together tracks that live in that overlap; bubbly and bitcrushed, from saw waves and squares to arps and lush pads. A document of rappers who aren't just inspired by games but actively borrowing their sonic vocabulary to build something new.

nts2.n: Alpine Ambience & Divine Yodel w/ Nicolas Parker
nts2.d: From the depths of the Swiss Alps: beautiful, transcendent yodel melodies accompanied by bells, alphorns, field recordings, and other traditional Swiss instruments — old-world harmonies to ground you. Unhurried and timeless.

I wanted to make a mixtape highlighting the varied sounds of Yugoslavia spanning the 1980s. I've long been playing these tunes on my radio shows and thought I'd take the moment to share some of my favorite songs, hopefully shining a light on famous as well as lesser known artist and show the many movements of sound that existed and overlapped with each other at the time. I want to dedicate this mixtape to Monika and Selma, who showed me so much of this music and who always kindly helps me translate lyrics.

nts2.n: TRUE AS FEW W/ MARK -  On Stefania Turkewich
nts2.d:
Diving into classical composition with Mark aka Klon Dump…
From the host: "This month focuses on the works of Ukrainian composer Stefania Turkewich, paying particular attention to her works for voice and piano, bookended by excerpts from the String Quartet and first Symphony"

nts2.n: GREAT SOUTHERN LANDS W/ JAMES W

nts2.d:
Broadcasting out of Birmingham, Arrhythmia is a monthly exploration of interesting new audio and abnormal rhythms. Expect irregular, dark or heavy - but not always.
From the host: "The evolution of Argentine Rock through some of my favorite artists, bands, and tracks. In chronological order, from 1965 - 2000. Focuses mainly on Luis Alberto Spinetta (Almendra, Pescado Rabioso, Invisible), Charly García (Serú Girán, Sui Generis), Gustavo Cerati (Soda Stereo)."
Kaifeng based DJ & producer Yu Su cooks up an hour of warm meanderings once a month.
Brooklyn musician Nick Hakim takes over the NTS airwaves.
Cult LA songwriter and Mk.gee and Dijon collaborator Paul Dally presents Spirit – a program featuring his favourite examples of evocative spirit conveyed through music.
Ahwlee & Pink Siifu (B. Cool-Aid) head up BRWN, exploring hip hop's varied and far-reaching experimental corners - live once a month from Los Angeles.

West Coast Fog with Erik Bluhm, broadcasts monthly from far-flung natural environments, bringing along site specific, privately released meditational music from past decades for a deeply involved sensory experience.
From the host: "Hear attuned pop sounds plus healing and devotional music from the '70s up to the new millennium. Catch wafting sounds from the past, lingering above Massachusetts, Palo Alto, Seattle, Eugene, Canada, and Redondo Beach."

nts2.n: RVNG Intl. Presents Friends & Fiends w/ Félicia Atkinson & Christina Vantzou
Blooming In Filth w/ Murder Club

nts2.d: LA saxophonist, producer and composer Brian Allen Simon aka Anenon shares an hour of atmospheric sounds surrounding the new album Dream Temperature.

Tune in to a monthly show where Bapari's goal is to put the spotlight on emerging
underground talent.

Vocaloid ballads uniquely demonstrate the soaring emotional possibilities of voice synthesis while also showcasing bedroom songwriters steeped in otaku culture. The selections here span a range of tender feelings sung by the digital voices of Hatsune Miku, GUMI, and IA.

Cianalas (pronounced kee-an-al-as): a profound, often melancholic, sense of homesickness, longing, or deep-seated nostalgia for one’s home or roots. This mix is for the people who are looking for home, their story, belonging or something they can't put their finger on.

nts2.n: DIGGING INTO MANDOPOP W/ SPECTRAL SOURCE
nts2.d: Mandopop can be a melancholic, passionately urban world, where individuals and experiences collide. Captivated by the works of Hou Hsia Hsien, Jia Zang-ke , asian culture is evolving rapidly, still tinged with a rich palette of emotion and brilliant narrative skill. I tried to capture this vastness.
Swedish composer and film scorer Shida Shahabi absorbs us into dream-like and moody soundscapes, slowly uncovering sounds from classical, to DIY/lo-fi music to electronics and folk recordings.
New releases and current inspirations from your hosts Jonnine Standish and Nigel Yang aka HTRK.

nts2.n: Through the Ground Floor Window w/ Abdullah Miniawy
nts2.d: Egyptian artist, writer and composer Abdullah Miniawy presents the results of his research and searching for meaning across cultures. Recording sounds during his travels, encounters with tribes, and discoveries in the East.
Writer, podcaster, musician, documentary maker, raconteur: Limerick's finest son Blindboy Boatclub lends his idiosyncratic musical tastes to regular NTS transmissions.

nts2.n: NO WEAPON IS ABSOLUTE w/ DJ SUNDAE
nts2.d: Cosmo Vitelli & DJ Sundae head up the monthly hour-long slot for No Weapon Is Absolute, direct from Paris…
Take a Kokonut Trip with Tim Koh, playing vintage pop, kaleidoscopic rock & folk, and whatever else he feels.
1-hour monthly show with composer and musician Astrid Sonne. Sharing everything from rock to dub to classical to pop.

Enter the mythology of NYC artist James K - her sound, equally organic and electronic, is a combination of odd dreams, hybridizing enchanted samples, ripped, broken and morphed rhythms, and vocals, both incomprehensible and appealing.

Chime into a world of noise and melancholia. 
Ranging from ambient to 70s Italian film scores. 
Perfect time for a long long walk.

nts2.n: THE WINDMILLS OF YOUR MIND w/ Taylor Rowley
nts2.d: DJ & music supervisor Taylor Rowley forages for the forgotten musical past every month, uncovering soundtracks, psych rock & other treasures.

nts2.n: SOLID STATE SURVIVOR W/ SHAGS CHAMBERLAIN
nts2.d:
Broadcasting from LA, Shags dives deep into his wild array of records, bringing a strong thematic focus each month.
From the host: "Songs I've made, played on, mixed, mastered and/or sang on Pt. 2"
NTS selects an hour of emotional and measured guitar bands from the resonance of the post-hardcore explosion - Music from Codeine, Acetone, Empress, Duster and more
The inimitable Kranky Ltd, live from LA once a month.

nts2.n: Music To Relax w/ Mydreamfever - 6. Noise Is the New Silence
Troth: A Postcard From Europe w/ Nein Rodere

nts2.d: Past, present and future soundtracks from Beirut to Bangkok via Bombay.

From the host: "This episode spotlights composer/engineer Seigen Ono, who leads Japan’s premier mastering and recording studio Saidera Mastering. Abiu selects from his wide range of work, fully presenting both the richness of the music and the quality of its sound."

nts2.n: THE HEAD ZONE W/ RIPLEY JOHNSON
The Jazz-n-Groove Mix w/ Bobby Ghanoush
nts2.d: The mix is celebrating and shedding light on a fantastic productions and remixes of Brian Tappert and Marc Pomeroy otherwise known as Jazz-n-Groove. Also known as Urban Blues Project, Soulsearcher and Cleptomaniacs they put out a hefty amount of original productions and remixes on labels such as Bassline, Sub-Urban, Strictly Rhythm and AM:PM. They're unique sound- a perfect synthesis of thumping beats, syncopated swung percussion and deep gorgeous jazz chords and melodies- is truly 100% their own and is impossible to copy. This mix is my nod to their legendary productions, so thank you J-N-G for the jazz and grooves!

Sons Rurals is a sonic exploration of rural folklore, with a primary focus on the unique qualities of Occitan music from the 1970s. At its heart, the mixtape celebrates the richness of Occitan traditions while seamlessly incorporating elements from other folk traditions, particularly those of Catalonia and regions of France that share similar evocative soundscapes.
The selection spans a diverse range of genres, including folk, chanson, jazz, psychedelic rock, and experimental music. Rare archival recordings, field recordings, and traditional instrumentation are thoughtfully paired with contemporary works that reimagine these timeless influences through innovative production techniques.
Sons Rurals bridges the gap between regional traditions and modern experimentation. It is a tribute to the enduring legacy of Occitan sounds and their resonances across neighboring traditions.

nts2.n: Blogspot Hauntings and Digital Apparitions w/ Jean Christo
STEEL PAN BANGERS W/ VIOLET COWBOY
nts2.d: Shimmering steel bands and wonky electronic steel sounds. The history of the instrument. My quest to unearth the ultimate steel pan record from charity shops and car boot sales.
Melbourne based producer YL HOOI share an hour of dub, electronics, postpunk and more every month…

nts2.n: Mountain Cry w/ Marylou - Grounding Special
nts2.d: French selector Marylou invites us on a trip through genre-defying and cinematic selections, weaving ambient, dub, noise, traditional music, jazz dance, footwork and more, always guided by "intuition and a soft refusal of codes.".

Your monthly dose of curated sounds from the world of movie soundtracks with actress Agathe Rousselle.
From the host: "Bits of forever favorites.""
Indie-pop legends Saint Etienne take over NTS for a weekly special residency.

Georgia are Justin Tripp and Brian Close - and they make music, videos, films and performances from their base in Chinatown, New York. Tune in for a monthly hour of oddball electronics; whether ambient or fit for the floor…
From the host: "All music made by GEORGIA for NTS"

nts2.n: AUTOTUNE THE WORLD W/ CHRISTOPHER KIRKLEY - Limpopo
nts2.d:
International portal for contemporary pop, leaning into the autotuned future… from the mind of Christopher Kirkley, the mastermind behind record label Sahel Sounds.
From the host: "This month Autotune the World is broadcasting from Limpopo, South Africa - focusing on Tsa Manyalo wedding music! High energy synthesizers over beats for the day of celebration."

Join John Olson (aka Inzane Johnny, of Wolf Eyes) and music writer Rich Tupica as they mine deep into Michigan's wilderness of sounds. No private press rarity is off limits: soul, gospel, oddities, punk, experimental, rockabilly, and garage rock. It's all unearthed. Strap in for an insanely bizarre ride across the Great Lakes State.
From the host: "Rich and Johnny’s Inzane Michigan mutates onward, unearthing the near-erased, basement-bred transmissions of Kier McDonald’s Medusa Cyclone unit—a project that flickered in and out of phase from 1992 to 2002, like a signal that never agreed to exist in one decade at a time."

nts2.n: Prison Divorce Bombshell w/ Karly Hartzman
nts2.d: Music for surviving the many unending divorces of life. From Wednesday's Karly Hartzman.

nts2.n: SACRED POOLS W/ DJ OCTOBER - Dutch Techno Speciaal
nts2.d: Sacred Pools is a monthly show, rotating between DJ October, Faso and Toshiki Ohta. All three hosts showcase a varied and intriguing combination of music on the Sacred Pools show - from harshly metallic noise rock, unhinged post-punk, chillingly minimal synth, breakbeat rave and pervy wedding disco.

Dark Entries reissues archival and contemporary gems that prove to be crucial to our modern idea of bodily music that exists out of time. Electro, italo, cold wave, new beat, house, techno from a one-of-a-kind digger who visits dusty basements to rescue lost tapes.

nts2.n: Kiss Cafe: Time 2 Get Wize w/ Maraschino
nts2.d: Piper Durabo AKA Maraschino serves up an assortment of hyperdelic jams from her NYC HQ

Join us for monthly sessions with Carlos René, co-founder of Casa Calypso record shop in Mexico City. Carlos specializes in uncovering rare Latin American recordings and has a passion for a diverse range of genres, including cumbia, Latin funk, Mexican garage rock & psych, boogaloo, and other rare grooves. Let Carlos take you on a captivating musical journey through the vibrant sounds of Latin America that will keep you grooving any time of the day.
From the host: "A holiday in Guatemala that turned into a digging session. Vinyl finds from across the region . Guatemalan artists, Central American pressings of Mexican, SalvadorIan, Panamanian and Latin American music. All on wax. All on Familiar Grounds."
BUDGIE – serving up jazz, hip-hop, soul, beats & more from his sprawling record collection, once a month from our LA studio.

Casual Play was launched in 2010 by DJ Spiñorita to serve the people fresh, new music from diverse styles and genres. Handpicked by Spiñorita, you can expect to hear music ranging from Broken Beat, Hip Hop, Latin, Disco and then some – monthly on NTS Radio LA.
From the host: "Celebrating 10 years on NTS"

Screwboss MFK Marcy Mane heads up a 2 hour round-up of the latest in underground hip hop, screwed down classics and cuts from his extensive catalog.
From the host: "New mix for april straight from flexico with screwboss rare collection"

nts2.n: 626COMPANY RADIO W/ DJ GONORRHEA & DJ诸葛亮 & MC蒙古包
nts2.d: Shanghai, China collective 626 Company, headed up by Jackzebra deliver monthly broadcasts from the collective and their friends.
nts2.j: DJ GONORRHEA & DJ诸葛亮 & MC蒙古包

subtle.d: In:flux Audio are a record label based in Leeds, UK founded by production duo Tik&Borrow. Since the label's inception in October 2013, they have dabbled in multiple dance music styles with the emphasis always being on the tracks being unique, and packed full of bass. Today the primary output for the label is from their Dubstep roster and this is focus of their show on Subtle - giving the artists and remixers chance to showcase their sounds with an hour guest mix, all hosted by label bossman Tik.

I’m a 19 year old DJ and producer from Newcastle upon Tyne, mainly playing and producing music around the 140bpm mark. I started out playing old 2-step and jungle records, before moving my focus to mainly dubstep and grime. Along with my friends Pirata and White Label, I run Newcastle’s only current 140 night, Well Irie.

subtle.n: THE CULTURE HEAD w. SAINT VOX
Skelecta presents: South London Dance Factory

subtle.d: With a monthly show on Subtle, Niceup is a London based DJ with a unique style. Pushing UKG, Breakbeat, Bass, Electro and 140. Weaving through genres showcasing new releases and older cuts.

Jedah started DJ'ing at the tender age of 18 years of age specialising in UK Garage/Grime. 

Jedah is currently the DJ for Grime Original Hollowman Jendor of the former Grime Collective Essentials/ OG's.

Responsible for “that massive grime tune with the Psycho strings”, UK producers/DJs Trends & Boylan are well versed in what the scene has to offer. They use this platform to showcase music from across the 140 spectrum from Grime to Dubstep. Anything goes!!

XOSÉ is a DJ and aspiring producer mixing and making mostly UK Garage, Grime and UK Funky. FORWARD PRESSURE largely focuses on the aforementioned UK born genres, though you can expect a variety of UK influenced sounds.

Sempra is a producer & DJ based in London, specialising in the fusion of UK Garage, Jungle and Breakbeat with dreamy vocal melodies and the beautiful instrumentation found in Soul and contemporary RnB. Sempra’s consistent output has been released by labels such as Future Classic and Headroom, gaining industry support from the likes of Nia Archives, Sherelle, Yung Singh and Dr Dubplate.

Cicely is a northern raised London based music photographer and DJ. From learning to mix during lockdown, she has since played at clubs across the country in venues such as Fabric, Corsica and Fold.

Cicely runs a collective and events night under the name NRG CRU which focus heavily on the high energy rave sound. She is highly influenced by the northern sound she grew up with, and you can hear this within her sets. Expect heavy blends of techno and hardcore while she shines the light on her favourite northern artists.

Live & Direct is the show that promises to make you dance and make you laugh, with your fave tunes that make you go 'Rahhh ain't hear this in time' and all the cheesy jokes from me, Hannah :) Lock in for the sickest guests and the most relatable stories about daily slips and hilarious situations I find myself in.

Bristol-born and London-based, Not Bad For A Girl resident DaiSu has been involved in the music scene since 2018 and has featured on stations such as Radio 1 Dance, BBC 6 Music and Melodic Distraction, as well as playing on line ups for Keep Hush, Skiddle and Red Bull. Her penchant for bass, breaks and 2-step has landed her gigs supporting acts such as Interplanetary Criminal, Main Phase, Craigie Knowes and Big Ang. In September 2022, her debut EP ‘Lightwork’ was released with Gather Records and was featured in DJ Mag's staff picks that same month.

Vinyl DJ from Leigh-on-Sea. Loves collecting records with a wide array of taste ranging from House and Techno to Grime, Dubstep and Jungle. Garage holds a special place in his heart and makes up the largest part of my collection and DJ sets.

DJ & Producer hailing from Watford influenced by Hip-Hop, Dubstep, Grime with releases on NSX records & Ghost Phone, spinning electronic genres from R&B edits, Drum and Bass and everything in between.
Highlander brings you 2 hours of the freshest Jungle, Hardcore, Dubstep and Breaks. 3rd Saturday 7-9pm lock in!

2Step Selects is a monthly show hosted by the Choki Biki Records crew. Founded in Dublin, Ireland, CBR consists of DJ Sharpson, 2step.png, Frame Shift, and MICK. The show consists of an all round breaks, electro, ukg and jungle hour, followed by an Irish showcase, demonstrating all the best Ireland has to offer collective/party/Dj & Producer wise!

Saraphim (aka RNS & RDubz) are two Essex based Producer/DJ’s currently focused on Dubstep & the 140 spectrum, mixing ethnical and grime influences into their music. You can expect anything from deep and melodic meditative vibes to high energy driven music.
London based DJ who enjoys exploring the darker, bassier corners of Jungle, Breaks, Techno and Drum & Bass, especially when it is laced with a bit of acid.

subtle.n: The Ice Box Sessions with Frigid

subtle.d: Hailing from northern Italy, STANDARD is a collective of artists, DJs and producers that aims at the promotion of bass music: Garage, UK Funky, Breaks, Leftfield Bass, Leftfield Techno, Dubstep, everything in between and beyond. They focus on the Italian Bass scene, showcasing new up-and-coming artists as well as established talents from all over the peninsula.

Oddkut is a producer & DJ hailing from Balakovo City, Russia. He's part of the Russian collective "United Gang" who are a Moscow-based community of local musicians, DJ's and promoters teamed up by their genuine love & interest in UK underground music, "TAЭT VREMYA" art group and the creator of the Moscow parties "Judder".
"The calm before summer"

Kishan is a DJ and Producer currently based in London. Actively involved in the music scene already, he produces and mixes a wide range of genres including UK Garage, Drum & Bass, House, Disco and Afrobeat.

subtle.n: Out of Phase With Sam Ling

subtle.d: Sessions w/ Amy B is an underground multi-genre music show, showcasing the most prolific guests from all corners of the music industry.

Originally linking up in Wellington and playing shows with the Breaking Beats crew, Swampman and Zero Crumbs have reunited – bringing their smooth mixing and flexible selections to East London. 

In their monthly show, the pair explore the deep, dark, and soulful ends of DNB. Expect fresh underground tunes, deep cut classics, and a bit of banter in Kiwi accents.

subtle.n: Dun Roamin w/ Tomi From Brockley

subtle.d:
Midlands born, London based producer and DJ influenced by UK funky, broken techno, garage and early dubstep. 

Has released on Keysound recordings, RKS, eatmybeat, ec2a, Durkle Disco and Dimsum records

Established in 2013, eatmybeat is a hybrid record label & club brand that was brought together by a group of friends with a passion for electronic music and building community. With diverse tastes, they support and champion the underground bass music scene through events and releases on the label. Refusing to be pigeonholed into a specific sound, they showcase everything from hip-hop, bass & breaks, through to techno, jungle, dubstep and grime. As long as it's tasty and full fat, you best believe eatmybeat are snacking on it.

subtle.n: BASICS RECORDS: L MAJOR, TAMEN & SIDESHOW

subtle.d: Born in Sunderland, based in London, Kitschko's productions and selections display a deep appreciation of UK club culture, and an affinity for late night, introspective sounds.

As a drummer since childhood, Chacho has always had an insatiable appetite for new and unique beats.
Combining his love of global percussion, dark grooves and deeper low end frequencies, 
his sets feature a blend of dubstep, grime, garage, breaks, hip hop and other flavours from around the world.
Hailing from Belfast but making moves in Nottingham, Nikki O is a DJ and radio presenter whose sets blend 4/4 rhythms, breakbeats, and the bassier side of techno.

DJ & Producer, B-Say has been a Subtle resident DJ for over 3 years.

His radio shows tend to focus on the 140 bass music scene and aims to showcase unreleased/forthcoming music from upcoming artists, as well as premiering his own unreleased material. 

More recently B-Say has ventured out from solely DJ'ing Dubstep, with his bi-monthly 'Dub & Steppers Show' which he uses to premier his own productions.

Slick is a London-based Producer and DJ combining the sounds of Grime and Drill.
Expect sets to span from sparse wave and weightless to classic NY and UK Drill, with everything in between
J:KENZO is a producer and record label owner (Artikal Music & Lion Charge) from the UK. Known for his minimalistic dark tribal sound in the both the dubstep and drum and bass scenes since 2006.

subtle.n: Pattern Recognition w/ R A L L Y
Mid Morning Matters w/ Alfie Danger
Taking the Peel w/ Badly Drawn Banana

subtle.d:
DJ and radio producer TILDA will be live from the studio to kick off your weekend. She’ll cover all corners of electronic music as well as showcasing unmissable new tracks & chatting about motives for the weekend. 

Each month TILDA will be closing the show with a signature mix.

Live & Direct is the show that promises to make you dance and make you laugh, with your fave tunes that make you go 'Rahhh ain't hear this in time' and all the cheesy jokes from me, Hannah :) Lock in for the sickest guests and the most relatable stories about daily slips and hilarious situations I find myself in.

Following his widely acclaimed breakout moment winning the 2013 DMC World Online Championships, Jon1st quickly built an international reputation as a ‘DJ’s DJ’. Not only proving himself as one of the world’s most diverse and musical turntablists, Jon also established himself as an on the pulse club DJ mixing across the alternative bass music spectrum and as an in demand live performer around the world, as well as recording acclaimed guest mixes for everyone from DJ Mag to Boiler Room to Ninja Tune’s Solid Steel to Mary Anne Hobbs to Keep Hush.

subtle.n: 5 Decades of Dub w/ Joe Vincent

subtle.d: ixxi and Fei take you on a percussive journey with their show syncop8, moving from downtempo ambient to breaks, polyrhythms, and high energy beats, exploring every rhythmic and textural nuance in between.
Representing UK Bass Music Culture across a range of styles, sounds and tempos.

Rolling through varied tempo's and genres, Jimmy 2 Shoes brings dance floor orientated vibes and flows to wriggle your toes to. Expect eclectic selections and the freshest sounds from the UK underground.

subtle.n: The Wheel Up Show w/ Tripta
subtle.d: Tripta is a Toronto based producer/DJ focusing on the darker sounds of Grime. Also blending elements of Drill, Dubstep and even Jungle all together for a unique sound you won't hear anywhere else. Signed to Slimzee's 'Slimzo's Recordings' and ‘Rotpot Records’.

Sound Arkitek is a Chicago hip-hop DJ with a love for all things drum & bass. 

With over 20 years experience playing house parties, raves, clubs and bars he has an encyclopedic knowledge of music but has always been a participant in jungle culture. In the early 2000s he was exposed to the genre primarily through Dieselboy and Danny The Wildchild mixes as well as the “Jungle Book” series of mixtapes. He became obsessed after hearing Bad Company’s “The Nine” and “Four Days,” as well as Pendulum’s “Vault.” 

As time went on, he got deeper into the scene, going to raves while buying records and posting on Drum & Bass Arena and DNBForum before turning 21 at which point he began going to Lava Lounge for Bass By The Pound’s “Seminar” series. The first legal drum & bass party he attended was Chase & Status at the aforementioned Lava Lounge, and he will never forget the energy when the duo played DJ Hazard’s “Killers Don’t Die�� and it got a rewind. His love for the scene led to him playing drum & bass parties, opening for artists like Original Sin, Gridlok, and LTJ Bukem among others. 

His Subtle Radio show is an hour of the finest tunes in the scene at the moment. 

To keep up, follow @djsoundarkitek on Instagram!

Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.

subtle.n: Into The Lion's Den w/ Restrikt

subtle.d: Potent Funk records’ Matt Frost - Well known for his technical abilities and Sleazy bass music style, Frost covers all angles from UK alt Rap to the LA beat scene.

subtle.n: BASICS RECORDS: L MAJOR, TAMEN & SIDESHOW

subtle.d: Get ready to embark on a captivating sonic journey of underground music with Charlie Kelly. Including new and timeless sounds from the depths of rap, drill, grime, and experimental genres from the vibrant streets of the UK and beyond. Charlie unearths the very best in music that pushes boundaries. Off the mic, Charlie is a Radio Producer and Social Media Executive with an eclectic music taste spanning across many different cultures, decades, and styles. Hailing from North London with a love for audio and pop culture, you can expect to hear tune after tune when you’re locked into Charlie Kelly on Subtle.
BirdE brings an hour of the freshest Drum and Bass covering everything from liquid to techy steppers - the deeper the better.

Originally from Cambridge, Counter Culture's selection deftly navigates across a myriad of influences and exclusives from himself & friends.
Expect some of the freshest rolling soul straight to your headspace.

subtle.n: Strictly Business Show w/ Qua Rush & Flipz MC
What's The Weather, Selecta? w/ Megan Wroe

subtle.d:
Hailing from Mumbai, now living in London, Chaitu is energised by the UK sound and scene.

With a love of all things weird, weighty and groovy; his mixes and productions bridge electro, techno, breaks, UKG and Jungle, with sprinklings of everything in between.

Chaitu champions provocative beats that break down the barriers of style, genre and expectation, all while keeping you moving.

Along with my friends Pirata and White Label, I run Newcastle���s only current 140 night, Well Irie.

Skelecta presents: South London Dance Factory

In September 2022, her debut EP ‘Lightwork’ was released with Gather Records and was featured in DJ Mag's staff picks that same month.

Vinyl DJ from Leigh-on-Sea. Loves collecting records with a wide array of taste ranging from House and Techno to Grime, Dubstep and Jungle. Garage holds a special place in his heart and makes up the largest part of my collection and DJ sets.

DJ & Producer hailing from Watford influenced by Hip-Hop, Dubstep, Grime with releases on NSX records & Ghost Phone, spinning electronic genres from R&B edits, Drum and Bass and everything in between.
Highlander brings you 2 hours of the freshest Jungle, Hardcore, Dubstep and Breaks. 3rd Saturday 7-9pm lock in!

2Step Selects is a monthly show hosted by the Choki Biki Records crew. Founded in Dublin, Ireland, CBR consists of DJ Sharpson, 2step.png, Frame Shift, and MICK. The show consists of an all round breaks, electro, ukg and jungle hour, followed by an Irish showcase, demonstrating all the best Ireland has to offer collective/party/Dj & Producer wise!

Saraphim (aka RNS & RDubz) are two Essex based Producer/DJ’s currently focused on Dubstep & the 140 spectrum, mixing ethnical and grime influences into their music. You can expect anything from deep and melodic meditative vibes to high energy driven music.
London based DJ who enjoys exploring the darker, bassier corners of Jungle, Breaks, Techno and Drum & Bass, especially when it is laced with a bit of acid.

subtle.n: The Ice Box Sessions with Frigid

subtle.d: Hailing from northern Italy, STANDARD is a collective of artists, DJs and producers that aims at the promotion of bass music: Garage, UK Funky, Breaks, Leftfield Bass, Leftfield Techno, Dubstep, everything in between and beyond. They focus on the Italian Bass scene, showcasing new up-and-coming artists as well as established talents from all over the peninsula.

Oddkut is a producer & DJ hailing from Balakovo City, Russia. He's part of the Russian collective "United Gang" who are a Moscow-based community of local musicians, DJ's and promoters teamed up by their genuine love & interest in UK underground music, "TAЭT VREMYA" art group and the creator of the Moscow parties "Judder".
"The calm before summer"

Kishan is a DJ and Producer currently based in London. Actively involved in the music scene already, he produces and mixes a wide range of genres including UK Garage, Drum & Bass, House, Disco and Afrobeat.

subtle.n: Out of Phase With Sam Ling

subtle.d: Sessions w/ Amy B is an underground multi-genre music show, showcasing the most prolific guests from all corners of the music industry.

Originally linking up in Wellington and playing shows with the Breaking Beats crew, Swampman and Zero Crumbs have reunited – bringing their smooth mixing and flexible selections to East London. 

In their monthly show, the pair explore the deep, dark, and soulful ends of DNB. Expect fresh underground tunes, deep cut classics, and a bit of banter in Kiwi accents.

subtle.n: Dun Roamin w/ Tomi From Brockley

subtle.d:
Midlands born, London based producer and DJ influenced by UK funky, broken techno, garage and early dubstep. 

Has released on Keysound recordings, RKS, eatmybeat, ec2a, Durkle Disco and Dimsum records
Hailing from Belfast but making moves in Nottingham, Nikki O is a DJ and radio presenter whose sets blend 4/4 rhythms, breakbeats, and the bassier side of techno.

DJ & Producer, B-Say has been a Subtle resident DJ for over 3 years.

His radio shows tend to focus on the 140 bass music scene and aims to showcase unreleased/forthcoming music from upcoming artists, as well as premiering his own unreleased material. 

More recently B-Say has ventured out from solely DJ'ing Dubstep, with his bi-monthly 'Dub & Steppers Show' which he uses to premier his own productions.

Slick is a London-based Producer and DJ combining the sounds of Grime and Drill.
Expect sets to span from sparse wave and weightless to classic NY and UK Drill, with everything in between
J:KENZO is a producer and record label owner (Artikal Music & Lion Charge) from the UK. Known for his minimalistic dark tribal sound in the both the dubstep and drum and bass scenes since 2006.

subtle.n: Pattern Recognition w/ R A L L Y
Mid Morning Matters w/ Alfie Danger
Taking the Peel w/ Badly Drawn Banana

subtle.d:
DJ and radio producer TILDA will be live from the studio to kick off your weekend. She’ll cover all corners of electronic music as well as showcasing unmissable new tracks & chatting about motives for the weekend. 

Each month TILDA will be closing the show with a signature mix.

subtle.n: VIOLENTLYILL'S WAX HEAVY w/ GILLY MAN GIRO

subtle.d: Live & Direct is the show that promises to make you dance and make you laugh, with your fave tunes that make you go 'Rahhh ain't hear this in time' and all the cheesy jokes from me, Hannah :) Lock in for the sickest guests and the most relatable stories about daily slips and hilarious situations I find myself in.

Following his widely acclaimed breakout moment winning the 2013 DMC World Online Championships, Jon1st quickly built an international reputation as a ‘DJ’s DJ’. Not only proving himself as one of the world’s most diverse and musical turntablists, Jon also established himself as an on the pulse club DJ mixing across the alternative bass music spectrum and as an in demand live performer around the world, as well as recording acclaimed guest mixes for everyone from DJ Mag to Boiler Room to Ninja Tune’s Solid Steel to Mary Anne Hobbs to Keep Hush.

Established in 2013, eatmybeat is a hybrid record label & club brand that was brought together by a group of friends with a passion for electronic music and building community. With diverse tastes, they support and champion the underground bass music scene through events and releases on the label. Refusing to be pigeonholed into a specific sound, they showcase everything from hip-hop, bass & breaks, through to techno, jungle, dubstep and grime. As long as it's tasty and full fat, you best believe eatmybeat are snacking on it.
Born in Sunderland, based in London, Kitschko's productions and selections display a deep appreciation of UK club culture, and an affinity for late night, introspective sounds.

As a drummer since childhood, Chacho has always had an insatiable appetite for new and unique beats.
Combining his love of global percussion, dark grooves and deeper low end frequencies, 
his sets feature a blend of dubstep, grime, garage, breaks, hip hop and other flavours from around the world.

Sound Arkitek is a Chicago hip-hop DJ with a love for all things drum & bass. 

With over 20 years experience playing house parties, raves, clubs and bars he has an encyclopedic knowledge of music but has always been a participant in jungle culture. In the early 2000s he was exposed to the genre primarily through Dieselboy and Danny The Wildchild mixes as well as the “Jungle Book” series of mixtapes. He became obsessed after hearing Bad Company’s “The Nine” and “Four Days,” as well as Pendulum’s “Vault.” 

As time went on, he got deeper into the scene, going to raves while buying records and posting on Drum & Bass Arena and DNBForum before turning 21 at which point he began going to Lava Lounge for Bass By The Pound’s “Seminar” series. The first legal drum & bass party he attended was Chase & Status at the aforementioned Lava Lounge, and he will never forget the energy when the duo played DJ Hazard’s “Killers Don’t Die” and it got a rewind. His love for the scene led to him playing drum & bass parties, opening for artists like Original Sin, Gridlok, and LTJ Bukem among others. 

His Subtle Radio show is an hour of the finest tunes in the scene at the moment. 

To keep up, follow @djsoundarkitek on Instagram!

Dubversified showcases Canadian West Coast DJ talent specializing in Bass Music. Hosted by Mirage, founder of Dubversified Culture, a community-led organization focusing on music, art, and events. Mirage, originally from Québec, blends house and hip-hop since 2010, now exploring 140bpm and Deep D&B.

subtle.n: Into The Lion's Den w/ Restrikt

subtle.d: Potent Funk records’ Matt Frost - Well known for his technical abilities and Sleazy bass music style, Frost covers all angles from UK alt Rap to the LA beat scene.

Get ready to embark on a captivating sonic journey of underground music with Charlie Kelly. Including new and timeless sounds from the depths of rap, drill, grime, and experimental genres from the vibrant streets of the UK and beyond. Charlie unearths the very best in music that pushes boundaries. Off the mic, Charlie is a Radio Producer and Social Media Executive with an eclectic music taste spanning across many different cultures, decades, and styles. Hailing from North London with a love for audio and pop culture, you can expect to hear tune after tune when you’re locked into Charlie Kelly on Subtle.
BirdE brings an hour of the freshest Drum and Bass covering everything from liquid to techy steppers - the deeper the better.

Originally from Cambridge, Counter Culture's selection deftly navigates across a myriad of influences and exclusives from himself & friends.
Expect some of the freshest rolling soul straight to your headspace.

subtle.n: Strictly Business Show w/ Qua Rush & Flipz MC
5 Decades of Dub w/ Joe Vincent

subtle.d: ixxi and Fei take you on a percussive journey with their show syncop8, moving from downtempo ambient to breaks, polyrhythms, and high energy beats, exploring every rhythmic and textural nuance in between.
Representing UK Bass Music Culture across a range of styles, sounds and tempos.

Rolling through varied tempo's and genres, Jimmy 2 Shoes brings dance floor orientated vibes and flows to wriggle your toes to. Expect eclectic selections and the freshest sounds from the UK underground.

subtle.n: The Wheel Up Show w/ Tripta
subtle.d: Tripta is a Toronto based producer/DJ focusing on the darker sounds of Grime. Also blending elements of Drill, Dubstep and even Jungle all together for a unique sound you won't hear anywhere else. Signed to Slimzee's 'Slimzo's Recordings' and ‘Rotpot Records’.
With a monthly show on Subtle, Niceup is a London based DJ with a unique style. Pushing UKG, Breakbeat, Bass, Electro and 140. Weaving through genres showcasing new releases and older cuts.

Jedah started DJ'ing at the tender age of 18 years of age specialising in UK Garage/Grime. 

Jedah is currently the DJ for Grime Original Hollowman Jendor of the former Grime Collective Essentials/ OG's.

Responsible for “that massive grime tune with the Psycho strings”, UK producers/DJs Trends & Boylan are well versed in what the scene has to offer. They use this platform to showcase music from across the 140 spectrum from Grime to Dubstep. Anything goes!!

XOSÉ is a DJ and aspiring producer mixing and making mostly UK Garage, Grime and UK Funky. FORWARD PRESSURE largely focuses on the aforementioned UK born genres, though you can expect a variety of UK influenced sounds.

Sempra is a producer & DJ based in London, specialising in the fusion of UK Garage, Jungle and Breakbeat with dreamy vocal melodies and the beautiful instrumentation found in Soul and contemporary RnB. Sempra’s consistent output has been released by labels such as Future Classic and Headroom, gaining industry support from the likes of Nia Archives, Sherelle, Yung Singh and Dr Dubplate.

Cicely is a northern raised London based music photographer and DJ. From learning to mix during lockdown, she has since played at clubs across the country in venues such as Fabric, Corsica and Fold.

Cicely runs a collective and events night under the name NRG CRU which focus heavily on the high energy rave sound. She is highly influenced by the northern sound she grew up with, and you can hear this within her sets. Expect heavy blends of techno and hardcore while she shines the light on her favourite northern artists.

Live & Direct is the show that promises to make you dance and make you laugh, with your fave tunes that make you go 'Rahhh ain't hear this in time' and all the cheesy jokes from me, Hannah :) Lock in for the sickest guests and the most relatable stories about daily slips and hilarious situations I find myself in.

Bristol-born and London-based, Not Bad For A Girl resident DaiSu has been involved in the music scene since 2018 and has featured on stations such as Radio 1 Dance, BBC 6 Music and Melodic Distraction, as well as playing on line ups for Keep Hush, Skiddle and Red Bull. Her penchant for bass, breaks and 2-step has landed her gigs supporting acts such as Interplanetary Criminal, Main Phase, Craigie Knowes and Big Ang. In September 2022, her debut EP ‘Lightwork’ was released with Gather Records and was featured in DJ Mag's staff picks that same month.

Vinyl DJ from Leigh-on-Sea. Loves collecting records with a wide array of taste ranging from House and Techno to Grime, Dubstep and Jungle. Garage holds a special place in his heart and makes up the largest part of my collection and DJ sets.

DJ & Producer hailing from Watford influenced by Hip-Hop, Dubstep, Grime with releases on NSX records & Ghost Phone, spinning electronic genres from R&B edits, Drum and Bass and everything in between.
Highlander brings you 2 hours of the freshest Jungle, Hardcore, Dubstep and Breaks. 3rd Saturday 7-9pm lock in!

subtle.n: The Wheel Up Show w/ Tripta
What's The Weather, Selecta? w/ Megan Wroe

subtle.d:
Hailing from Mumbai, now living in London, Chaitu is energised by the UK sound and scene.

With a love of all things weird, weighty and groovy; his mixes and productions bridge electro, techno, breaks, UKG and Jungle, with sprinklings of everything in between.

Chaitu champions provocative beats that break down the barriers of style, genre and expectation, all while keeping you moving.

In:flux Audio are a record label based in Leeds, UK founded by production duo Tik&Borrow. Since the label's inception in October 2013, they have dabbled in multiple dance music styles with the emphasis always being on the tracks being unique, and packed full of bass. Today the primary output for the label is from their Dubstep roster and this is focus of their show on Subtle - giving the artists and remixers chance to showcase their sounds with an hour guest mix, all hosted by label bossman Tik.

I’m a 19 year old DJ and producer from Newcastle upon Tyne, mainly playing and producing music around the 140bpm mark. I started out playing old 2-step and jungle records, before moving my focus to mainly dubstep and grime. Along with my friends Pirata and White Label, I run Newcastle’s only current 140 night, Well Irie.

Skelecta presents: South London Dance Factory

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy
